docs.title: tdfs4ds — Teradata Feature Store for Data Scientists
docs.text:
tdfs4ds

A temporal feature store for Teradata Vantage with full time-travel, lineage, LLM-powered documentation, a conversational agent and an automated query optimizer — built for data scientists.

Get started Training User guide AI capabilities Deployment Recipes API reference

docs.title: Why tdfs4ds
docs.text:
tdfs4ds lets you register, store, and serve features inside a Teradata Vantage database while keeping the full history of every feature value, every process, and every dataset. All catalogs are temporal — point-in-time queries, re-runs, and audit are first-class.

On top of the storage layer, the package adds:

  • Auto-documentation of every process and dataset with an LLM (OpenAI, Azure, vLLM or AWS Bedrock).
  • A business dictionary with three-level hierarchical structure designed for chunking-free RAG.
  • A conversational consumer agent (LangGraph + Chroma + Gradio) so business users can query the store in natural language.
  • A query optimizer that scores Teradata EXPLAIN plans, generates candidate rewrites, and proposes a FilterManager when a partition opportunity is detected.
  • A bundle of agent skills (SKILL.md files) that teach any code-aware LLM how to use the package end-to-end.

docs.title: 30-second snippet
docs.text:
import teradataml as tdml
tdml.create_context(host=..., username=..., password=...)

import tdfs4ds
tdfs4ds.connect(database='my_db', create_if_missing=True)
tdfs4ds.create_data_domain('CUSTOMER_ANALYTICS')

# Register a feature engineering view
df = tdml.DataFrame(tdml.in_schema('my_db', 'FEAT_ENG_CUSTOMER'))
tdfs4ds.upload_features(
    df,
    entity_id     = {'CUSTOMER_ID': 'BIGINT'},
    feature_names = ['total_amount', 'avg_amount'],
    dataset_view_name = 'ds_customer_spend',
)

That single call registers the entity, registers the features, registers a process for re-execution, ingests data into the temporal feature tables, builds a denormalised dataset view, and (when an LLM is configured) generates business descriptions and an EXPLAIN plan score.

docs.title: Compatibility
docs.text:
Requirement Version Python ≥ 3.6 teradataml ≥ 17.20 Teradata Vantage Any release with temporal table support

See Installation for a full dependency list.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text: The authoritative changelog lives at the repository root.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text: All notable changes to tdfs4ds are documented in this file.

docs.title: Added — cross-store read federation (read-only extended connections)
docs.text:
• tdfs4ds.SCHEMA_READONLY (default []) + extend_read_stores(databases) / detach_read_stores(databases=None) / list_read_stores() and connect(..., databases_readonly=[...]) (also TDFS4DS_SCHEMA_READONLY env / JSON schema_readonly) — connect to a primary store read-write as usual and extend the session to one or more other feature stores read-only. Each store is a string ('DB_B') or a dict descriptor {'schema','schema_view','vector_prefix'}.
  • Reads federate, writes don't. When read-only stores are attached, catalog / business-dictionary / lineage reads become a UNION ALL across the primary + read-only stores (each row tagged with a synthetic FEATURE_STORE provenance column, primary first), and build_dataset can pull a read-only store's features into a dataset built in the primary. upload_features / documentation / lineage writes still target only tdfs4ds.SCHEMA.
  • Byte-identical default. SCHEMA_READONLY == [] ⇒ every read funnel keeps its historical single-store form (the union builders return None) — no behavioural change unless a read-only store is attached. Same opt-in shape as the database_view split.
  • Teradata vector search federates live — search_teradata_index searches each store's own materialized collections by (vector_prefix, target_database) and merges hits (no re-index; a store without collections is skipped fail-open, still answerable via catalog/BD paths). Chroma (single local index) does not federate — out of scope.
  • New module tdfs4ds/read_federation.py (attach/validate + the shared catalog_union_sql / federated_* builders) and accessors read_view_schemas() / read_table_schemas() / read_stores(). Attach validation rejects a version-skewed / ungranted store (fail-open); optional has_bd/has_lineage capabilities are probed so a store without docs or lineage never black-holes those unions.
  • Documented collision limit: the consumer agent's identity/counting/domain-resolution is store-blind — exact for stores with disjoint domains/entities/feature names (the common case); a same-(domain, entity, feature)-across-stores clash resolves to the primary. Full provenance-in-identity hardening is a named follow-up.
  • Offline unit tests: tests/test_read_federation.py.

docs.title: Added — parallel-execution planner, FS_TIME_MANAGER, "Execution plan" admin panel
docs.text:
• tdfs4ds.analyze_parallel_plan(seeds=None, all_domains=True) — seed a list of processes / datasets / features, resolve each to its producing process(es), expand to the upstream process closure (everything needed to (re)build them — a self-contained migration unit), and group the closure into dependency waves: a topological layering of the process DAG (derived from the persisted FS_LINEAGE_GRAPH) where every process in a wave is mutually independent and can run in parallel, and waves run in order. seeds=None plans the whole store. The result also reports per-wave manager groups, unify_candidates (equivalent but distinct FilterManager/TimeManager objects), a serialize_together write-conflict guard, and processes with unknown lineage.
  • tdfs4ds.generate_execution_script(plan, mode='process_pool', ...) / write_execution_script(plan) — turn a plan into a standalone .py that runs waves sequentially and processes within a wave in parallel via separate OS processes (each worker opens its own Teradata context from TDFS4DS_TD_* env — safe against the module-global and shared-connection-pool hazards that make in-process threading unsafe).
  • tdfs4ds.unify_filter_managers / unify_time_managers (opt-in, dry_run=True by default) — repoint parallel-candidate processes onto one canonical manager (rewrite the process view's manager reference + update the catalog row).
  • New FS_TIME_MANAGER catalog — a per-process TimeManager association mirroring FS_FILTER_MANAGER, exposed as TIME_DATABASE_NAME/TIME_VIEW_NAME/TIME_TABLE_NAME on FS_V_PROCESS_CATALOG. It is populated by roll_out — as roll_out(process_list, time_manager, …) runs it records each process's TimeManager (idempotent, non-fatal). A TimeManager is a roll-out driver, not a registration-time property, so upload_features is unchanged (no timemanager= argument; registration SQL is byte-identical). Fully backward compatible and self-populating: connect() creates the (empty) table on pre-existing installs before refreshing the catalog view, and simply running your existing rollout fills it in — no migration step, including for processes registered by older versions.
  • Admin "Execution plan" panel — pick datasets/processes, see an interactive wave-layered workflow graph (what runs in parallel vs sequentially), and download the generated .py.

docs.title: Added — database_view: optional separate database for views vs tables
docs.text:
• New opt-in configuration for environments that keep views in one database and tables in another (governance / access-control policy). setup(database, database_view=...) and connect(database=..., database_view=...) accept a second database dedicated to VIEW objects, exposed as the global tdfs4ds.SCHEMA_VIEW (also settable via the TDFS4DS_SCHEMA_VIEW environment variable / .env, or the schema_view JSON config key — same tier as SCHEMA).
  • Fully backward compatible. database_view=None (the default) makes SCHEMA_VIEW resolve to SCHEMA through the new tdfs4ds.view_schema() accessor (SCHEMA_VIEW or SCHEMA), so every generated statement is byte-identical to before — no behavioural change unless a view database is explicitly configured.
  • When set: every physical TABLE stays in SCHEMA (feature FS_T_* tables, all catalog/_HIDDEN, business-dictionary, and lineage base tables) and every VIEW — process views, dataset views, all framework FS_V_* public views, per-feature value views, and the business-dictionary / lineage / process-catalog / dataset-catalog views — is created and read in SCHEMA_VIEW. Implementation: table sites keep tdfs4ds.SCHEMA; view create/read sites resolve through tdfs4ds.view_schema(). A view's FROM that reads a table keeps SCHEMA (tables never move); only a view-over-view FROM resolves to the view database.
  • Unqualified names now default to the view database. Registering a process/dataset with an unqualified name (e.g. upload_features(..., target_view_name='my_view')) places the view in SCHEMA_VIEW automatically (relaxing the previous hard "must be schema-qualified" rejection); an explicit db.view is still honoured. Covers _resolve_feature_source, build_dataset, and get_process_id.
  • FEATURE_DATABASE (feature catalog) stays the table database so cross-database dataset views resolve their "{FEATURE_DATABASE}"."{FEATURE_TABLE}" sources; DATASET_DATABASE records the dataset view database. DatasetCatalog gained schema_name_view / existing_view_objects.
  • Operational precondition (not enforced in code): the view database must be granted SELECT (and WITH GRANT OPTION to expose views to downstream consumers) on the table database.
  • Migration for an existing single-database store: set database_view and call connect(create_if_missing=True) to (re)create the framework views in the new database. Stale views left behind in the old database are not dropped automatically.
  • Out of scope (unchanged): TimeManager / FilterManager backing objects (placed by their own explicit schema_name) and the Enterprise Vector Store collection's own target_database.

docs.title: Fixed — openai-compatible provider now uses response_format, not vLLM guided_json (LiteLLM / Mistral gateways)
docs.text:
• Bug: documenting/scoring a SQL query through an OpenAI-compatible gateway that fronts a non-vLLM backend (e.g. a LiteLLM proxy → Mistral) failed with UnprocessableEntityError: 422 … "loc":["body","guided_json"], "msg":"Extra inputs are not permitted". Root cause: the openai-compatible (and vllm) provider path sent vLLM's guided_json structured-output parameter — a vLLM-only extension, not part of the OpenAI API — which such gateways reject. A provider literally named "openai-compatible" must speak the OpenAI API, i.e. use response_format.
  • Fix: openai-compatible is reclassified into the response_format json_schema group (alongside openai/azure/mistralai; strict is set only for openai/azure, which Mistral and generic gateways reject). vllm is unchanged — a real vLLM server still gets guided_json. Use provider vllm for a raw vLLM server, openai-compatible for any OpenAI-protocol gateway.
  • Centralized the provider → structured-output decision (mechanism and schema wrapping) into a single source of truth, _build_provider_llm_caller(llm, provider, base_schema, schema_name) in genai/documentation.py. The scattered per-site provider branching (build_documentation_json_schema, build_sql_documentation_chain, build_explain_documentation_chain, and the inline dataset path) — the reason the branches had drifted — now all route through it.
  • One-shot fallback: if a backend rejects the structured-output parameter (_is_structured_output_rejection — matches the 4xx extra_forbidden / "Extra inputs are not permitted" / "unknown|unsupported field/parameter" shapes naming guided_json / response_format / format), the caller retries once unconstrained and relies on the fence-tolerant _robust_json_parser. A genuine model error (context length, rate limit, content policy) never names those parameters, so it is not swallowed. This makes any gateway work even if it supports neither mechanism, so this class of bug cannot recur.
  • No config change required for existing users; no behaviour change for vllm, openai, azure, bedrock, gcp, litellm, or ollama. Immediate workaround for anyone already hitting this on a Mistral-backed gateway: set INSTRUCT_MODEL_PROVIDER='mistralai' (or now 'openai-compatible').
  • Tests: tests/test_structured_output_provider.py (pure unit, no Teradata/network) — mechanism selection per provider, the rejection detector against the real ticket message, and the one-shot fallback (incl. that unrelated errors are not swallowed).

docs.title: Added — OFS backing-table storage for TimeManager / FilterManager (Vantage Cloud Lake + tdstone2 Apply)
docs.text:
• Opt-in storage= constructor kwarg on both TimeManager and FilterManager (time_management.py, filter_management.py): None (default) = system default (block storage), 'OFS' = Object File System (MAP = TD_MAP2, STORAGE = TD_OFSSTORAGE). A companion ofs_map= kwarg (default 'TD_MAP2') overrides the map. The choice is per manager, never a global — a user can run block-storage (full primary cluster) processes and OFS/Apply processes side by side in the same session.
  • Context: on VCL, a tdstone2 model scored via the Apply backend runs on the Analytical Compute Cluster (ACC), which reads from OFS. storage='OFS' lets a manager's backing table live on OFS instead of block storage.
  • Making a manager-referencing view score on the ACC needs BOTH (confirmed live on VCL2): (1) the manager table on OFS (storage='OFS') so the ACC can read it — a block-storage read raises Error 6881; and (2) the filter written as a JOIN to the manager view, not a correlated scalar subquery — the ACC rejects ... WHERE d <= (SELECT BUSINESS_DATE FROM <tm_view>) with 6881 but accepts a JOIN. Verified: OFS + JOIN scores (~114s); OFS + scalar subquery still 6881s. So storage='OFS' is necessary but not sufficient on its own — it is genuinely part of the working recipe (the ACC reads the manager table through the JOIN), and the JOIN filter shape is the user's responsibility (a property of the scoring view's SQL). A JOIN keeps the reference dynamic (time-travel via update() still works); a hard-coded literal also passes but freezes the date. No literal-inlining helper or roll_out integration is added — the query shape is authored by the user.
  • roll_out/run() still cannot drive a tdstone2-Apply model — its ingestion CTAS (CREATE VOLATILE TABLE AS SELECT * FROM <live scoring view>) also hits 6881; call model.score() first, then upload_features.
  • New helper module utils/storage.py: resolve_storage, storage_clause(storage, ofs_map) (returns the CREATE TABLE options fragment — '' for the default so DDL is byte-identical to before, or , MAP = …, STORAGE = TD_OFSSTORAGE for OFS), is_acc_ineligible_error, and ACC_6881_HINT. The clause is injected immediately after the table name in the 5 backing-table CREATE sites (TimeManager load_time_steps / clone_timer hard / take_ownership; FilterManager load_filter / clone_filter hard).
  • Clearer 6881: _compute_features_batch (execution.py) now logs ACC_6881_HINT (still re-raises the original) when its own ingestion CTAS hits a 6881.
  • Docs: the OFS + JOIN Apply pattern (and the score()-then-upload_features sequencing) is documented across the time-manager / filter-manager user guides, the fs-rollout / fs-filter skills, and the tdstone2-troubleshooting 6881 table. No score-hook / query rewriter is coded — the filter shape is the user's responsibility (guidance only).
  • Backward compatibility: default behaviour unchanged (no MAP/STORAGE clause). New kwargs are keyword-only with defaults; existing positional calls and run()'s read-only FilterManager(...) reconstruction are unaffected. Offline tests in tests/test_manager_storage.py.

docs.title: Added — 'uaf art view' process type (UAF features whose value lives in a secondary ART layer)
docs.text:
• New opt-in, additive PROCESS_TYPE 'uaf art view' for UAF (Unbounded Array Framework) features whose value lives in a secondary ART (Analytical Result Table) layer — ARIMA fit-metrics (R_SQUARE/MAPE/AIC in ARTFITMETADATA), residual tests over ARTFITRESIDUALS, TD_PACF, etc. Reaching such a layer needs two statements (materialise the ART, then TD_EXTRACT_RESULTS over a LAYER), which the single ingestion CTAS (CREATE VOLATILE TABLE AS (SELECT * FROM <view>)) cannot express — so the inline-EXECUTE FUNCTION-in-a-view trick that serves inline-output operators (TD_ACF, TD_DICKEY_FULLER) does not extend to it.
  • upload_features(..., art_sql=[...], art_layers=[...]) — new kwargs. art_sql is a list of EXECUTE FUNCTION ... INTO VOLATILE ART(<name>) statements; art_layers names the declared LAYER(...)s the feature view reads. df/sql_query+target_view_name still build the plain feature view (reading the layer via TD_EXTRACT_RESULTS(ART_SPEC(..., LAYER(...)))).
  • Storage: no DDL migration. art_sql/art_layers are stored in the existing METADATA JSON(32000) column of the process catalog (a JSON array preserves the execution order of art_sql, which is load-bearing — a later statement may read an ART an earlier one created). PROCESS_TYPE is parameterised via kwargs.get('process_type', 'denormalized view') in the registration builders, so existing callers produce identical SQL.
  • run(): a new elif process_type == 'uaf art view' branch reads art_sql/art_layers back from METADATA and threads art_sql through _upload_features → _compute_features_batch, which — per FilterManager partition, after filtermanager.update(), before the ingestion CTAS, in the same session — calls the new _materialise_art(art_sql): for each statement in list order, DROP TABLE <art> (idempotent re-run) then execute it. The session-scoped volatile ART is then visible to the CTAS reading the feature view. Partition-awareness comes from the store filter living in the series-input view's scalar subquery (same pattern as FilterManager/fs-filter).
  • AI-feature gates: optimize_query is force-disabled for this type (art_sql is an opaque UAF invocation and the view EXPLAIN needs the ART materialised first); document_process_incremental skips the view-EXPLAIN and feeds the art_sql operator + art_layers into the column-documentation LLM context; get_process_info additively surfaces ART_SQL/ART_LAYERS.
  • Lineage traces through the ephemeral ART: _extract_series_art_table_names (lineage/lineage.py) parses TABLE_NAME(...) out of SERIES_SPEC/ART_SPEC (inert on non-UAF SQL), and record_process_lineage(art_sql=, art_layers=) rewrites the dangling feature_view → <ephemeral ART> edge into a two-hop path feature_view → <operator/layer node> → series_input_source (fail-open).
  • Backward-compatible: art_sql=None takes the classic 'denormalized view' path byte-for-byte; the 'denormalized view'/'tdstone2 view' branches are untouched.
  • Live-verified end-to-end on Vantage Cloud Lake (Vantage 20): ARIMA fit-metrics R_SQUARE/MAPE from ARTFITMETADATA, 2-partition FilterManager — registration, METADATA, both-partition ingestion (values matching the ART ground truth), idempotent run(force_compute=True), and lineage-through-ART all pass. See the notebook under notebook dev/uaf art view/. Creation-time lifecycle constraint (verified): a view referencing a volatile ART cannot be REPLACE VIEW-created unless the ART already exists in the session (Error 3807) — materialise the ART once before creating the feature view; run() re-materialises it per partition thereafter.

docs.title: Added — fs-tdstone2 skill
docs.text: • New Claude Code skill fs-tdstone2 (a tdfs4ds skill) documenting the one-call bridge tdfs4ds.upload_tdstone2_scores(model) — registering a trained tdstone2 model's per-partition predictions / computed features as governed features (entity derived from the model's mapper; snapshot vs recomputable-view trade-off; the ML-model lineage the ca-algorithm skill walks backwards).

docs.title: Tests
docs.text: • tests/test_uaf_art_view.py — DB-free unit tests for the 'uaf art view' helpers (_extract_art_name, _extract_series_art_table_names, _uaf_art_rewrite).

docs.title: Changed (Claude Code fs-* skills — sync verified gotchas + TDFS4DS_TD_* credentials)
docs.text:
• New shared reference .claude/skills/fs-shared/gotchas.md: verified against tdfs4ds 0.3.1.141 and a live Vantage 20.0 instance. Covers connection, the setup(database, if_exists='fail') signature (and why if_exists='replace' is destructive), the "a data domain has no table of its own" create-vs-select semantics, the three upload_features input shapes, grouped=True not being persisted, the business-date-defaults-to-now behaviour, temporal-MERGE idempotency, Teradata SQL traps (ORDER BY in from_query, LIKE [...] character classes, CHAR-padding, BEGIN/END(VALIDTIME), copy_to_sql type binding), lazy tdml.DataFrame, and catalog object-name / return-type quirks.
  • All ten fs-* skills: connection boilerplate now reads credentials from TDFS4DS_TD_HOST/USERNAME/PASSWORD/DATABASE (the current convention used by reconnect/serve) instead of the obsolete TDML_* vars; each skill now ends with a ## Gotchas section linking to ../fs-shared/gotchas.md.
  • fs-setup: setup(database=...) is required (the old no-arg call would TypeError); never if_exists='replace'; explicit create-vs-select data-domain rules with a fresh-store hedge pattern; LIKE ... ESCAPE cleanup SQL. The Vector Store / TeradataAI config block is retained.
  • fs-inspect: accurate catalog column comments (process catalog uses ENTITY_ID, real dataset-catalog columns, FEATURE_VIEW vs VIEW_NAME) and select_data_domain(...) instead of a direct DATA_DOMAIN = assignment.
  • fs-upload: new "three input shapes of upload_features" section (DataFrame / view-name / sql_query+target_view_name), the silent-None-on-failure warning, the always-schema-qualify rule, and the data-domain hedge in the code sample.

Documentation/skills only — no package code changed.

docs.title: Added (Teradata Enterprise Vector Store — retry transient gateway 502/503/504 errors)
docs.text:
• Motivation: live VCL2 testing repeatedly hit genuine transient 502 Bad Gateway responses from the Vector Store REST service (and one collection_status='create_failed' after an otherwise-clean-looking from_datasets() call) — pure infrastructure flakiness unrelated to any code/config issue, that resolved on a simple retry a few seconds later. These were previously classified correctly as "service down" by _reraise_if_service_down but required the caller to notice and manually retry.
  • Fix (agent/vector_store_teradata.py): new _is_transient_gateway_error() (detects a bare HTML 502/503/504 body, distinct from a genuine unreachable/unlicensed service) + _retry_if_transient_gateway_error() (retries for up to 60s, then falls through to the existing clear _SERVICE_DOWN_MSG). Wired into every REST-facing call site that showed this live: _lookup_store_info's CollectionManager.list(), build_teradata_index's from_datasets/ add_datasets calls, reset_teradata_store's from_datasets call, and _safe_destroy's destroy() call. _reraise_if_service_down now returns normally (leaving the caller's retry loop to handle it) for this third transient-exception class, alongside the existing "not in a ready state" 409 and per-query staging-object race.

docs.title: Added (Teradata Enterprise Vector Store — collection description=)
docs.text: • build_teradata_index()/reset_teradata_store() now pass a fixed, human-readable description= per collection at creation time (_SOURCE_DESCRIPTIONS in agent/vector_store_teradata.py), visible via CollectionManager().list()'s collection_description field — makes the three collections self-documenting when inspected outside tdfs4ds (e.g. via a shared VCL2 instance with many unrelated collections). Verified live.

docs.title: Fixed (Teradata Enterprise Vector Store — _inputdata view permanently referencing a garbage-collected ephemeral view)
docs.text:
• Symptom: on a fresh VCL2 test deployment, build_teradata_index() created all three collections without error and collection_tdfs4ds_<source>_index (the embeddings table) held real, non-empty rows (13/16/73 for objects/sections/columns) — but every similarity_search call, and even a plain SELECT COUNT(*) against collectionV_tdfs4ds_<source> from a completely unrelated client (a teradata-mcp5 MCP session, a fresh teradataml session), failed identically with Error 3807 / TDML_2412: Object 'data_scientist.ml___frmqry_v_<id>' does not exist — every time, not transiently (3/3 retries failed identically; the referenced object was confirmed absent from DBC.TablesV).
  • Root cause, found by inspecting the actual DDL of the backing Teradata objects: collection_tdfs4ds_<source>_inputdata is itself a view, and its stored SHOW VIEW DDL hardcoded FROM "data_scientist"."ml___frmqry_v_<id>" — a short-lived materialized-query view that Vantage garbage-collects almost immediately. collectionV_tdfs4ds_<source> (the join view search reads through) depends on _inputdata, so it inherited the same permanent breakage. Traced this to tdfs4ds's own call site (not just the teradatagenai client library, as the 0.3.1.131 fix assumed): _from_datasets_kwargs() built its data= argument via tdml.DataFrame.from_query("SELECT ... FROM <permanent view>") — and confirmed live that DataFrame.from_query(...) itself sets _table_name to a freshly materialized ephemeral view at construction time, before teradatagenai is even called. teradatagenai then bakes whatever _table_name it receives permanently into _inputdata's DDL — so reading from a permanent source view (the 0.3.1.131 fix) was necessary but not sufficient; the from_query() wrapper itself was creating a doomed ephemeral view on every call.
  • Fix (agent/vector_store_teradata.py): _from_datasets_kwargs() now passes data= as a plain fully-qualified "schema.view" string naming the permanent FS_V_BUSINESS_DICTIONARY_* view directly — never a from_query() result — guaranteeing no ephemeral materialization ever happens. Matches teradatagenai's own documented simplest input shape (its from_datasets docstring's own Example 1 passes a plain table name string).
  • No row-level filtering of data= — by design, not an oversight: checked directly that neither from_datasets nor add_datasets exposes any filter/predicate/where-clause parameter. Considered and rejected pointing data= at a filtered subset for scoped/incremental reindexing: _inputdata is a live view (re-evaluated on every query, not a frozen snapshot), so a filtered data= would permanently narrow what the whole collection can ever return. Separately, _index's embeddings are keyed by TD_ID, which _inputdata (re-)assigns via ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY <text column>) on every evaluation — a stable-looking key that is actually recomputed by row position, not a persistent identity. Always pass the entire, unfiltered source view.
  • Required recovery step for existing installs: collections created before this fix have the broken ephemeral-referencing _inputdata/collectionV_* DDL baked in permanently — an incremental add_datasets on top will NOT repair it. Call tdfs4ds.agent.vector_store_teradata.reset_teradata_store() (requires admin) once to destroy and recreate the collections using the fixed code path.

docs.title: Fixed (Teradata Enterprise Vector Store — search_teradata_index silently returning 0 hits on genuine matches)
docs.text:
• Symptom: after fixing the _inputdata/ephemeral-view bug above and confirming similarity_search() genuinely returns real, relevant hits when called directly, tdfs4ds.search_vector_index()/search_teradata_index() — the actual public entry point — still returned 0 rows for the same query against the same collection, with no error or warning logged.
  • Root cause: _parse_one()'s _TEXT_KEYS (("content", "page_content", "text", "document")) assumed a generic/Chroma-like hit shape. A real Teradata Vector Store hit is flattened differently: the text lives directly under the source's actual business-dictionary column name — business_description (objects/columns) or section_content (sections) — not any of the generic keys, and metadata (object_name, section_name, column_name, etc.) is flattened into the same dict rather than nested under a metadata key. Every hit's text therefore stayed empty, silently dropped by the if not text.strip(): return None guard — 5 genuine hits parsed down to 0 rows with no diagnostic trace.
  • Fix (agent/vector_store_teradata.py): added "business_description" and "section_content" to _TEXT_KEYS. Verified live: the same query that previously returned 0 rows through search_vector_index() now returns 8 real, relevant hits across objects and sections, matching what similarity_search() returns directly.

docs.title: Known limitation (Teradata Enterprise Vector Store — build_teradata_index's reuse branch cannot pick up new rows via add_datasets; data_refresh=True investigated but found unreliable)
docs.text:
• Symptom: calling build_teradata_index() a second time (collection already exists, e.g. after documenting one more table/column) fails with TDML_2412 ... Response Code: 400, Message: You can only add object names that do not exist in the collection — and this 400 is then misrouted through _reraise_if_service_down's generic "response code:" catch and re-raised as the unrelated _SERVICE_DOWN_MSG ("service is unreachable or not enabled"), even though the service is clearly up (the same collection had just been created moments earlier).
  • Root cause: add_datasets()'s real semantics are "attach a new source object not yet registered on the collection," not "resync/upsert rows from an already-attached source." _from_datasets_kwargs() always names the SAME FS_V_BUSINESS_DICTIONARY_* view on every call, so any second call through the reuse branch is guaranteed to hit this 400 once the source is already attached — i.e. new business-dictionary rows are NOT currently picked up by a second build_teradata_index() call.
  • Investigated and rejected as a fix: Collection.update(data_refresh=True) — the documented mechanism ("refresh/regenerate vectors for the collection using existing backing/source tables," per its own docstring's Example 8) — looked like the correct replacement and succeeded once in isolated live testing (a new business-dictionary row correctly appeared in _index/_inputdata/ collectionV_*, all in lockstep, 74/74/74). However it then failed consistently on every subsequent attempt — with a fresh TeradataVectorStore instance, and even with a genuinely new, never-indexed row present in the source view — with "At least one of collection_index, collection_parameters, collection_description, or new_collection_name must be provided for update". This looks like an intermittent/stateful server-side issue in teradatagenai, not something reproducibly fixable from the client side, so it was not adopted as the fix — shipping it would trade one known-broken path for a differently unreliable one. build_teradata_index's reuse branch remains on add_datasets() (pre-existing behavior, known limitation). A real fix needs a dedicated follow-up once data_refresh's actual failure conditions are understood.

docs.title: Fixed (Teradata Enterprise Vector Store — add_datasets() silently corrupting collections; "no exception" ≠ "write succeeded")
docs.text:
• Symptom: a live deployment showed build_teradata_index logging "indexed 'tdfs4ds_objects' (reused existing collection)" (no exception raised) while CollectionManager().list() showed the SAME collection's collection_status as 'update_failed'. Searching for a document's own verbatim indexed text (search_vector_index(<verbatim text>, top_k=5)) returned zero hits at the same time "indexed successfully" was logged — proving the collections held no usable data despite the success log. A timeline pinned the corruption to build_teradata_index itself: all three stores were collection_status='ready' at 16:46, then update_failed/create_failed at 16:49 — a ~3-minute window matching exactly one build_teradata_index run.
  • Leading root cause: Collection.add_datasets()/update() accept an update_style parameter ("MINOR"/"MAJOR") that tdfs4ds never passed. Checked the teradatagenai client source directly: every single documented usage example of update()/add_datasets()/delete_documents() in the library's own docstrings explicitly passes update_style="MINOR" — none omit it, and no default is documented in the parameter metadata. This is a strong signal that omitting it is an unsupported code path server-side. Fixed: build_teradata_index's add_datasets call now passes update_style="MINOR" (routine incremental upsert), and a MAJOR finalize pass (collection.update(update_style="MAJOR")) runs once per touched store after the per-store loop completes — matching the client library's intended MINOR-for-churn / MAJOR-for-periodic-consolidation split.
  • Secondary, defense-in-depth fix: add_datasets()/from_datasets() are asynchronous — a call returning without an exception only means "the request was accepted," not "the write succeeded." Added _verify_write_succeeded() (agent/vector_store_teradata.py), which polls the real collection_status (via the generalized _lookup_store_info(), extracted from the existing _lookup_store_database() — which previously discarded this field) until it reports 'ready', called right after every write in build_teradata_index, before logging success. Raises clearly on timeout or if collection_status reports a terminal *_failed state instead.
  • New failed-state detection: _is_failed_status() matches any ..._failed collection_status (confirmed live values: update_failed, create_failed). Both build_teradata_index (pre-write) and search_teradata_index (pre-search) now check this first and raise/log a clear, actionable message pointing at reset_teradata_store() — instead of silently waiting/retrying into a collection that can never recover on its own, or (for search) silently skipping it behind an opaque "query failed (... not ready for asks ...), skipping" warning.
  • Usability fix: search_vector_index() now accepts k= as an alias for top_k= (the near-universal convention in the surrounding ecosystem — Chroma, FAISS, LangChain retrievers all use k) instead of raising a raw TypeError: unexpected keyword argument 'k'.

docs.title: Added (proactive readiness wait before every Vector Store write)
docs.text:
• Motivation: 0.3.1.137's _retry_if_not_ready fixed the classification of the transient "not in a ready state" 409 (async Collection writes can still be busy when the next write targets them) but only retried by repeating the write itself, capped at 120s — not enough for a slower collection (tdfs4ds_columns, in a real deployment, kept 409ing well past that window).
  • Fix (agent/vector_store_teradata.py): added _wait_until_collection_ready(collection, name, timeout_seconds=1800.0, poll_interval=5.0), which polls the read-only Collection.get_details() every 5s until it succeeds — treating any exception (including the 409) as "still processing a prior async operation" — before the real write is attempted at all. Unlike retrying the write, a read-only probe can't itself add server-side contention. Raises the last observed exception if the collection is still not ready after 30 minutes (a genuinely stuck/failed operation surfaces clearly instead of hanging forever).
  • Wired into both write paths in build_teradata_index(): before the initial from_datasets() create attempt (only when the collection is confirmed to already exist via _lookup_store_database — a genuinely nonexistent collection isn't "not ready", it's "not there", so the wait is skipped there), and before the add_datasets() reuse-branch call. The existing _retry_if_not_ready write-retry stays in place underneath as a belt-and-suspenders fallback.

docs.title: Fixed (Teradata Enterprise Vector Store — transient "not in a ready state" 409 misclassified as service-down)
docs.text:
• Symptom: build_teradata_index() (real deployment, after PAT auth started working per 0.3.1.136) failed indexing the third of three stores with Teradata Vector Store service is unreachable or not enabled ... Detail: [Teradata][teradataml](TDML_2412) Failed to execute collection. Response Code: 409, Message: Collection tdfs4ds_columns cannot be updated as it is not in a ready state. — even though the service was clearly reachable (the first two stores had just been indexed successfully in the same run).
  • Root cause: every Collection write operation (create/update/ destroy) on the Enterprise Vector Store is asynchronous — the earlier logs show "Collection update request is accepted and in progress" for each store, meaning the collection can still be busy processing that request when the next store's operation targets it (or when a rapid create→reuse retry targets the same collection). Calling add_datasets() while the collection is still busy raises a 409 whose message contains both "not in a ready state" AND "Response Code:" — the latter is exactly the substring _reraise_if_service_down used to detect a down/unlicensed service, so this transient, retryable condition was being raised as a permanent RuntimeError telling the user to check licensing.
  • Fix (agent/vector_store_teradata.py): added _is_not_ready_error(exc), checked first (before the generic "response code:" branch) inside _reraise_if_service_down so this specific 409 shape is never misclassified — _reraise_if_service_down now returns normally for it, leaving it to the caller to retry. Added _retry_if_not_ready(fn, name, timeout_seconds=120.0, poll_interval=5.0), which retries a callable while it keeps raising this transient 409 (up to the timeout, then re-raises), and wrapped the add_datasets() call in build_teradata_index()'s reuse path with it.

docs.title: Added (tdfs4ds now manages teradataml.set_auth_token(...) automatically)
docs.text:
• Motivation: VECTOR_STORE_BACKEND='teradata' authenticates against the Vantage Enterprise Vector Store REST API via teradataml.set_auth_token(...) — a separate auth channel from the regular tdml.create_context() DB connection. Previously tdfs4ds never called this; users had to call it themselves, and forgetting to do so produced the _NOT_CONFIGURED_MSG error (added in 0.3.1.135).
  • New module tdfs4ds.utils.vector_store_auth.configure_vector_store_auth() (utils/vector_store_auth.py): reads new TDFS4DS_VECTOR_STORE_AUTH_* pass-through environment variables (same tier as TDFS4DS_TD_*) and dispatches to teradataml.set_auth_token(...), trying three mechanisms in priority order (first fully-configured group wins):
  • PAT — BASE_URL + PAT_TOKEN + PEM_FILE (+ optional USERNAME)
  • Basic — BASE_URL + USERNAME + PASSWORD
  • OAuth — BASE_URL + CLIENT_ID

Returns the mechanism name used (or None), and never raises — any set_auth_token failure is caught and logged as a non-fatal warning. Never logs secret values (pat_token, pem_file path, password, client_id), only base_url/username. - Wired in at every point a DB context is already established: lifecycle.setup(), lifecycle.connect() (both log which mechanism was used, or that none was configured), and serve/bootstrap.py's _post_connect_setup() (called before the vector index build, so the build succeeds once auth is configured). - config.py: the six new vars added to _PASSTHROUGH_ENV_VARS, matching exactly how TDFS4DS_TD_* is handled (read directly via os.environ, .env support via the existing passthrough re-export mechanism). - _NOT_CONFIGURED_MSG (agent/vector_store_teradata.py) updated to describe the new automatic behavior and the three env var groups, since reaching this error now means either no TDFS4DS_VECTOR_STORE_AUTH_* vars were set, or set_auth_token itself failed (check the connect/setup log for a "Vector Store auth ... failed" warning). - JWT and Keycloak (two other set_auth_token mechanisms) are intentionally out of scope — only PAT/Basic/OAuth are implemented.

docs.title: Fixed (misleading "service unreachable" message when the Vector Store client isn't authenticated)
docs.text:
• Symptom: build_teradata_index/build_vector_index failed with Teradata Vector Store service is unreachable or not enabled ... check that the Enterprise Vector Store feature is licensed/activated, with Detail: Invalid URL 'None/api/v2/collections?page_size=100000': No scheme supplied — even though the service itself was fine.
  • Root cause: _reraise_if_service_down treated every requests.exceptions.RequestException as "service down/unlicensed." But Invalid URL 'None/...' is requests.exceptions.MissingSchema/InvalidURL — a client-side failure: the Vector Store REST client's base URL is None because teradataml.set_auth_token(...) (or equivalent session auth) was never called before using the backend. tdfs4ds does not call set_auth_token on the user's behalf (it's a connection/auth concern outside its scope), so this is a setup-order gap, not a licensing problem — but the error message pointed the user at the wrong fix.
  • Fix: _reraise_if_service_down (agent/vector_store_teradata.py) now checks for MissingSchema/InvalidURL (and the equivalent wrapped TeradataMlException message shape, "no scheme supplied"/"invalid url") before the generic RequestException check, raising a new, distinct _NOT_CONFIGURED_MSG RuntimeError that tells the user to call set_auth_token(...) — instead of the misleading "unreachable/unlicensed" message.

docs.title: Fixed (reset_teradata_store — wait for async destroy() before recreating)
docs.text:
• Symptom: even with an explicit destroy() that reported success ("Collection destroy request is accepted and in progress"), a following from_datasets() call could still raise TeradataGenAIException: Collection with name '...' already exists. — from Collection.create()'s own self.exists check.
  • Root cause: Collection.destroy() is asynchronous — it returns as soon as the request is accepted, not once the collection is actually gone server-side. reset_teradata_store() (added in 0.3.1.133) called _safe_destroy() then immediately from_datasets(), racing the still-in- progress destroy.
  • Fix: added _wait_until_destroyed(name, timeout_seconds=60.0) (agent/vector_store_teradata.py), which polls _lookup_store_database() until the collection is confirmed gone (or the timeout elapses, logged as a warning rather than raised — the subsequent from_datasets() call will surface a clear error itself if the collection is genuinely still there). reset_teradata_store() now calls this between _safe_destroy() and the recreate.

docs.title: Fixed (Teradata Enterprise Vector Store — clear gateway errors + drop the admin-only destroy from routine rebuilds)
docs.text:
• Symptom 1: tdfs4ds.rebuild_vector_indexing(force_rebuild=True) (and any build_vector_index/build_teradata_index call) could crash with a raw teradataml.common.exceptions.TeradataMlException (TDML_2412) wrapping an HTML 502 Bad Gateway body, several frames deep inside langchain_teradata/teradatagenai internals (Collection.exists() → CollectionManager().list()), instead of the clear "Vector Store service is unreachable or not enabled" message tdfs4ds already raises elsewhere.
  • Symptom 2 (surfaced once the gateway/auth issue above was resolved): the same call then failed with TeradataGenAIException: Collection with name 'tdfs4ds_objects' already exists. — raised by Collection.create()'s own authoritative self.exists check, immediately after build_teradata_index() had just attempted to destroy that same collection.
  • Root cause (symptom 1): the clear-message classification (_raise_service_unavailable, detecting a transport-level failure or an HTML/status body embedded in a TeradataMlException) previously lived only inside _lookup_store_database(). The actual TeradataVectorStore.from_datasets()/add_datasets() calls in build_teradata_index() had no exception handling at all, so a gateway error from those calls (which internally re-enter the same Collection.exists() check) bypassed the classification and propagated raw.
  • Root cause (symptom 2): build_teradata_index(force_rebuild=True) called _safe_destroy() before every rebuild, which only invoked destroy() when Collection.status() reported a non-empty operation history — but status() reports async create/destroy/update operations, not current existence, so an existing collection with no reported history was wrongly concluded "nothing to destroy" and left in place. Separately, Collection.destroy() requires admin privileges ("Only admin users can use this method" per its own docstring); any destroy failure (e.g. a non-admin session) was caught and only logged as a warning, silently leaving the stale collection for the following from_datasets() call to collide with.
  • Fix:
  • Extracted the service-down classification into a shared _reraise_if_service_down(exc) helper (agent/vector_store_teradata.py), reused by _lookup_store_database() and wrapped around every from_datasets()/add_datasets() call site.
  • build_teradata_index() no longer destroys anything. It always attaches-or-creates via from_datasets()/add_datasets() (which creates the collection on first use and upserts/appends rows keyed on ID afterward — no per-id duplication), sidestepping the admin requirement entirely. force_rebuild is now purely informational for logging; both branches behave identically. This means rows whose source business-dictionary entry was deleted are not purged by this call (no admin-free per-id deletion API is available yet — Collection.get_ids() is currently an unimplemented stub upstream).
  • Added reset_teradata_store(sources=None) — an explicit, admin-only destroy-and-recreate for when a true clean wipe (stale-row purge) is needed and the connected user has admin rights. The routine build_vector_index()/rebuild_vector_indexing() path deliberately does not call it, so non-admin sessions keep working.
  • rebuild_vector_indexing()'s and build_vector_index()'s docstrings updated to describe the Teradata backend's reuse-only behavior (the Chroma backend's force_rebuild=True still deletes and rebuilds from scratch, unchanged).

docs.title: Fixed (business-dictionary views not (re)created on installs from an older tdfs4ds version)
docs.text:
• Root cause: the three FS_V_BUSINESS_DICTIONARY_OBJECTS/_SECTIONS/_COLUMNS views (introduced in 0.3.1.131 for the Teradata Enterprise Vector Store) were only created inside documentation_tables_creation(), which connect(create_if_missing=True) only calls when the underlying business-dictionary tables are detected as missing. On any install where those tables were created by an older tdfs4ds version, connect() saw the tables already present, skipped the creation branch entirely, and the views were never materialized — causing Error 3807: FS_V_BUSINESS_DICTIONARY_OBJECTS does not exist the first time the Teradata vector store backend queried them.
  • Fix: extracted the view-creation loop out of documentation_tables_creation() into its own documentation_business_dictionary_view_creation() (genai/documentation.py, re-exported from tdfs4ds.genai). connect() now calls it unconditionally on every connect, right alongside the existing unconditional feature_store_catalog_view_creation() / process_store_catalog_view_creation() refresh in the "Refreshing catalog views..." step (lifecycle.py) — the same self-healing pattern those two already used, rather than depending on a table-existence probe to infer whether the views need (re)creating. REPLACE VIEW is idempotent, so this adds no meaningful cost and guarantees the views exist after any connect(), regardless of what version created the underlying tables. setup() was already covered (it always calls documentation_tables_creation(), which now includes the view creation via this refactor).

docs.title: Fixed (Teradata Enterprise Vector Store — real fix for staging-database bug)
docs.text:
• Root cause found: target_database (0.3.1.129/130) only tells the Vantage REST API where to register the FINAL collection's metadata — it does NOT control where langchain_teradata's from_documents()/add_documents() (LangChain Document-list input) stage their intermediate data. That path internally calls teradataml.DataFrame.from_dict(..., persist=True) / DataFrame.materialize(), which takes no database argument at all and — per teradataml's own docstring — may materialize as a view or a volatile table, "decided and taken care by teradataml" internally. A volatile table is session-scoped and invisible to the Vector Store's separate REST session, reproducing TDML_2412: Object ... does not exist regardless of target_database.
  • Fix: build_teradata_index() now calls TeradataVectorStore.from_datasets()/ add_datasets() instead of from_documents()/add_documents(), reading directly from three new permanent CURRENT VALIDTIME views — FS_V_BUSINESS_DICTIONARY_OBJECTS/ _SECTIONS/_COLUMNS (documentation_tables_creation(), tdfs4ds.DOCUMENTATION_BUSINESS_DICTIONARY_*_VIEW) — via plain SQL + tdml.DataFrame.from_query(). No Document conversion, no pandas round-trip, no ephemeral/volatile/temporary table of any kind — the vector store reads the same permanent business-dictionary table every other part of tdfs4ds treats as the source of truth. The views bake in LOCKING ROW FOR ACCESS, a synthesized ID column (matching the existing Chroma-backend doc-id convention), and a blank/NULL-description filter (mirroring _objects_to_docs/etc.'s if not text.strip(): continue guard).

docs.title: Fixed (Teradata Enterprise Vector Store — orphan prevention + service-down handling)
docs.text:
• target_database on every TeradataVectorStore call: the incremental-attach path in build_teradata_index() and search_teradata_index() now pass target_database (scoped to tdfs4ds.SCHEMA) alongside the creation path fixed in 0.3.1.129, so attach/search are explicit about which database they resolve against instead of relying on the Vantage session's ambient current database.
  • Pre-destroy visibility via CollectionManager: new _lookup_store_database(name) helper (tdfs4ds/agent/vector_store_teradata.py) calls teradatagenai's CollectionManager().list() to discover which database a same-named store actually lives in before attaching. _safe_destroy now destroys the store at its discovered database (not blindly at tdfs4ds.SCHEMA) and logs a warning when it's found somewhere unexpected — closing an orphan risk where a stale store from before the target_database fix would never be reached/destroyed on force_rebuild, leaving a duplicate behind. build_teradata_index also logs the intended target database once per run.
  • Service-down handling: _lookup_store_database distinguishes a genuinely unreachable or unlicensed Vector Store service (transport-level requests errors, or a gateway 404/502 HTML body embedded in a TeradataMlException) from real in-service errors, and raises one clear RuntimeError ("...unreachable or not enabled... check licensing/activation...") instead of a raw requests traceback or an HTML-in-exception message. This propagates correctly through build_teradata_index's incremental-attach path (previously it would have been misclassified as "store doesn't exist, rebuilding").

docs.title: Fixed (Teradata Enterprise Vector Store)
docs.text: • target_database on vector store creation: TeradataVectorStore.from_documents(...) in build_teradata_index() now passes target_database=tdfs4ds.SCHEMA explicitly. Without it, the Enterprise Vector Store staged its intermediate object in the Vantage session's ambient "current database" (the login user's default DB), not tdfs4ds.SCHEMA / the teradataml context's database=/temp_database_name= — causing TDML_2412: Object ... does not exist when those differ.

docs.title: Fixed (eval + dashboard + agent + API)
docs.text:
• Eval progress bar: shows elapsed time and estimated remaining time during SSE run (⏳ 2/44 — 0:12 elapsed, ~0:45 remaining; done shows total duration)
  • Eval report width: #ev-report now has width: 100% so the report fills the full panel
  • Judge — EXPLAIN grounding: _verified_names_from_context now extracts source_columns table/column names and calculation_sql formula tokens from the trace, so EXPLAIN-derived facts (e.g. CLAIMS_RAW, quantity) are verified rather than treated as fabrications; S05-style all-1 misscores fixed
  • Judge — EXPLAIN prompt rule: added conditional "EXPLAIN GROUNDING RULE" clause to _judge_turn prompt for EXPLAIN turns
  • Judge — DEFINITION completeness: added "DEFINITION COMPLETENESS RULE" clause so judge does not penalise for omitting navigation metadata (domain, entity_name) from the answer body
  • Visualization — radar chart: legend moved below chart with wider bottom margin (height=520, margin b=140, horizontal y=-0.22); labels no longer truncated to 1 character
  • Visualization — bar chart: increased height and bottom margin (height=420, margin b=100, y=-0.28); metric labels no longer truncated
  • Dashboard run history: x-axis now shows view names (vw_aggregate_features) instead of process UUIDs; _dash_run_history_figs accepts pid_to_view mapping from caller
  • Query optimizer API: new POST /v1/optimize endpoint — accepts {"sql": "...", "user_request": "..."}, returns {optimized_sql, optimization_strategy, token_consumption}; /v1/chat/completions for tdfs4ds-query-optimizer model now includes optimized and score_delta in the usage block
  • Domain typo: node_detect_domain now fuzzy-matches object_name against known data domains when detect_data_domain_for_object returns nothing (e.g. "anomalu" → "anomaly"); _build_context_update normalises a typo domain against the existing graph's known domains as a last-resort guard, preventing duplicate domain nodes in the knowledge graph visualization

docs.title: Fixed (admin web shell — 13 UI rendering issues)
docs.text:
Multi-fix release addressing visual and functional bugs in the Tera admin shell:

Chatbot: removed the "Loading…" spinner flash when the Chatbot nav item is first selected (the iframe replaces content immediately so no spinner is needed).

Query Optimization: - "Current SQL" left column now shows the actual view SQL (PROCESS_SQL key, not the missing SQL key). - "Process detail" card renders styled HTML directly (badges, warnings, SQL block) instead of passing HTML-injected markdown through marked() which escaped the tags. - "Process context" footer (_Process context — Entity: … | Features: …) is now a styled light-grey info bar instead of raw italic text. - Score Summary and other ## sections with GFM pipe-tables are now converted to real <table> HTML before rendering.

Evaluation: - "Generate scenarios" button now shows an animated orange progress bar while the request is in flight and re-enables on completion. - By-skill score table replaced with a grouped bar chart (0–5 scale, one bar per metric per skill) — more readable at a glance. - Radar chart legend moved to the right side (orientation="v", x=1.02) so it no longer overlaps the polar area. - Scenario list table header <th> cells now carry color:#fff; background:#00233C inline — previously only the <tr> had these styles, which some CSS resets overrode. - Agent answers and judge rationale in the HTML report are now rendered as HTML (bold, inline code, bullet lists) instead of raw escaped text.

Dashboard: - "Run history" charts: follow_up_report_many().to_pandas() moves PROCESS_ID to the pandas index (it is the PRIMARY INDEX); added .reset_index() so the column is available for grouping — previously all queries returned empty. - "Compute row counts" button now shows an animated progress bar while the (potentially long-running) per-table COUNT queries are executing.

Layout: - All panels no longer shrink horizontally when content loads; #td-main now has width: calc(100% - 280px); box-sizing: border-box and .td-panel has width: 100% — panels were previously relying on default width: auto (shrink-to-fit), which collapsed when wide content was injected. - Added @keyframes td-slide animation to td.css for the progress bars.

docs.title: Fixed (context knowledge graph — orphan feature nodes on a multi-entity SUMMARY)
docs.text:
Follow-up to 0.3.1.113. Asking "what are the available features in the claims anomaly detection data domain?" still rendered some features (e.g. pharmacy_lifetime_avg_amount) as isolated nodes — no edge to their entity or data domain — even though the catalog has both. Invariant: a feature must ALWAYS link to an entity AND a data domain.

Root cause: _build_context_graph_pyvis (agent/graph.py) renders features in three places. A feature_list pre-seed loop minted each feature's node id from _focus_entity (EMPTY for a multi-entity SUMMARY — node_skill_summary only sets it when there's exactly one entity) and no domain, producing a blank-identity node feature___NODOM____NOENT__<name> with zero edges. The real render loops mint the SAME feature with its true (entity, domain) triplet → a different node id, correctly linked. The node-merge net never merges two Feature nodes with different ids (triplet identity is intentional, so same-name/different-entity features stay distinct), so the blank-id pre-seed node survived as the floating orphan.

Fix: the pre-seed loop now recovers each feature's REAL (entity, domain) from the accumulated graph (features keyed by the DOMAIN::ENTITY::NAME triplet) and mints one node per entity variant, each linked to its entity (and the entity to its domain) — so the node ids collide with the real loops (one node, properly linked) and all same-name/different-entity/domain variants are preserved. A feature with no accumulated entry yet (pure group-pronoun-freshness) is anchored to the domain node so it is never isolated. Shared edge logic factored into a _link_feature_node helper.

Defense in depth: skill_summary's cg_features (agent/consumer_agent.py) now stamps each feature's domain (active tdfs4ds.DATA_DOMAIN), and node_skill_summary passes the resolved summary domain (not the possibly-stale resolved_data_domain, and never the unscoped "all") to _build_context_update — so features are never domain-less in the accumulated graph.

docs.title: Fixed (context knowledge graph — entity-aware feature↔dataset linking)
docs.text:
Two bugs in the chatbot "Context knowledge graph" (the pyvis viz built from accumulated_context_graph), both visible after a SUMMARY question ("what are the available features in a data domain?"):

  • Orphan single features. A feature like unique_drugs rendered as an isolated node — no edge to its entity, dataset, or the data-domain node.
  • Dataset linked to the wrong same-named feature. ds_pharmacy_stats (grain pharmacy_id) drew dataset→feature edges to BOTH the pharmacy-entity AND the provider-entity features of the same name (total_amount, avg_amount, …).

Root cause (single): _feature_dataset_names_map() (agent/consumer_agent.py) keyed its feature→dataset map on FEATURE_NAME alone, dropping the entity dimension. A feature's identity is the pair (FEATURE_NAME, ENTITY_NAME), so a name-only key conflated two distinct features and gave each the union of every dataset exposing that name.

Fix: the dataset _FEATURES table carries FEATURE_ID (the feature's identity); the feature catalog (FS_FEATURE_CATALOG, keyed by FEATURE_ID BIGINT) resolves it to the authoritative ENTITY_NAME/DATA_DOMAIN. _feature_dataset_names_map() now joins the two on FEATURE_ID (in pandas over single materializations — no per-row DB query) and returns two maps: by_entity keyed on (ENTITY_UPPER, FEATURE_NAME_UPPER) (primary) and a by_name fallback for features whose FEATURE_ID has no catalog match (entity-less / cross-domain). skill_summary looks up the (entity, name) key first, falling back to name-only only when the feature has no entity — so a dataset links only to the entity it actually exposes, and entity-less features stay connected. Per-turn memoization and fail-open behavior unchanged.

docs.title: Fixed (reverse-proxy path prefix — Gradio chatbot gradio_api URLs 404'd behind /tdfs4ds2)
docs.text:
Follow-up to 0.3.1.111. The FastAPI shell + /ui/* routes resolved correctly under the prefix, but the Gradio chatbot did not: its page emitted browser-facing asset/API URLs as root + api_prefix (e.g. /gradio_api/queue/join) with no prefix, because that root comes from the mount's root_path, not from the ASGI scope the _strip_prefix middleware rewrites. So the browser called https://…/gradio_api/... (un-prefixed) → nginx had no matching location → 404. (0.3.1.111 mounted Gradio plainly at /chatbot with no root_path, which is what caused this.)

Fix, both in serve/app.py:

  • Mount Gradio at the full prefixed path with a matching root_path: gr.mount_gradio_app(app, blocks, path=root_path+"/chatbot", root_path=root_path+"/chatbot"). In Gradio 6.x, root_path governs both the browser-facing root URL and the inner ASGI app's own route matching — they must be equal or every Gradio route 404s (mounting plainly OR passing only root_path both break). With them equal, Gradio emits the prefixed root (…/tdfs4ds2/chatbot) so the browser calls …/tdfs4ds2/chatbot/gradio_api/....
  • The _strip_prefix middleware now SKIPS the Gradio mount path ({prefix}/chatbot) — Gradio owns the full prefixed path and matches it directly; only the prefix-less shell//ui/*//v1/* routes are stripped. The mount path is stashed on app.state.td_chatbot_mount.

The iframe src="chatbot" is relative, so it resolves under <base href="/tdfs4ds2/"> to the prefixed mount automatically. No nginx changes needed.

Verified via TestClient in all three modes (prefix forwarded unstripped / stripped / root): shell, /ui/*, static, and /chatbot/config (with root = …/tdfs4ds2/chatbot) all resolve.

docs.title: Fixed (reverse-proxy path prefix — landing page 404'd behind /tdfs4ds2)
docs.text:
The admin UI returned {"detail":"Not Found"} when served behind a reverse proxy under a path prefix (e.g. https://api-dmproject.myddns.me/tdfs4ds2). Two causes, both fixed in serve/app.py:

  • The app passed root_path to the FastAPI constructor, which (a) does not make routes match a forwarded-but-unstripped prefix and (b) broke the /ui/static StaticFiles Mount and the Gradio /chatbot mount. Removed the constructor root_path.
  • The prefix is now handled by a per-request _strip_prefix ASGI middleware: when the proxy forwards /tdfs4ds2/... unstripped, it strips the prefix so the (prefix-less) routes + mounts match, and stashes the external prefix on request.state so the shell injects the correct <base href="/tdfs4ds2/">. Works whether or not the proxy strips the prefix, and at root.
  • Gradio is mounted plainly at /chatbot (no root_path= kwarg) — the middleware already handles the external prefix; passing both made Gradio's internal routes 404.

Verified: shell, CSS/JS, /ui/*, /health and /chatbot all resolve under /tdfs4ds2/..., under a stripped prefix, and at the root.

docs.title: Fixed / Added (triplet identity, judge, bidirectional lineage, admin UX, resilient startup)
docs.text:
• Context graph respects the feature TRIPLET (DATA_DOMAIN, ENTITY_NAME, FEATURE_NAME) (agent/graph.py). _build_context_graph_pyvis keys feature nodes by the full triplet (_feat_node_id) and entity nodes by (domain, entity), so the same feature name under two entities (e.g. amount[provider_id] vs amount[pharmacy_id]) renders as distinct nodes (same label) instead of collapsing into one. The post-pass dedup no longer merges Feature/Entity nodes by label. _feat_cache_key now includes the active domain (DOMAIN::ENTITY::NAME) — since the domain can change mid-conversation, a cached feature from domain A is never served for domain B; _feat_cache_lookup stays back-compatible with legacy keys.
  • Within-domain entity disambiguation in name-only skills (agent/consumer_agent.py): skill_usage and skill_dataset constrain by the hinted/named entity (or return the ambiguous_feature clarification) instead of silently taking the first row; multi-feature freshness fans out per (entity, process) so a name under two entities reports both.
  • LLM-as-judge no longer scores correct counts as fabrication (agent/evaluation.py). _expected_facts for DATA_DOMAINS / SUMMARY now carries the real feature_count and feature_count_by_entity (triplet-deduped); the judge registers those numbers as fact-hits and the prompt states catalog-derived counts are real. Fixes the S17 class of straight-1s scoring.
  • Bidirectional, object-level lineage over the persisted graph: skill_lineage's primary path now positions the resolved view in FS_LINEAGE_GRAPH via _position_object_from_graph (both get_ancestors + get_descendants), so "what depends on X?" (downstream) works, not just upstream. A _lineage_direction(question) signal (EN/FR) selects upstream/downstream/both.
  • Admin UI: panels are now persistent (each rendered once into its own container, toggled by display) so switching panels no longer destroys content — the chatbot iframe is never reloaded, so conversation survives. The Evaluation panel regained skills selection, scenario table view, export, import (replace/append) and gained live per-turn SSE progress plus a radar chart + color-coded per-skill table (serve/static/app.js, serve/panels.py, serve/app.py, _ev_radar_chart_standalone/_ev_score_table_standalone in agent/chatbot.py).
  • Resilient startup + in-app Connect (serve/bootstrap.py): bootstrap() no longer raises when Teradata is unreachable — the app boots disconnected, /health reports connected, and a Connect page (top-bar Account icon) lets the user enter credentials and connect at runtime (POST /ui/connect → connect_teradata). Data panels degrade gracefully when disconnected; a status dot reflects the live state.
  • New packaged skill eval-inspect (tdfs4ds/skills/eval-inspect/): read-only stdlib query tool for an eval results JSON — by scenario id, worst N turns, intent mismatches, per-skill aggregates (with judge rationale).

docs.title: Changed (admin app — Gradio only for the chatbot; custom Teradata-styled web shell for everything else)
docs.text:
The Gradio tabbed admin app (launch_chatbot_app) is replaced by a server-rendered HTML/JS admin web shell served by the FastAPI app, reproducing the Teradata "Tera" console look. Gradio now renders only the conversational chatbot, embedded in an iframe at /chatbot. The whole UI runs on TDFS4DS_SERVE_PORT. The app supports a reverse-proxy path prefix (TDFS4DS_SERVE_ROOT_PATH, e.g. /tdfs4ds2), and for backward compatibility the standalone Gradio chatbot is also still served on its own port (TDFS4DS_SERVE_CHATBOT, default on; TDFS4DS_SERVE_CHATBOT_PORT, default 7860).

  • tdfs4ds/serve/app.py — _add_admin_ui(app) mounts the chat-only Gradio Blocks at /chatbot (gr.mount_gradio_app), serves the shell at /, static assets at /ui/static, and /ui/* JSON/HTML endpoints for the Dashboard, Lineage, Query optimization, Evaluation, Parameters and Skills panels.
  • tdfs4ds/serve/panels.py (new) — Gradio-free panel logic returning JSON/HTML; delegates to the existing module-level builders in agent/chatbot.py.
  • tdfs4ds/serve/templates/index.html, serve/static/td.css, serve/static/app.js (new) — the Teradata-styled shell (top bar + teradata. wordmark, ~280px left rail with lavender active pill + BETA badges, per-panel content area).
  • agent/chatbot.py — build_chat_blocks() builds the embeddable chat-only Blocks (Tera hero + rounded-pill input + suggestion chips); _build_chat_ui(with_skills_pane=False) omits the skills column (skills + Parameters move to the shell). launch_chatbot / launch_chatbot_with_index are unchanged.
  • Path-prefix support — set TDFS4DS_SERVE_ROOT_PATH (e.g. /tdfs4ds2); create_app passes it to FastAPI root_path + Gradio's mount, and the shell injects a <base href> so every asset/fetch/iframe URL (relative on the client) resolves under the prefix. uvicorn runs with proxy_headers=True. Example target: https://api-dmproject.myddns.me/tdfs4ds2.
  • Deployment — Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, .env.example keep ports 8000 (UI + API), 7860 (standalone chatbot) and 8080 (docs). Run with python -m tdfs4ds.serve.

docs.title: Fixed (consumer agent — compound EXPLAIN+ALGORITHM question routed to EXPLAIN only when MCP on; invented STO SQL syntax)
docs.text:
Two issues with the ALGORITHM skill when MCP is enabled:

1. Compound question routed to EXPLAIN only. A question like "How is ml_risk_score computed? What algorithm is behind it?" has both EXPLAIN signals ("how is … computed") and ALGORITHM signals ("algorithm"). With MCP enabled, the classifier anchored on the EXPLAIN shape and routed to the EXPLAIN node — the user got the SQL formula explanation but not the ML model details.

  • Routing guard (_node_classify_impl in agent/graph.py): when MCP is on and the classifier returns EXPLAIN, re-route to ALGORITHM if _looks_like_algorithm_question also matches. Symmetric with the MCP-off guard that routes to ALGORITHM_UNAVAILABLE.
  • Dual-intent execution (node_skill_algorithm): detects both signals (_EXPLAIN_SHAPE_RE
  • _looks_like_algorithm_question) and runs skill_explain first when both are present, prepending its answer above the ALGORITHM answer with a --- divider. Column sources from the EXPLAIN pass are persisted to resolved_column_sources so EXPLAIN→DEFINITION drill-down works on the next turn.

2. Fabricated CALL sto.sto(...) SQL syntax. When asked about the STO invocation or how to build a similar model, the LLM reconstructed a CALL sto.sto(PARTITION BY ..., SCRIPT_COMMAND '...', RETURNS (...)) — syntax that does not exist in Teradata. The real invocation is FROM Script(ON ... PARTITION BY ... ORDER BY ... SCRIPT_COMMAND(...) RETURNS(...) CHARSET('LATIN')) AS d inside a REPLACE VIEW.

Root cause: _algorithm_system_prompt included parsed STO fields (partition_by, order_by, script_command, returns, charset) as JSON — the LLM had reconstruction material and invented a plausible-looking but wrong call syntax.

  • _raw_ddl anti-fabrication guard (agent/consumer_agent.py): skill_algorithm stores the raw training STO view DDL as sto_invocation["_raw_ddl"]. _algorithm_system_prompt pops _raw_ddl before JSON serialisation (removing the parsed fields as reconstruction material) and injects the raw DDL as a clearly-labelled authoritative block with explicit instruction "quote the FROM Script(...) block verbatim — NEVER write CALL syntax or fabricate SQL".

Test: tests/test_algorithm_params.py::test_algorithm_system_prompt_raw_ddl_injected_and_stripped_from_json.

docs.title: Fixed (feature ingestion — CHAR entity-column length truncated; NULL in PI keys causes cartesian MERGE)
docs.text:
Two data-correctness bugs in upload_features when entity_id is passed as a list or string (type inference path):

1. CHAR entity-column length silently truncated. A source view with CLI_CD CHAR(14) produced an FS_T_* feature table with CLI_CD CHAR(1). Root cause: get_column_types (utils/info.py) applied the length-preserving path only for VARCHAR, not CHAR. The raw _td_column_names_and_types value for a CHAR column drops the length, and the FS_T_* DDL was built verbatim from that dict.

Fix: get_column_types now delegates all character columns (CHAR and VARCHAR) to get_feature_types_sql_format — the existing exact-type extractor used for feature columns. It is type-agnostic and length+charset-preserving (reads CAST(col AS <type>) from show_query()). Fail-open: if the exact extraction raises, falls back to the previous behaviour for the affected columns.

2. NULL in PI key columns causes cartesian-product MERGE / skew. NULLs in entity/primary-index columns were only handled when entity_null_substitute was passed. Without it, the MERGE ON clause used plain equality so un-substituted NULL keys never matched, causing rows to fan out (cartesian-like) with massive runtime and storage cost.

Fix: a NULL-count check runs immediately after the existing duplicate check in prepare_feature_ingestion (feature_store/feature_data_processing.py). If any entity column still has NULLs in the volatile table (i.e. after any COALESCE from entity_null_substitute), raises ValueError naming the column(s). Mirrors the duplicate-row guard exactly: same location, same log+raise pattern, same DEBUG_MODE sample query.

Tests: tests/test_entity_type_inference.py, tests/test_null_entity_guard.py.

docs.title: Fixed (consumer agent — OUT_OF_SCOPE regression on compound EXPLAIN/ALGORITHM questions)
docs.text:
v0.3.1.96's tightened ca-explain description (an adversarial "SKIP when the user asks which ML ALGORITHM …" clause) made the small classifier (ministral-14b) bail to OUT_OF_SCOPE on a compound, in-scope question under multi-turn history — e.g. "How is ml_risk_score computed? What algorithm is behind it?" (MCP on) wrongly refused, where v0.3.1.94 answered it as EXPLAIN. The conflicting EXPLAIN + ALGORITHM cues plus 9 turns of context tipped the model over.

  • Description softened (skills/ca-explain/SKILL.md): dropped the adversarial ALGORITHM-SKIP line; kept the "how/formula is ALWAYS EXPLAIN, even for ML-backed features and even when the sentence also mentions the algorithm" anchor. The ca-algorithm classifier example already disambiguates the algorithm-only case.
  • Deterministic safety net (_rescue_out_of_scope in agent/graph.py, wired into _node_classify_impl): when the classifier returns OUT_OF_SCOPE but the question names a KNOWN catalog feature/object (_known_catalog_names, word-boundary match), re-route — EXPLAIN for a how/formula shape (EN/FR), DEFINITION otherwise. Grounded in the catalog so a genuinely off-topic question (e.g. "what's the weather?") still refuses, and the algorithm-only question still routes to ALGORITHM (MCP on) / the "enable MCP" message (MCP off).

Verified live (demo, MCP on/off): the compound question answers (EXPLAIN), algorithm-only → ALGORITHM, off-topic → refusal. Test: tests/test_out_of_scope_rescue.py.

docs.title: Fixed (consumer agent — EXPLAIN "Variables involved" accuracy)
docs.text:
For rule_based_score, EXPLAIN's "Variables involved" listed 13 columns when the formula references only 5, and rendered subquery aliases (flags, base_data) as if they were tables.

  • Over-listing → transitive-closure pruning. The LLM populates source_columns from the whole DDL; _validate_source_columns (agent/consumer_agent.py) only appended, never pruned. New _formula_column_closure(calculation_sql, ddl) keeps the formula's direct variables plus the grain keys reached through their derivation (window PARTITION BY/ORDER BY and GROUP BY keys — e.g. patient_id, drug_code, claim_date behind a ROW_NUMBER()-based duplicate flag), and drops the plain arithmetic operands of an already-listed derived feature (provider_avg, amount, …). Wired into skill_explain via _prune_source_columns. Fail-open: no pruning when formula/DDL absent.
  • Alias-as-table → resolve or drop. _resolve_source_table_alias + _extract_ddl_table_aliases resolve an LLM-claimed source_table to the real base table (FROM claims_raw c → c ⇒ CLAIMS_RAW), or return None for a subquery/wrapper alias (flags, base_data, ps). Applied inside _validate_source_columns so a non-table alias never survives into result["source_columns"] — the renderer and the accumulated context graph both inherit clean data. graph.py _build_context_update additionally normalizes LINEAGE source_tables via _normalize_source_tables (drop names not in the view's FROM/JOIN set). Fail-open on missing DDL.

docs.title: Added (consumer agent — ALGORITHM: input grounding + reproduction-grade parameters)
docs.text:
The ALGORITHM skill can now describe everything needed to rebuild a similar tdstone2 process, and grounds the algorithm in what its inputs mean:

  • Input grounding (D): skill_algorithm resolves the business descriptions of the model's column_names_X inputs (reusing _explain_input_column_context) and injects them, so the explanation grounds each SQL input in its meaning. result["input_meanings_resolved"].
  • Reproduction-grade parameters (E): parses the whole TDS_MODEL_REPOSITORY.ARGUMENTS (adds sto_parameters — column typing + output_format); reads the training grain from TDS_MAPPER_REPOSITORY (model_structure: id_row/id_partition/id_fold/dataset); and parses the training STO view DDL via new _parse_sto_invocation (sto_invocation: partition_by/order_by/script_command/returns/charset). The LLM prompt (now the DB-free seam _algorithm_system_prompt) frames these as a tdstone2 HyperModel configuration recipe, grounded in the real package API. All bounded + fail-open.

docs.title: Changed (classifier clarity — DEFINITION vs EXPLAIN vs ALGORITHM)
docs.text:
Tightened the ca-definition / ca-explain / ca-algorithm SKILL.md descriptions and classifier examples so DEFINITION=meaning, EXPLAIN=formula/"how computed" (the cheap path), and the SQL-intensive ALGORITHM fires ONLY on explicit ML-model/algorithm/code/package/ parameter requests. "How is X computed / what is the formula" always routes to EXPLAIN, even for ML-backed features.

Tests: tests/test_explain_formula_closure.py, tests/test_explain_alias_resolution.py, tests/test_algorithm_params.py.

docs.title: Added (consumer agent — ALGORITHM skill: explain the ML model behind a feature)
docs.text:
A new ALGORITHM intent/skill answers data-scientist questions about the machine-learning model behind an ML-backed feature: which algorithm, which Python packages, the model code, the hyperparameters, how many partitions were trained. It is distinct from EXPLAIN (which explains the feature's SQL formula) — ALGORITHM explains the ML model.

ML features are produced by tdstone2 via a Teradata Script Table Operator (STO). skill_algorithm (agent/consumer_agent.py) walks the tdfs4ds → tdstone2 metadata chain entirely via tdml.execute_sql:

  • Resolve the feature → its process view (same resolver as EXPLAIN) → SHOW VIEW DDL.
  • Detect the STO (_is_sto_backed) and parse the scoring-mapper UUID from the TDS_STO_SCORING[_LIVE]_<uuid> reference (_parse_sto_mapper_uuid). A non-ML (SQL-rule) feature returns error=not_an_ml_feature with a message pointing the user to EXPLAIN.
  • TDS_MAPPER_REPOSITORY (ID = uuid) → trained-models table; a capped DISTINCT join (_build_distinct_model_join_sql, ≤ tdfs4ds.ALGORITHM_MAX_CODES=20) resolves ID_TRAINED_MODEL → ID_MODEL → ID_CODE + ARGUMENTS in one round trip.
  • ARGUMENTS JSON → hyperparameters + column_names_X (model inputs). The CODE blob is read only when the question is about code/packages/algorithm internals, via FROM_BYTES(CODE,'ascii') (_read_code_blob; a plain CAST(... AS VARCHAR) errors), truncated to tdfs4ds.ALGORITHM_CODE_MAX_CHARS=8000; packages via _extract_packages_from_code.
  • Trained-models summary (_read_trained_models_summary) reports partition count + STATUS. Freshness caveat: when TD_TIMECODE is the open-end sentinel (year ≥ 9999, _is_sentinel_timestamp) the exact training date is reported as unavailable — never printed as 9999-01-01. Exact package versions are not stored in metadata; the answer says so.
  • The skill returns its final answer directly (no second LLM pass).

MCP-gated availability: the ALGORITHM intent is registered in the graph/classifier only when mcp_enabled=True (build_graph adds local_intent_map["ALGORITHM"] under the gate; the existing intent filter then keeps it out of the dynamic Pydantic Literal when MCP is off). When MCP is off, an algorithm-shaped question is detected deterministically (_looks_like_algorithm_question) and answered with a fixed "enable MCP" message (EN/FR) via the ALGORITHM_UNAVAILABLE pseudo-intent.

New files/config: tdfs4ds/skills/ca-algorithm/SKILL.md; tdfs4ds.ALGORITHM_MAX_CODES, tdfs4ds.ALGORITHM_CODE_MAX_CHARS. Tests: tests/test_algorithm_{sto_parse,packages,sentinel,join_sql,gating}.py.

docs.title: Fixed (consumer agent — multi-feature DEFINITION misrouted to RECAP; "(from flags)" annotation broke lookup)
docs.text:
