A multi-feature question that LISTS named features — e.g. "what are these features: flag_provider_zscore_3 (from flags), provider_z_score (from flags), …" — was misclassified as RECAP (returning "no features in conversation context") instead of DEFINITION. The "what are these features" phrasing collides with the RECAP few-shots/the "these"-pronoun hint, and a small classifier (ministral-14b) anchors on it and discards the explicit list. The trailing "(from flags)" annotation additionally broke the exact-match catalog lookup.

Fixes (in agent/graph.py and agent/consumer_agent.py): - Deterministic RECAP→DEFINITION guard in _node_classify_impl: when the question lists ≥2 known catalog feature names (or ≥1 after a colon), re-route to DEFINITION and set object_name to the matched names. A token counts if it EXACTLY matches a catalog name OR fuzzy-matches one with high confidence (tdfs4ds.FUZZY_NAME_MATCH_CUTOFF, default 0.90), so a single typo in the list still routes correctly. Typo'd tokens are passed as typed, so skill_definition_multi's existing per-element correction emits "did you mean …?". Exact- or-high-cutoff gating keeps a genuine RECAP ("what did we discuss?") from being hijacked. - _clean_object_name extended to also strip ONE trailing parenthetical annotation (provider_z_score (from flags) → provider_z_score), applied per comma-split element at every multi-name exact-lookup node (node_skill_definition, node_skill_lineage, node_recognize_entity). Interior characters are never touched (names have no parens; composite grain claim_id,region is unaffected). - Size-adaptive fuzzy backend _best_name_similarity (the user's scale point): Python difflib for small per-turn-cached catalogs (zero DB round-trip — the consumer-agent case), Teradata in-database StringSimilarity (jaro_winkler) above tdfs4ds.FUZZY_INDB_THRESHOLD (default 500) for large catalogs. - Prompt hardening (both classifier paths + ca-definition/SKILL.md): DEFINITION now states "one or more, comma-separated; an explicit list is DEFINITION not RECAP"; RECAP states "only when no explicit names are listed"; plus a contrasting few-shot.

Verified live: the failing question now defines all 5 features (no "(from flags)" leakage), a typo'd element ("provdr_z_score") re-routes and surfaces the "did you mean" correction, a real RECAP stays RECAP, and the prior 7-turn session is unchanged. Covered by tests/test_recap_definition_routing.py and tests/test_clean_object_name.py.

docs.title: Fixed (consumer agent — EXPLAIN crashed in the streaming / chatbot path)
docs.text:
Asking an EXPLAIN question in the Gradio chatbot (e.g. "How is provider_z_score calculated? Walk me through the formula.") returned "An error occurred: 'NoneType' object is not iterable". The streaming entry point consumer_agent_stream (agent/graph.py) iterates graph.stream(…, stream_mode="updates") and did final_state.update(node_delta) for every node. The EXPLAIN path includes the no-op trace node node_pre_skill_explain which returns {}; LangGraph surfaces an empty-dict update as a None delta in updates mode, so final_state.update(None) raised and aborted the whole turn before skill_explain ran. (Pre-existing — only the streaming path is affected; consumer_agent() via .invoke always handled EXPLAIN correctly. Not a regression from 0.3.1.91/0.3.1.92.)

Fixed by guarding the merge: if node_delta: final_state.update(node_delta) — empty/None deltas are skipped (state is unchanged; the real work node runs next) while the trace step is still emitted. EXPLAIN now returns the full formula walkthrough through the chatbot, identical to the .invoke path. Covered by tests/test_stream_none_delta.py; verified live across a 7-turn session (overview → counts → search → definition → explain → compare → explain).

docs.title: Fixed (consumer agent — DEFINITION failed on markdown-quoted feature names)
docs.text:
A DEFINITION question that wrapped the feature name in markdown backticks or quotes — e.g. "What exactly is provider_z_score?" — could return "feature not found … did you mean …?" instead of the definition. The LLM intent classifier sometimes returns object_name with the wrappers kept (`provider_z_score`), and every catalog lookup is an exact case-insensitive match, so the wrapped name matched nothing and the skill fell through to the fuzzy near-miss path. (Pre-existing fragility, exposed by classifier nondeterminism — not introduced by 0.3.1.91.)

Fixed at the single chokepoint: _node_classify_impl (agent/graph.py) now passes the classifier's object_name, the explicitly-named entity_name, and each low-confidence interpretation's object_name through a new _clean_object_name helper that strips surrounding backticks / single / double quotes / whitespace only (interior characters — _, digits, and composite-grain commas like claim_id,region — are untouched, so matching is not loosened). Every downstream skill now receives a clean name. Covered by tests/test_clean_object_name.py; verified live (the quoted question now defines correctly, while a genuinely missing feature still gets the graceful "did you mean" response).

docs.title: Performance (consumer agent — collapsed N+1 query loops, per-turn SQL memo)
docs.text:
Multi-feature questions (summary, multi-feature definition, feature-group freshness, lineage) issued one Teradata round-trip per feature / entity / process / dependency node in loops, and re-materialized whole catalog / business-dictionary tables several times within a single turn. Measured live on the demo feature store, four representative turns went from 232 → 67 SQL round-trips (−71%) with identical answers (e.g. domain summary 61→7, 3-feature definition 91→18).

  • Batched business-dictionary helpers (_query_bd_columns / _query_bd_sections / _query_bd_objects in agent/consumer_agent.py) now accept list kwargs (table_names / column_names / object_names) and emit a single UPPER(col) IN (…) predicate via the shared _in_clause builder. The singular kwargs remain back-compatible.
  • _get_feature_catalog_with_docs rewritten from per-entity get_list_features + per-feature _query_bd_columns + a full all-domains process_catalog materialization (tens of round-trips) to ~3 scoped reads: one feature-catalog read, one batched get_feature_versions per entity, one scoped PROCESS_ID IN (…) map (_processes_by_id), one scoped BD_COLUMNS IN (…) read joined in pandas.
  • _build_multi_object_context, skill_lineage, and both freshness paths now issue one batched query for all views / datasets / dependency nodes / process_ids instead of looping. New follow_up_report_many(process_ids) in process_store/process_followup.py fetches all follow-up history in one PROCESS_ID IN (…) query.
  • SUMMARY no longer scans the whole BD_SECTIONS / BD_OBJECTS tables — the reads are scoped to the in-scope process views and registered datasets.
  • Per-turn SQL memo (_TURN_CACHE / _turn_cached, reset at the start of every turn in node_classify): scoped catalog / BD reads, and the unavoidable whole-store scans used for object-name resolution (_all_processes_cached / _all_features_cached), are materialized at most once per turn. Strictly intra-turn — the cross-turn accumulated_context_graph cache is unchanged.

Behaviour is preserved: same aggregates, same answers. Covered by DB-free unit tests in tests/test_agent_query_batching.py.

docs.title: Fixed (pyvis lineage graph — tables and datasets misclassified as plain views)
docs.text: FS_LINEAGE_GRAPH.PARENT_OBJECT_TYPE / CHILD_OBJECT_TYPE are CHAR(1) columns. Teradata pads fixed-width CHAR values, so the Python driver returns "T " / "D " instead of "T" / "D". The lookup {"V":…, "T":…, "D":…}.get("T ", "view") fell through to the "view" default in get_persisted_graph, making every table and dataset node appear as a plain (blue) view in the pyvis diagram. Fixed by stripping whitespace from both type columns immediately when loading rows in load_lineage_digraph.

docs.title: Added (pyvis lineage graph — legend)
docs.text: A fixed-position legend (bottom-left corner, semi-transparent white card) is now injected into the generated HTML, showing the four node types with their colors: orange (process view), blue (plain view), green (table), purple (dataset).

docs.title: Changed (pyvis lineage graph — three-tier node coloring)
docs.text:
The interactive lineage graph (plot_lineage_pyvis) previously used a single color for all view nodes, making it impossible to distinguish feature-engineering process views from plain intermediate views at a glance.

  • Three-tier color scheme in _PYVIS_COLOR (lineage/lineage_store.py):
  • Blue #6baed6 — plain / intermediate view (not a registered process)
  • Orange #e6550d — registered process view (present in FS_V_PROCESS_CATALOG)
  • Green #31a354 — source table
  • Purple #756bb1 — dataset node
  • Process-view resolution: plot_lineage_pyvis now queries process_catalog(all_data_domains=True) once before the node loop and builds a (db, obj) lookup set. Each "view" node is classified as "process" or "plain view" against that set. Degrades gracefully (falls back to plain-view color) when the catalog is unavailable.
  • Tooltip labels updated to "type: process view" / "type: plain view" / "type: table" / "type: dataset".

docs.title: Fixed (lineage graph — valid-time temporal writes, no history churn)
docs.text:
FS_LINEAGE_GRAPH is a valid-time temporal table, but record_process_lineage wrote it with a blind CURRENT VALIDTIME DELETE + INSERT of every edge on each call — so re-running on an unchanged graph closed and re-opened identical rows, inflating the validity history (observed: 46 active edges accumulating to 952 rows across repeated rebuilds). A row's ValidPeriod must reflect when an edge actually existed, not when the rebuild happened to run.

  • Diff-based writes (record_process_lineage): read the current edges for the parents in the new graph, then only DELETE edges that disappeared, INSERT edges that are new, and UPDATE the OBJECT_TYPE of matched edges whose type changed. Unchanged edges are left untouched (their ValidStart is preserved). Re-running on an unchanged graph now writes zero rows. Returns the number of edge changes (0 = no change).
  • rebuild_lineage_graph no longer wipes. The unconditional CURRENT VALIDTIME DELETE FROM <table> at the start of a full refresh was removed (it defeated the diff by making every edge look new). Pruning of edges left by since-dropped objects is now a targeted, in-memory desired-set diff (prune=True, default) that closes only the edges no longer produced by any seed — never re-opening still-valid ones.
  • Teradata syntax fix: the existing-edges read used the unsupported row-constructor predicate WHERE (a, b) IN ((..),(..)) (which threw, leaving the diff empty so everything re-inserted); replaced with a flat WHERE UPPER(PARENT_OBJECT_NAME) IN (...) + a Python (db, obj) re-check.

Verified live on the demo: rebuild from empty → 46 edges; rebuild ×2 more → 0 changes, table stays 46/46; delete one edge then scoped-refresh → exactly 1 change (re-inserted), confirming real changes are still detected.

docs.title: Changed
docs.text: • Demo notebooks (claims-anomaly-detection) now call tdfs4ds.connect(..., create_if_missing=True) so the lineage graph (and all catalog tables) auto-create on a fresh schema and the write hook always has its table.

docs.title: Added (persisted, temporal lineage graph)
docs.text:
Lineage was previously dynamic and downstream-only — every query re-derived the graph by walking SHOW VIEW / SHOW TABLE DDL, and there was no way to cheaply ask "what depends on this?". This release persists the graph so both directions are available, and drives documentation from it.

  • FS_LINEAGE_GRAPH — new VALIDTIME temporal edge table (one row per directed parent → child object edge; nodes reuse the business-dictionary identity (DATABASE_NAME, OBJECT_NAME) + OBJECT_TYPE; no PROCESS_ID on edges — an edge connects objects, not processes). Public view FS_V_LINEAGE_GRAPH. Created when missing at setup() / connect(create_if_missing=True). New module tdfs4ds/lineage/lineage_store.py.
  • Write hook — upload_features now records the process view's lineage subgraph (record_process_lineage) after the optimize/document step (non-fatal). Edges come from the existing build_teradata_dependency_graph (so feature-store technical tables are skipped and datasets are nodes); refresh is idempotent per parent object. Seeds from the view's SHOW VIEW DDL, not SELECT * FROM view — a plain SELECT has no target and yields 0 edges.
  • tdfs4ds.rebuild_lineage_graph(objects=None, all_data_domains=True, check=True) — full refresh (wipe + re-derive from every process view + dataset) or scoped refresh of just the objects you edited (no wipe; runs the impact check after).
  • In-memory query layer (networkx) — load_lineage_digraph reads the table once into a DiGraph; get_descendants (impact / "what depends on this") and get_ancestors (upstream sources) traverse in memory. Falls back to an on-the-fly build when the persisted table is empty/unavailable, so query/visualisation always works (logs a hint to run rebuild_lineage_graph()).
  • tdfs4ds.genai.rebuild_documentation(objects=None, force_update=False) — document the whole store from the persisted graph (topo-sorted leaves-first, shared visited-sets → each process/dataset documented exactly once), or re-document only the impact set of edited objects. Shares the new _document_from_graph walk with document_feature_store_incremental.
  • tdfs4ds.check_downstream_processes(objects) — deterministic EXPLAIN SELECT * FROM <view> (compile-only) on every registered process downstream of a change, so a changed view's result-column change that breaks a consumer is caught: returns {checked, ok, broken:[{view, process_id, error}]}.
  • tdfs4ds.resolve_objects(objects, require_exists=True) — identity-resolution helper: normalises "db"."NAME" / db.name / NAME (any case) and verifies against the graph then DBC.TablesV, raising on any unmatched name (no silent no-op).
  • plot_lineage_pyvis(graph, output_path=...) — interactive HTML network for the persisted graph (UTF-8 safe on Windows).
  • Opt-in cascade re-documentation — upload_features(..., cascade_redocumentation=True) re-documents downstream processes/datasets, but only when the process's own documentation actually changed (document_process_incremental now reports _doc_changed).
  • Consumer agent — impact direction — ca-lineage / skill_lineage now also answers "what depends on / consumes / would be impacted by this feature?" (new impacted_objects from the persisted graph). New developer skill fs-lineage-graph covers the admin operations (rebuild, scoped refresh, impact check, re-document, pyvis).

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text: • resolve_process_id_for_object read the process catalog via a hand-rolled CURRENT VALIDTIME SELECT … FROM FS_V_PROCESS_CATALOG, which errors on some installs (the public view already applies validtime) and returned None for every process. Now reads via process_catalog().to_pandas() — also fixes the cascade resolver.

docs.title: Fixed (dataset catalog — two registration bugs surfaced by the 0.3.1.79 split)
docs.text:
Re-running build_dataset for a flat view (ds_investigation_queue) under the corrected entity/feature split (0.3.1.79) exposed two latent DatasetCatalog bugs. Both were masked before the split fix because flat views previously had an empty dropped_entity set.

  • add_dataset deleted the features it had just inserted. The feature-cleanup loop iterated dropped_entity instead of dropped_features, so every column moved out of the entity grain by the new split was valid-time-closed in the FEATURES table seconds after being MERGE-inserted. Result: the dataset registered with the correct 2-key entity but 0 current features (verified live: 16 feature rows each living ~4 s before being re-closed). One-word fix: iterate dropped_features. After the fix, ds_investigation_queue registers cleanly as 2 entity / 16 features.
  • drop_dataset silently did nothing. (1) The guard if self.catalog[…].shape[0] == 1: read a stale snapshot bound in __init__ and had no else, so a miss dropped nothing with no log or error. (2) It only CURRENT VALIDTIME DELETEd (soft close), with no way to purge closed history. (3) The module-level drop_dataset() wrapper constructed DatasetCatalog with the default name='DATASET' → wrong tables (FS_DATASET_* instead of FS_FS_DATASET_*). Fixed: re-query the catalog view fresh, raise ValueError on a miss, add purge=True (NONSEQUENCED VALIDTIME DELETE) for a full reset, and pass name=tdfs4ds.DATASET_CATALOG_NAME in the wrapper.

Live-verified against the demo: T10 (score-direction note agrees with EXPLAIN), T12 (pharmacy_z_score surfaced), and T15 (dashboard uses the real ds_investigation_queue, CASE-orders risk_band, reports provider/pharmacy as uncovered rather than fabricating a join) all pass.

Version bump to ship the dataset-registration fix (0.3.1.79) and the consumer-agent / serve / reconnect work (0.3.1.77–78). Re-run the affected build_dataset calls to re-register datasets with the corrected entity/feature split.

docs.title: Fixed (dataset registration — authoritative entity/feature split)
docs.text:
• build_dataset now registers the entity/feature split it already knows, instead of re-deriving it from the dataset view's DDL. Dataset._retrieve_entities_and_features classified a column as a FEATURE only when the view DDL matched the canonical feature-store subquery (B1.FEATURE_VALUE AS … WHERE FEATURE_ID=… AND FEATURE_VERSION=…) and treated everything else as an ENTITY. A flat / non-canonical dataset view therefore registered 0 features with every column as an entity (observed: ds_investigation_queue). The role of a column is dataset-specific (the same name may be a feature in one process and an entity in another), so it must come from the build, not the catalog or DDL.
  • Dataset.__init__ gained optional entity= / features= parameters; when both are supplied they are used verbatim and the DDL-regex path is skipped. build_dataset computes the split via the new compute_dataset_split(entity_id, selected_features) (same column aliases the view builder emits) and fills SQL types from the built view's columns. Backward compatible: Dataset(view_name=…) / Dataset(df=…) without the split still use the regex derivation; build_dataset falls back to it if the split can't be computed.
Follow-ups from the live demo replay of the T10/T12/T15 fixes, plus a connection-resilience tool.

docs.title: Fixed (consumer agent — dashboard grounding, live-verified)
docs.text:
• Entity columns now drive the dataset cover (T15). skill_compose_dashboard read the wrong catalog column (ENTITY_ID; the live catalog uses ENTITY_NAME) so available_entities was always empty and provider/pharmacy never reached the cover. Fixed to use ENTITY_NAME, and _build_dataset_entity_keys now reads the actual ENTITY column returned by get_dataset_entity (was probing non-existent names → empty join keys). _extract_feature_list_from_question gained a deterministic entity-key fallback (scans the question for known entity stems when the LLM returns none).
  • Cross-grain identifiers handled honestly. _cover_features_with_datasets only JOINs an entity-providing dataset when it shares a key with the chosen set; different-grain stats datasets (e.g. provider_id-only) are reported as cross_grain (rendered as a grain caveat) instead of being force-joined or triggering a false "cannot compose". The dashboard now answers with the real claim-grain dataset, CASE-ordered band, and projected entity ids.
  • MCP no longer bypasses the grounded dashboard path. With MCP enabled, dashboard-shaped questions classified as DATA_QUERY previously routed to free-form MCP SQL (which fabricated columns / used ORDER BY band DESC / LIMIT). node_skill_mcp_tools now short-circuits dashboard requests to skill_compose_dashboard, and synthesize_answer routes DATA_QUERY results carrying dashboard_intent through the grounded R5 renderer. MCP-on and MCP-off now produce the same grounded answer.

docs.title: Added (Teradata reconnection)
docs.text: • tdfs4ds.reconnect_teradata() — re-establishes the teradataml context from TDFS4DS_TD_* env / .env credentials (tdfs4ds/utils/reconnect.py). execute_query now auto-detects a dropped-connection error (is_connection_error), reconnects once, and retries the query transparently; the helper is also callable manually. SQL/logic errors are not misclassified as connection errors.

Consumer-agent quality fixes from the business-user-journey eval (T10, T12, T15). New deterministic helpers replace fragile LLM/substring heuristics; all are pure and unit-tested in tests/test_agent_pure_helpers.py (no Teradata needed).

docs.title: Fixed (consumer agent — dashboard query synthesis, eval T15)
docs.text: • Grounded dashboard composition. synthesize_answer's dashboard path no longer lets the LLM pick the dataset, the ranking column, or invent a join. New pure helpers do it deterministically: _choose_ranking_metric() prefers a numeric risk/score column (inverted scores → ORDER BY … ASC) and, when only a categorical band is available, emits a CASE-expression ordering instead of an invalid ORDER BY risk_band DESC; _cover_features_with_datasets() greedily covers the requested features across datasets and derives the JOIN keys from the shared entity columns. When no requested feature is exposed, columns are reported as unavailable rather than backed by a fabricated dataset. The dataset allow-list is verified against _known_catalog_names(), and the LLM is constrained to present the Python-composed SQL verbatim.

docs.title: Fixed (consumer agent — SEARCH feature omission, eval T12)
docs.text: • Depluralizing, entity-aware token matching. skill_search's catalog pre-filter previously substring-matched question tokens against FEATURE_NAME + DATA_DOMAIN only, with no stemming — so "pharmacies" missed the feature pharmacy_z_score. New _normalize_tokens() / _depluralize() map plurals to singulars, _row_token_set() also matches the entity name, and matches are bidirectional prefix-aware (provider ↔ provider_id). Matched rows are now ranked by match strength so the strongest survive the SEARCH_CATALOG_MAX_ROWS cap.

docs.title: Fixed (consumer agent — score-direction contradiction, eval T10)
docs.text: • DEFINITION ↔ formula reconciliation. DEFINITION trusted the stored business description, which could claim "higher = riskier" while the formula inverts the score (1 - …, so 0 = highest risk) — contradicting EXPLAIN. New detect_score_inversion() (shared single-source util in genai/documentation.py) + _direction_correction_note() append an authoritative "lower = higher risk" clarification when the prose contradicts an inverted formula. The DDL fetch is gated on a cheap prose check so the cache fast-path stays O(1) for non-risk features.

docs.title: Added (HTTP server — tdfs4ds[serve])
docs.text:
• OpenAI-compatible API + LangServe playground — new optional tdfs4ds.serve submodule (pip install tdfs4ds[serve]) exposes both agents over HTTP. A single FastAPI app serves GET /v1/models + POST /v1/chat/completions where the model field selects the agent (tdfs4ds-consumer → consumer_agent, tdfs4ds-query-optimizer → query_optimizer_agent), so any OpenAI client can drive them. The query optimizer takes its SQL inline in the chat message. LangServe routes (/consumer, /query-optimizer) are mounted alongside for the interactive playground.
  • Run modes — python -m tdfs4ds.serve, or the new root Dockerfile / docker-compose.yml (docker run --env-file .env -p 8000:8000 tdfs4ds-serve). Configuration is entirely TDFS4DS_* env vars / .env; the new TDFS4DS_TD_HOST / _TD_USERNAME / _TD_PASSWORD (+ optional _TD_LOGMECH, _TD_DATABASE) open the Teradata context at startup, and TDFS4DS_SERVE_BUILD_INDEX toggles the vector-index pre-build. See .env.example and the new docs page Deployment → OpenAI-compatible API server.
  • Conversation continuity maps the OpenAI user field to the agent's thread_id (stateless per request otherwise); stream: true is supported (single content chunk + [DONE]).

docs.title: Changed (query optimizer agent — report as a link, not in the answer)
docs.text:
• OPTIMIZE answers are now a compact summary + a downloadable report link. query_optimizer_agent's OPTIMIZE path previously returned the entire Markdown report as the answer. It now writes the full report to disk (under QUERY_OPTIMIZER_REPORT_DIR/<DATA_DOMAIN>/, same naming convention as the upload_features optimizer) and the answer carries only the score delta, optimized SQL, FilterManager applicability, and a reference to the report (report_path on the skill result; the full text stays available as report_full). Benefits every caller — the OpenAI API, the LangServe playground, and notebooks.
  • tdfs4ds.serve exposes the reports for download. GET /reports lists available reports and GET /reports/{path} downloads one (path-traversal-guarded). The OpenAI /v1/chat/completions response rewrites the relative report_path into an absolute …/reports/… URL using the request's base URL.

docs.title: Added (admin chatbot app — launch_chatbot_app)
docs.text:
• launch_chatbot_app() — new additive wrapper in tdfs4ds.agent.chatbot that embeds the existing consumer-agent chatbot under a collapsible ⚙️ Parameters panel for managing, live: the feature store database (switch validated by connect(..., create_if_missing=False) — a DB without a feature store is rejected, no switch), the vector store (backend + Chroma path + a Refresh/rebuild button), and the instruct / embedding model settings (provider, model, URL, API key, proxy, dimension). Applying a data/model change rebuilds the index when needed, calls reset_consumer_agent(), rotates the thread, and clears the chat. The chat UI is factored into a shared _build_chat_ui helper; launch_chatbot / launch_chatbot_with_index are unchanged (backward compatible).
  • Served on a second port in Docker. python -m tdfs4ds.serve now also launches the admin chatbot in the same process (shared Teradata context + index), on TDFS4DS_SERVE_CHATBOT_PORT (default 7860), toggled by TDFS4DS_SERVE_CHATBOT (default true). The Dockerfile / docker-compose.yml expose 7860 alongside 8000.
  • Per-user feature-store database = one container per user. Configuration (SCHEMA + catalog globals + Teradata context) is process-wide, so the panel/DB switch applies to everyone in a process. Documented guidance: for per-user databases, run a separate container per user (same Teradata login, different TDFS4DS_SCHEMA) — no code change, no per-session isolation within a process.

docs.title: Added (outbound proxy for LLM / embedding calls — HTTP or SOCKS5)
docs.text: • INSTRUCT_MODEL_PROXY (with EMBEDDING_MODEL_PROXY falling back to it) routes the OpenAI-family LLM and embedding HTTP clients through a proxy; the URL scheme selects the protocol (http://… or socks5://…), empty = direct. Injected via an explicit httpx client (http_client= / http_async_client=) so it stays scoped to those calls and never touches the Teradata connection. socks5:// support comes from the new httpx[socks] dependency. Configurable via env / .env / JSON and live in the chatbot admin panel.
Consumer-agent reasoning fixes driven by the LLM-as-judge evaluation run of 2026-06-11 (scenarios E01, E06, E10, S01).

docs.title: Fixed (consumer agent — hallucinated lineage, eval E06)
docs.text: • Degenerate lineage cache hit — after a DEFINITION turn the accumulated context knows only feature → process_view (no upstream objects). _query_accumulated_context_for_lineage() served this as a cache hit, the LINEAGE fast path skipped the real DDL scan, and the LLM — given a dependency list containing only the process view itself — confabulated source table names. The helper now returns a cache miss when the process view is absent from source_views or has an empty depends_on, routing the question to the real build_teradata_dependency_graph scan. The fast-path LINEAGE prompt additionally forbids inventing object names and instructs the LLM to say so when no upstream is listed.

docs.title: Added (consumer agent — explicit entity short-circuit, eval E10)
docs.text:
• entity_name extraction in the intent classifier — both IntentResult and the dynamic intent model now carry an optional entity_name; the classifier prompt extracts an explicitly named entity/granularity ("total_amount for pharmacy_id"). _node_classify_impl writes it to resolved_entity_name (cleared on turns with no explicit entity), which has top priority in the _hint_entity chain — so an ambiguous feature name resolves without a clarification round-trip when the user already said which entity they mean.
  • _entity_from_question() deterministic safety net — before raising ambiguous_feature, skill_definition, skill_freshness, skill_lineage, and skill_explain scan the raw question for the candidate entity names (case-insensitive, _/space-normalised, multi-key entities match on any key component) and auto-resolve when exactly one matches.
  • Deterministic ambiguous_feature clarification — the clarification message in synthesize_answer is now a template (EN/FR via the new _question_is_french() heuristic) instead of an LLM call, which was observed embellishing the clarification with a guessed generic definition of the feature.

docs.title: Added (consumer agent — typo recovery, eval E01)
docs.text:
• Classifier rule: intent follows question shape — "what is X" classifies as DEFINITION even when X matches no known object (it may contain a typo); previously the unfamiliar token flipped the intent to SEARCH whose generic synthesis answered speculatively without touching the business dictionary.
  • Single-candidate fuzzy auto-correct — when _fuzzy_match_feature returns exactly one candidate, skill_definition and skill_freshness now answer for it directly with a correction_note ("'ml_rsk_scre' was not found — answered for the closest match 'ml_risk_score'") instead of a did-you-mean round-trip — mirroring the existing behaviour of skill_lineage / skill_explain. Multiple candidates keep the did-you-mean prompt.

docs.title: Added (consumer agent — answer verifiability, eval S01)
docs.text: • grounding_context on DEFINITION results — skill_definition returns the raw retrieved evidence (context_parts) the explanation was generated from, so the trace panel and LLM-as-judge evaluations can verify the answer is grounded rather than scoring a fully-grounded answer as unverifiable.

docs.title: Fixed (consumer agent — crashes in the 0.3.1.63 work)
docs.text:
• consumer_agent(return_context=True, return_trace=False) crashed with UnboundLocalError: trace_md; it now returns the documented 2-tuple (answer, accumulated_context_graph) (the 0.3.1.63 note has been corrected accordingly).
  • _build_context_update() crashed with UnboundLocalError: _feat_cache_key when a skill result carried a context_graph but no resolved_triplet (the import was nested inside the single-feature branch); the import is now function-level.

docs.title: Changed (consumer agent — accumulated context keying)
docs.text:
• Composite feature cache keys — accumulated_context_graph.features is now keyed by FEATURE_NAME::ENTITY_NAME (both uppercased) instead of a bare FEATURE_NAME, so the same feature name registered under two entities no longer collides in the cross-turn cache. New helpers _feat_cache_key() and _feat_cache_lookup() (in consumer_agent.py) centralise key construction and a 3-tier lookup: exact composite → FEATURE:: prefix scan → legacy plain-FEATURE key (backwards-compatible with older snapshots). All six _query_accumulated_context_for_* fast-path helpers and both skill_definition fast-paths route through _feat_cache_lookup.
  • Uppercase-keyed context sections — _merge_accumulated_context() (graph.py) now normalises keys to uppercase for the features, datasets, entities, and source_views sections for stable lookup. processes and freshness_timeline stay keyed by their process-id UUIDs.

docs.title: Added (consumer agent — EXPLAIN feeds the context graph)
docs.text: • EXPLAIN now accumulates context — _skill_explain_multi() returns a context_graph (one entry per explained feature, carrying process_id + view_name) and a resolved_triplet focus pointer; node_skill_explain merges this via _build_context_update / _merge_accumulated_context, mirroring node_skill_definition. Follow-up turns after an EXPLAIN can therefore resolve from cache. skill_explain now also captures PROCESS_ID per feature.

docs.title: Added (consumer agent — entity-scoped SUMMARY)
docs.text: • skill_summary(entity_filter=…) — a new entity_filter argument (and a new entity / data_domain recognition path in _recognize_object_type) scopes the summary to a single entity, answering "what features belong to entity X?". node_skill_summary derives the entity from conversation state and passes it through.

docs.title: Changed (consumer agent — API signature)
docs.text: • consumer_agent(return_context=True) returns (answer, accumulated_context_graph); combine with return_trace=True for the 3-tuple (answer, trace_md, acc_ctx). return_trace=True alone is unchanged ((answer, trace_md)). (Corrected in 0.3.1.64 — this entry originally claimed a 3-tuple for return_context=True alone, which never matched the docstring, tests, or code.)

docs.title: Added (consumer agent — conversation-management intents)
docs.text: • RECAP / OUT_OF_SCOPE intents now actually fire — these built-in intents were wired into the graph but had no classifier few-shot examples, so "what are the features we are talking about?" mis-routed to SUMMARY. _build_classifier_examples() now injects explicit high-confidence examples (3 RECAP variants, 2 OUT_OF_SCOPE variants) plus a contrasting SUMMARY/RECAP pair that teaches the scope-of-data boundary (SUMMARY = full catalog that exists, queries Teradata; RECAP = only what was already discussed, reads memory). build_graph() injects descriptions for both built-in intents into the dynamic Pydantic intent model and the classifier prompt.

docs.title: Added (consumer agent — entity-clarification recovery)
docs.text:
• Deterministic recovery from ambiguous-feature replies — when skill_definition returns error="ambiguous_feature" (a feature name exists under multiple entities), node_skill_definition now raises a tracked awaiting_feature_entity_clarification pending action (via the new _ambiguous_feature_pending() helper) carrying the valid entity list, the original feature, and the origin intent. The next reply is resolved deterministically by _node_classify_impl through the existing _resolve_clarification_choice (digit / ordinal / fuzzy) — the LLM classifier is short-circuited entirely.
  • Role separation + intent inheritance: the entity is taken from the reply while the feature (object_name) and intent (DEFINITION) are inherited from stored context, so mixed-concept replies ("the total_amount feature of the provider_id entity") and a bare "1" both resolve correctly. The chosen entity is written to a new resolved_entity_name state field (preferred over _focus_entity) and into accumulated_context_graph._focus_entity.
  • The ambiguous_feature synthesis prompt now renders a numbered list so a digit reply maps 1:1 to stored options.
  • Scope: DEFINITION only; node_skill_lineage keeps its existing early-return. Deferred: a general "feature X of entity Y" phrasing arriving with no prior pending state still can't role-separate (IntentResult has no entity_name field yet).

docs.title: Changed (chatbot UI — conversation management)
docs.text:
• Separate Clear and Reset buttons: The single "Clear conversation" button is now split into two distinct actions:
  • Clear Conversation — resets visual display (chat history, trace, viz panel, step indicator, message input) but preserves the LangGraph thread_id, so accumulated_context_graph and all agent memory persist. Use this to refresh the UI while maintaining context across turns.
  • Reset Conversation — resets visual display and rotates to a new thread_id UUID, starting a fresh LangGraph session with no prior context. Use this for a complete restart.
  • Gradio UI enhancement — the "Reset Conversation" button uses variant="stop" for a red/destructive visual cue to distinguish it from the safer "Clear" action.

docs.title: Changed (skills — semantic collision fixes + v0.3.1.35–37 content updates)
docs.text:
• Semantic collision disambiguation — added SKIP when … use <skill> instead clauses to 10 SKILL.md files so Claude Code selects the right skill in ambiguous situations:
  • td-explain ↔ qo-skill-explain (both analyze EXPLAIN plans; differ in output format and workflow role)
  • ca-lineage ↔ fs-lineage (both surface lineage; differ in audience and output — NL Q&A vs Python code + Sankey diagram)
  • ca-dataset ↔ ca-build-dataset ↔ fs-dataset (discovery vs agent-driven creation vs code generation)
  • ca-explain ↔ ca-definition (SQL formula → plain language vs business meaning)
  • fs-inspect ↔ fs-analyze (catalog audit vs single-process performance analysis)
  • fs-setup/SKILL.md — added Vector Store Backend optional config block: VECTOR_STORE_BACKEND, TERADATA_VECTOR_STORE_PREFIX, and all five TERADATA_AI_* globals (v0.3.1.37).
  • fs-agent/SKILL.md — added Option B (Teradata Enterprise Vector Store), AGENT_ANSWER_STYLE, and a Chart rendering section describing automatic Chart.js → Plotly conversion for teradata-mcp-server plot_* tools (v0.3.1.35–37).
  • fs-document/SKILL.md — added document_view_incremental() step, formula consistency validation columns table (MONOTONIC_DIRECTION, VALUE_DOMAIN, IS_ABSOLUTE_VALUE, FORMULA_CONSISTENCY_WARNING), and migrate_documentation_columns_schema() migration snippet (v0.3.1.35).

docs.title: Added (skills — structural completeness)
docs.text:
• .claude/skills/ expanded from 11 → 24 directories: all 10 ca-* skills and qo-skill-explain, qo-skill-optimize, qo-skill-simplify copied from tdfs4ds/skills/.
  • tdfs4ds/skills/td-analytics-workflow/ (new) — copied from global ~/.claude/skills/ so the canonical package directory is complete (25 skills total).

docs.title: Fixed (consumer agent — distinct feature count)
docs.text: • Feature counts no longer inflated by row duplicates (agent/consumer_agent.py, agent/graph.py) — a feature's identity is the pair (FEATURE_NAME, ENTITY_NAME); the same name registered under two entities is two distinct features and a feature appearing in several datasets must still count once. New shared _distinct_feature_count(df, group_by=None) helper de-duplicates on the identity pair (tolerant of ENTITY_NAME / ENTITY_ID column naming). Applied at get_data_domain() (was .size() over raw rows), skill_summary() domain-wide (total_features = len(feat_doc)), and skill_search() (was nunique("FEATURE_NAME"), which also referenced a non-existent ENTITY_ID column for the per-entity breakdown — now uses ENTITY_NAME with ENTITY_ID fallback). The two groupby("ENTITY_NAME").size().idxmax() dominant-entity picks in graph.py get the same dedup so they are robust to duplicate rows.

docs.title: Added (vector store — Teradata Enterprise Vector Store backend, opt-in)
docs.text:
• Backend selector (__init__.py, config.py) — new tdfs4ds.VECTOR_STORE_BACKEND config global ('chroma' default, 'teradata' opt-in) plus TERADATA_VECTOR_STORE_PREFIX (base name for the three named stores: {prefix}_objects, {prefix}_sections, {prefix}_columns). Registered in _FILE_VARS so .env, JSON config, and TDFS4DS_* env vars all work.
  • agent/vector_store_teradata.py (new) — Teradata backend mirror of the Chroma path. Reuses the existing backend-agnostic fetch + doc-builder helpers from vector_index.py (_fetch_objects/_sections/_columns, _objects_to_docs, etc.). build_teradata_index(force_rebuild) calls TeradataVectorStore.from_documents(name=, documents=, embedding=) (rebuild path) and tries add_documents (incremental path) with destroy + rebuild fallback. search_teradata_index(...) calls similarity_search(question=, top_k=, return_type="json") and parses results into the same six-column DataFrame schema (SOURCE, OBJECT_NAME, SECTION_NAME, COLUMN_NAME, TEXT, SCORE); the parser absorbs lists of Documents, dicts, .similar_objects objects, and JSON strings. section_names filter is applied post-hoc.
  • Backend dispatch in vector_index.py — build_vector_index and search_vector_index now branch on VECTOR_STORE_BACKEND; callers (chatbot.launch_chatbot_with_index, consumer_agent._vector_search_context, public agent.__init__ exports) are unchanged.
  • get_vector_store_embedding() (agent/embedding.py) — embedding factory for the active backend. When tdfs4ds.TERADATA_AI_API_TYPE is None, returns the existing LangChain Embeddings from get_embeddings() so vLLM / TEI / OpenAI-compatible / Bedrock setups keep working unchanged. When set, returns teradatagenai.TeradataAI(api_type=..., model_name=..., region=..., api_base=..., access_key=...); TeradataVectorStore accepts both object types at its embedding= argument.
  • New TeradataAI config globals (__init__.py, config.py) — TERADATA_AI_API_TYPE ('aws'|'azure'|'gcp'|'nim'|'hugging_face'|'onnx'), TERADATA_AI_MODEL_NAME, TERADATA_AI_REGION (aws), TERADATA_AI_API_BASE (nim), and TERADATA_AI_ACCESS_KEY (registered as a credential — .env / OS env var only; rejected from JSON config).
  • Dependencies (setup.py) — langchain_teradata was already present; teradatagenai added so the TeradataAI opt-in works out of the box (still lazy-imported so the package is optional at runtime).

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• The Enterprise Vector Store API does not yet guarantee per-id upsert, so a non-force_rebuild build attempts add_documents and falls back to destroy + rebuild.
  • Live-system items to verify when first switching to the Teradata backend: exact similarity_search(return_type="json") shape, Document.metadata round-trip, and add_documents availability. The parser and builder absorb either form; the rebuild fallback is automatic.

docs.title: Added (consumer agent — interactive charts from MCP tools)
docs.text:
• agent/chart_render.py (new) — host-side rendering of MCP chart data. teradata-mcp-server's plot_* tools return Chart.js JSON ({"data": {"labels", "datasets"}, "metadata": {...}}), not MCP-UI / MCP Apps resources. _chartjs_to_plotly_html(tool_name, spec) maps that JSON to a Plotly figure (plot_line_chart→Scatter, plot_pie_chart→Pie, plot_polar_chart→Barpolar, plot_radar_chart→Scatterpolar) and emits a self-contained HTML document with plotly.js inlined (include_plotlyjs=True) so it works air-gapped — no CDN. wrap_iframe() embeds it in a sandbox="allow-scripts" iframe (no allow-same-origin → isolated origin); charts_to_panel_html() stacks multiple charts.
  • Chart capture in the MCP skill (agent/consumer_agent.py) — _extract_chart_from_tool_output() recovers Chart.js payloads from the LangChain ToolMessage .artifact (structured_content) that langchain-mcp-adapters 0.2.2 already exposes. _skill_mcp_tools_async collects charts during on_tool_end (independent of the thread-local trace buffer — charts travel back via the return value) and returns them under skill_result["charts"].
  • Chart rendering in the chatbot (agent/chatbot.py) — new collapsible "Visualizations" panel (gr.HTML, same pattern as the context graph). Charts also render inline in the assistant's chat bubble (Chatbot(sanitize_html=False), each chart a sandboxed iframe). The panel resets per turn and on Clear.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text: • Only the Chart.js path is handled (generic ui:// MCP-UI / MCP Apps host rendering and the postMessage host↔iframe bridge are out of scope). Charts are self-contained interactive (Plotly native zoom/pan/hover); each inlined chart carries the full plotly.js bundle (~3.5 MB).

docs.title: Fixed (round-2 agent quality)
docs.text:
• Feature count wrong on non-overview SEARCH turns (agent/consumer_agent.py, synthesize_answer) — the LLM was computing its own count from context (e.g. "44 features" on entity-scoped turns) despite n_features being in the skill result. The synthesizer now quotes the exact value inline ("The total registered feature count is EXACTLY N") so the LLM has no room to invent a different number.
  • Dashboard query uses metric columns as identifiers (agent/consumer_agent.py, synthesize_answer) — when skill_compose_dashboard returns dashboard_intent=True, the synthesizer now builds a structured SQL template that places entity_keys in the SELECT's identifier columns and feature_names as measure columns, with an ORDER BY <ranking_metric> DESC clause. The LLM no longer picks arbitrary metric columns (e.g. provider_lifetime_avg_amount) for the provider/pharmacy slot.
  • Overview answers truncated to 4 sections (agent/consumer_agent.py, synthesize_answer) — post-LLM hard truncation when len(answer) > 1500 and is_overview=True. Splits on markdown headings and appends a "truncated" note. The existing soft prompt cap was ignored by ministral-14b.
  • $$ YES $$ LaTeX block rendered (agent/consumer_agent.py, EXPLAIN direct-answer path) — calculation_latex is now validated before wrapping in $$ ... $$. Strings that contain no LaTeX-ish characters (=, +, -, \, ^, _, (, ), *, /) are silently skipped instead of rendered as $$ YES $$.
  • Group-pronoun freshness empty after SEARCH turns (agent/graph.py, _capture_feature_group_from_search) — when SEARCH matches contain no type:'feature' entries (LLM emitted prose), the helper now falls back to scanning available_context for Feature: <NAME> lines and intersects against _known_catalog_names() to populate resolved_feature_list. Fixes turn-14 pharmacy freshness after a SEARCH turn.

docs.title: Added (documentation pipeline — formula-grounded documentation)
docs.text:
• _extract_interpretation_hints(sql) (genai/documentation.py) — full-text regex scan of the entire SQL string (WHERE, GROUP BY, subqueries, CTEs included) for ABS(, 1.0 -, STDDEV, PERCENTILE_CONT, SIGN(, and aggregation functions. Returns a dict of boolean signals passed to the LLM as a constrained context section. Avoids the AST-only approach which misses formulas embedded in subqueries.
  • Formula-grounded prompt (skills/qo-skill-explain/prompts/qo-document-sql.md) — upstream column docs are now "context, not authority." New FORMULA CONSISTENCY RULES section maps each interpretation_hints signal to a mandatory prose constraint (is_absolute_value → never use "signed"; is_inverted → explicitly state direction; etc.).
  • Post-validation gate with retry (genai/documentation.py, document_sql_query_columns) — after LLM returns descriptions, _find_hint_violations checks for prose that contradicts active hints (e.g. "signed" when is_absolute_value=True). On violation: one retry with explicit correction note in context. On persistent violation after retry: description prefixed with [FORMULA CONSISTENCY WARNING: ...] and logged as error.
  • migrate_documentation_columns_schema() (genai/documentation.py) — idempotent SHOW TABLE-based migration that adds MONOTONIC_DIRECTION, VALUE_DOMAIN, IS_ABSOLUTE_VALUE, and FORMULA_CONSISTENCY_WARNING columns to FS_BUSINESS_DICTIONARY_COLUMNS. Same pattern as migrate_documentation_explain_schema.
  • document_view_incremental(database, view_name, ...) (genai/documentation.py) — public entry point to document any Teradata view and its upstream dependencies, even when the view is not registered in the process or dataset catalog. Mirrors the inner loop of document_process_incremental but seeded from an arbitrary SELECT * FROM {db}.{view}. Exposed on tdfs4ds.genai.
  • Refresh notebook (notebook dev/genai/14 - Refresh Feature Documentation.ipynb) — three-cell workflow: (1) schema migration, (2) document_feature_store_incremental(force_update=True) for all catalog objects, (3) optional loop over additional non-catalog views using document_view_incremental. Includes a shared visited set so multi-seed runs don't re-document shared dependencies.

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text:
• MCP astream_events crash on multi-feature dashboard questions (agent/consumer_agent.py, _skill_mcp_tools_async) — data["output"] for inner-chain on_chain_end events is sometimes a list[BaseMessage], not a dict, so data.get("output", {}).get("messages", []) raised 'list' object has no attribute 'get' and killed the React loop. Output is now defensively unwrapped (dict, list, or other) and the top-level chain is gated via metadata.langgraph_node so intermediate tool messages no longer clobber the final answer.
  • skill_compose_dashboard ignores entity keys and partial matches (agent/consumer_agent.py) — _extract_feature_list_from_question previously returned a case-sensitive feature list only, so questions like "top 50 highest-risk claims with risk band, top driver, provider and pharmacy" matched <2 features and fell through to a literal single-feature search. The helper now returns (features, entities) (case-insensitive, parses ENTITY_ID for entity-key tokens), and skill_compose_dashboard handles the single-feature path by delegating to _skill_dataset_single with dashboard_intent=True and entity_keys set.
  • SEARCH does not populate resolved_feature_list (agent/graph.py, node_skill_search) — only SUMMARY captured the feature group, so follow-up group-pronoun freshness ("are these pharmacy features fresh?") had nothing to aggregate after a SEARCH turn. New _capture_feature_group_from_search() helper lifts feature names from skill_search matches and skill_compose_dashboard results into resolved_feature_list, and resolves resolved_entity_name when all matched features share one entity.
  • Hallucinated feature counts in overview answers (agent/consumer_agent.py, skill_search + synthesize_answer) — the LLM was counting its own bullets (52 in one turn, 125 in another for the same catalog). skill_search now emits catalog-grounded n_features and n_features_by_entity, and the synthesizer prompt instructs the LLM to quote those numbers verbatim instead of computing its own.
  • MCP React agent hallucinates feature/dataset names (agent/consumer_agent.py, _skill_mcp_tools_async) — the MCP system prompt previously listed dataset names only, leaving the React loop free to invent feature names (claim_amount_zscore_provider, ds_scoring observed in earlier runs). The prompt now lists every valid FEATURE_NAME from the catalog and forbids invented variants.
  • Lineage / EXPLAIN speculate about source-table provenance (agent/consumer_agent.py, skill_lineage + _skill_explain_multi) — when a source table has no business description, the LLM was inventing facts ("synthetic 20,000 records" vs "real healthcare claims"). Both system prompts now strictly forbid inventing facts about undocumented sources.
  • Overview SEARCH ignores AGENT_ANSWER_STYLE='standard' (agent/consumer_agent.py, synthesize_answer) — turn-1 overview answers were 1500+ words. A hard 200-word / 4-bullet-group budget is now enforced when the question matches overview cues (overview, what's available, new here, vue d'ensemble, ...).

docs.title: Added
docs.text: • Source citations on FRESHNESS and LINEAGE answers (agent/consumer_agent.py) — skill_freshness, skill_freshness_for_feature_list, and skill_lineage now emit a structured sources dict (process_ids, follow_up_method, seed_query, ddl_objects, edges_total, ...). New _render_sources_footer() in synthesize_answer appends a **Sources** — ... line that lets the user verify or escalate the claim. Suppressed under AGENT_ANSWER_STYLE='concise' to keep short answers short.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• AGENT_ANSWER_STYLE global (__init__.py) — controls verbosity of every consumer-agent response ('concise' | 'standard' | 'verbose'). Threaded into every _llm_call via a new _style_prefix() helper in agent/consumer_agent.py.
  • skill_freshness_for_feature_list (agent/consumer_agent.py) — new helper that aggregates freshness across a list of feature names. node_skill_freshness (agent/graph.py) now detects group pronouns ("these features", "the ones I just saw", "ces features") and, when a multi-feature list is remembered from a prior turn, routes the question to this aggregator instead of failing to resolve a single object.
  • skill_compose_dashboard (agent/consumer_agent.py) — new SEARCH-side composition path. When the question looks like a dashboard request (mentions dashboard / top N / SELECT and references multiple feature concepts), an LLM call extracts the implied feature names from the catalog, then re-uses multi-feature skill_dataset to find datasets exposing the whole set (or report a JOIN gap when none does). Wired in node_skill_search (agent/graph.py).
  • EXPLAIN formula completeness check (agent/consumer_agent.py) — new _extract_formula_identifiers, _extract_ddl_tables, and _validate_source_columns helpers. After skill_explain calls the LLM, every identifier in the formula is cross-checked against the LLM's source_columns list (missing ones appended with source_table='UNKNOWN') and each claimed source_table is annotated with source_confidence (high when it appears in the view's FROM/JOIN set, low otherwise).
  • _known_catalog_names helper (agent/consumer_agent.py) — returns the union of registered feature, dataset and process-view names. Used to drop unverified hits returned by the vector index and to filter LLM-generated search matches so fabricated feature names never reach the user. Dropped names are logged and returned as dropped_unverified on the skill result.
  • detect_data_domain_for_object(..., prefer_domain=) parameter (agent/consumer_agent.py) — when an object name exists in multiple DATA_DOMAINs, returns the previously-resolved one if it is among the matches. This eliminates silent domain switching on ambiguous names. node_detect_domain now passes the sticky current_resolved_domain as the preference.

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text:
• DEFINITION silently fails for near-miss feature names (agent/consumer_agent.py, skill_definition) — when the requested feature was not found in any catalog and the vector index returned nothing, the skill returned a generic "No documentation found" message. It now runs _fuzzy_match_feature first and returns feature_not_found_did_you_mean with up to 3 candidates so the synthesiser surfaces the near-matches (handles cases like provider_z_score → provider_amount_zscore / pharmacy_z_score).
  • USAGE answers list only one dataset, no SELECT (agent/consumer_agent.py, skill_usage) — extended to enumerate all datasets that expose the feature and to emit a paste-ready SELECT <entity_keys>, <feature> FROM <db>.<dataset> for each. Entity-key columns are derived from feature_catalog.ENTITY_ID so the projection is join-ready. The new datasets field on the skill result carries this information for downstream consumers.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• MCP_TIMEOUT config variable (__init__.py) — total seconds allowed for one MCP skill call covering connection, tool discovery, and ReAct loop. Default 120 s.
  • Live growing trace panel (agent/chatbot.py) — the "Agent trace" accordion now updates on every step: each graph node appends a row to a live markdown table (# | Step | Detail) instead of remaining blank until the answer arrives. MCP tool calls appear as indented sub-rows (↳ MCP · tool_name | input). The final structured trace still replaces the live table when the answer arrives.
  • MCP tool-call side-channel (agent/consumer_agent.py, agent/graph.py) — a threading.local() buffer (_mcp_trace_local) captures on_tool_start/on_tool_end events from the MCP ReAct agent; consumer_agent_stream drains the buffer after the MCP node and emits ("mcp_tool", tool_name, tool_info) events for the live trace.

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text:
• MCP calls hang indefinitely (agent/consumer_agent.py) — client.get_tools() had no timeout; agent.ainvoke() had no end-to-end timeout. Both are now wrapped with asyncio.wait_for (30 s for get_tools, MCP_TIMEOUT for the full ReAct loop). The non-Jupyter asyncio.run path was also missing a timeout; both paths now go through ThreadPoolExecutor.result(timeout=MCP_TIMEOUT + 5).
  • MCP ainvoke replaced with astream_events (agent/consumer_agent.py) — enables per-tool-call event capture for the live trace without breaking the existing answer extraction.

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
• Trace accordion open by default (agent/chatbot.py) — changed from open=False to open=True so the live trace is immediately visible without a click.
  • consumer_agent_stream step events now 4-tuples (agent/graph.py) — ("step", node_name, partial_state, step_num) replaces the old 3-tuple; step_num is a 1-based counter used by the live trace row formatter.

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text: • Redundant "Dataset availability" section on multi-feature questions (agent/graph.py, node_synthesize) — when a question asked about multiple features and mentioned "dataset", node_synthesize ran a secondary skill_dataset lookup and appended a full "Dataset availability" block even though the primary multi-object answer already contained complete per-feature dataset membership. The secondary block was suppressed when skill_result["_multi_object"] is set, eliminating the duplicate narrative.

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text: • Multi-feature questions crash (agent/graph.py, _build_context_update) — when a skill result contained a context_graph with shared_datasets stored as a list (the format returned by _build_multi_object_context), the code called .items() on it expecting a dict, raising 'list' object has no attribute 'items'. Now handles both list-of-names and dict forms gracefully.

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text:
• Graph canvas height (agent/graph.py, _build_context_graph_pyvis) — Network(height=750) was passing an integer; pyvis embeds the value verbatim in its HTML template producing invalid CSS height: 750; (no unit). Changed to Network(height="750px") so the canvas renders at the intended height.
  • Duplicate nodes in context graph (agent/graph.py, _build_context_graph_pyvis) — inconsistent node-ID schemes across code paths caused the same logical object to appear as two disconnected nodes (e.g. a dataset shown once connected and once isolated). Root causes:
  • Feature: feature_{name} (most paths) vs context_feature_{name} (multi-object context_graph path)
  • Source view: view_{name} (triplet path) vs context_view_{name} (context_graph path)
  • Entity: bare "entity" (early state path) vs entity_{name} (accumulated graph paths)
  • Dataset: raw UUID string (early state path) vs dataset_{name} (accumulated + context_graph paths) Fixed by normalizing all node IDs to a single uppercased name-based scheme throughout: feature_{NAME_UPPER}, source_view_{NAME_UPPER}, entity_{NAME_UPPER}, dataset_{NAME_UPPER}. Process nodes keep their UUID-based IDs (already unique). All edge references updated accordingly.
  • Post-processing consolidation (agent/graph.py, _build_context_graph_pyvis) — added a safety-net pass after nodes_dict is built that merges any remaining nodes sharing the same (type, label) pair. Duplicate edges and self-loops created by the merge are removed. This prevents visual duplicates even if future code paths introduce new ID inconsistencies.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• accumulated_context_graph state field (agent/graph.py) — new persistent AgentState field storing a structural relationship map that grows across conversation turns: features → {entity, domain, process_id, view_name, dataset_names}, processes → {view_name, entity_name, feature_names}, datasets → {dataset_id, database, feature_names, entity_names, process_ids}, entities → {domain, feature_names}, source_views → {referenced_by_process_ids}. Persisted via MemorySaver so it survives turn boundaries.
  • _merge_accumulated_context helper (agent/graph.py) — non-destructive deep merge for updating the accumulated structural map without overwriting existing entries.
  • _build_context_update helper (agent/graph.py) — extracts structural relationship updates from skill results (single-feature triplet path and multi-feature context_graph.features path) into the accumulated map format.
  • Context-first navigation in skill_definition (agent/consumer_agent.py) — before running the full feature catalog scan, checks if the feature is already mapped in accumulated_context_graph. If found, navigates directly to the known view via targeted BD_COLUMNS/BD_SECTIONS queries, skipping the catalog scan entirely. Trace shows ACCUMULATED_CONTEXT_MAP as a docs source.
  • Context-first navigation in skill_freshness (agent/consumer_agent.py) — checks accumulated_context_graph.features[X].process_id before querying feature_catalog → process_catalog. When process_id is already known, calls follow_up_report() directly.
  • Context-first navigation in _build_multi_object_context (agent/consumer_agent.py) — per-feature check against accumulated map; skips per-feature catalog scan and view/dataset lookups for already-mapped features.
  • Dataset membership lookup in skill_definition (agent/consumer_agent.py) — after resolving a single feature's triplet, looks up which datasets include that feature (via get_dataset_features() + dataset_catalog()), unless already present in the accumulated map. Returns resolved_dataset_id and dataset_name for graph rendering.
  • Process nodes in context graph (agent/graph.py, _build_context_graph_pyvis) — Process nodes rendered from accumulated_context_graph.processes with light sea green color (#20B2AA) and hexagon shape. Edges: Entity → Process and Feature → Process (replaces raw "Feature → View" for process views). Legend updated with Process entry.

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
• Full state for graph visualization (agent/graph.py, consumer_agent_stream) — graph visualization now reads graph.get_state(config).values (full MemorySaver-persisted state) instead of current-turn deltas, so nodes from all prior turns remain visible in the context graph.
  • Pyvis tooltip plain text (agent/graph.py, _build_context_graph_pyvis) — replaced HTML tags (<b>, <br>) in title attributes with plain text (\n separators) since vis.js renders title as plain text, not HTML. Previously showed raw HTML markup on hover.
  • Graph panel height (agent/graph.py) — increased Network(height=...) and iframe height from 500px to 750px for better visibility of complex graphs.
  • Dataset nodes from multi-feature context graph (agent/graph.py, _build_context_graph_pyvis) — now reads cg_feat["datasets"] when processing context_graph.features, adding Dataset nodes (purple triangle) with edges to their features.
  • Accumulated context written after each skill (agent/graph.py) — node_skill_definition, node_skill_freshness, and node_skill_summary all call _build_context_update + _merge_accumulated_context and persist the result in AgentState after each successful skill execution.
  • "Source view" legend label (agent/graph.py) — renamed "Computed by view" → "Source view" in the graph legend to distinguish raw/lineage views from process views and dataset views.

docs.title: Technical Details
docs.text:
• accumulated_context_graph keys are uppercased for case-insensitive lookup (e.g., features["FEAT_NAME"]); canonical casing is stored inside the value dict.
  • View name is stored directly in feature entries (denormalized from processes) so skills can navigate features[X]["view_name"] in one step.
  • Process view names label Process nodes (readable) with process_id in the tooltip; no separate "Process → View" edge since the view_name is the label.
  • Dataset views and process views are kept in separate sections; raw/lineage views (from LINEAGE/EXPLAIN traversal) go in source_views.

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
• Context graph visualization (agent/graph.py, agent/chatbot.py) — replaced static Plotly scatter plot with dynamic Pyvis network graph. Nodes are now draggable with physics-based layout, smooth edge arrows, and physics stabilization (150 iterations, Barnes-Hut gravity). Rich HTML hover tooltips on all nodes (type + name) and edges (relationship description: "entity has feature", "dataset exposes feature", etc.). HTML legend table below the graph shows all 6 node types with color swatches. Graph wrapped in <iframe srcdoc=...> for sandboxed rendering.
  • Dataset support in DEFINITION, LINEAGE, and FRESHNESS skills (agent/consumer_agent.py) — extended all three skills to handle registered dataset names as query objects:
  • DEFINITION: Quaternary path now calls _resolve_dataset() and _get_dataset_with_features() after BD_OBJECTS/BD_SECTIONS/BD_COLUMNS checks succeed, so dataset documentation is returned alongside business dictionary entries.
  • LINEAGE: New dataset branch at start of view-resolution section resolves the object as a dataset and uses dataset_name as the seed view for dependency graph construction. Returns resolved_dataset_id, resolved_dataset_features in result so graph builder can render dataset node with connected features.
  • FRESHNESS: New dedicated dataset branch (before feature catalog lookup) resolves the object, collects unique process_ids from dataset features, calls follow_up_report() for each, and aggregates per-entity freshness (entity name → feature count → last run date → status). Returns dataset-level reliability assessment.
  • AgentState multi-turn context (agent/graph.py) — added resolved_dataset_name: Optional[str] to persist the human-readable dataset name alongside resolved_dataset_id. Extended _skill_with_triplet() to propagate dataset context (resolved_dataset_id, resolved_dataset_features, resolved_dataset_name) from skill results to state, so all skills (not just SUMMARY) persist dataset context for follow-up questions. Updated node_skill_summary to also set resolved_dataset_name.
  • Dataset node rendering (agent/graph.py, _build_context_graph_pyvis) — dataset nodes now use resolved_dataset_name as the label (human-readable) instead of 8-char UUID prefix. Feature nodes referenced by resolved_dataset_features are now created in nodes_dict with proper edges. Entity nodes from dataset features are also added to the graph when available, showing the full entity→feature hierarchy within the dataset context.

docs.title: Technical Details
docs.text:
• No new temporal SQL introduced; all dataset and process catalog access reuses existing API functions (_resolve_dataset(), _get_dataset_with_features(), process_catalog(), follow_up_report()) whose CURRENT VALIDTIME semantics are baked into view DDLs.
  • Case sensitivity handled consistently: dataset name matching uses .upper() on both sides; seed SQL for lineage uses original catalog case (NOT CASESPECIFIC behavior).
  • Pyvis physics configuration: stabilization.iterations=150, barnesHut.gravitationalConstant=-8000, springLength=120, smooth dynamic edges, directional arrows.

docs.title: Added
docs.text: • Mistral AI embedding provider (agent/embedding.py) — added support for mistralai provider via langchain_mistralai.MistralAIEmbeddings. Set EMBEDDING_MODEL_PROVIDER = "mistralai" to enable. Model defaults to "mistral-embed" but is configurable via EMBEDDING_MODEL_MODEL.

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
• Vector index upsert semantics (agent/vector_index.py) — replaced _add_documents_batched with _upsert_documents_batched to enable update-if-exists behaviour. Re-running build_vector_index(force_rebuild=False) now replaces stale embeddings when documentation is updated, instead of silently skipping rows whose IDs already exist. Upsert is automatic and transparent to the user.
  • Configurable embedding batch delay (agent/vector_index.py, __init__.py, config.py) — added EMBEDDING_BATCH_DELAY global (default 0.0 seconds). Set to a positive value (e.g. 0.5) to throttle embedding batch submissions and prevent vLLM/TEI server overload / panic mode. Delay is applied between batches, never after the final batch. Override via TDFS4DS_EMBEDDING_BATCH_DELAY env var or .env file.

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text: • Embedding model token limit handling (agent/vector_index.py, config.py, __init__.py) — fix chatbot startup error "Input validation error: inputs must have less than 512 tokens" that occurs when business dictionary sections exceed the TEI server's token cap. Added configurable global EMBEDDING_MODEL_MAX_TOKENS (default 450 = 12% safety margin below 512 for intfloat/multilingual-e5-large). New _truncate_text() helper in vector_index.py truncates all text (objects, sections, columns) to max_tokens × 4 chars before embedding. Users can override via TDFS4DS_EMBEDDING_MODEL_MAX_TOKENS env var or .env file for larger-context models. Rebuild the vector index with force_rebuild=True after changing the limit.

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
• Comprehensive consumer agent documentation (docs_src/ai/consumer-agent.md) — expanded the AI capabilities guide with full LangGraph workflow diagram (10 intents + routing), graph singleton pattern explanation, AgentState as a named-entity knowledge graph (all 9 persistent fields documented), MCP integration architecture with governance guards, and hybrid retrieval strategy (structured SQL + vector fallback per skill). Documentation now covers business dictionary 3-level hierarchy (OBJECTS, SECTIONS, COLUMNS), Chroma collections, and per-skill retrieval patterns.
  • LaTeX rendering fix (agent/consumer_agent.py) — strip LLM-added $ delimiters before wrapping in fresh delimiters to prevent double-delimited $$...$$ $$...$$ that KaTeX rejects as red text. LLM now correctly renders EXPLAIN formulas despite sometimes ignoring the "no delimiters" instruction.
  • Chatbot UI improvements (agent/chatbot.py):
  • Added show_copy_button=True to enable copy-to-clipboard on all messages.
  • Fixed session context persistence: thread_id now rotates to a new UUID on "Clear conversation" so LangGraph MemorySaver checkpoint resets and cross-turn state (domain, entity, feature group) is forgotten as expected.
  • Added collapsible "Context knowledge graph" accordion showing interactive Plotly network visualization of accumulated context (domain → entity → features → views → datasets).
  • Context graph visualization (agent/graph.py) — new _build_context_graph_plotly(state) builds a NetworkX directed graph from all context fields and renders as color-coded Plotly network (blue diamond for domain, green square for entity, orange circles for features, grey squares for views, purple triangles for datasets, red nodes for column sources). Graph updates after each turn to reflect new resolutions and multi-object DEFINITION expansions. Returns None when state is empty (no context yet).

docs.title: Changed
docs.text: • Gradio trace panel now renders the multi-object context graph (agent/graph.py, agent/consumer_agent.py) — skill_definition_multi() now includes a context_graph key in its result with per-feature resolution status (found/not found, entity, view, has docs, dataset membership), shared view names, and shared dataset names. The trace panel renders a "Context Graph (multi-object)" section showing exactly which nodes were resolved and which docs were fetched, making it easy to diagnose missing documentation or wrong entity disambiguation.

docs.title: Changed
docs.text: • Multi-object DEFINITION now uses a graph-based shared context (agent/consumer_agent.py, agent/graph.py) — new _build_multi_object_context() helper resolves all requested features in a single catalog pass, then fetches shared documentation (process view sections, dataset overviews) once per unique node rather than once per feature. New skill_definition_multi() function assembles a unified context string and makes a single LLM call that sees entity, process, dataset, and per-feature descriptions simultaneously, enabling genuine cross-referencing. Features without business descriptions are clearly flagged; if nothing is documented the LLM is skipped entirely and a structured "not documented yet" response is returned. The post-hoc consolidation pass in synthesize_answer is removed — it is no longer needed.

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
• skill_definition no longer speculates when no documentation exists (agent/consumer_agent.py) — when context_parts contains only structural metadata (entity name, process view name, catalog boilerplate) with no actual business descriptions, the LLM call is skipped and a clear "registered but not documented yet" message is returned instead. This eliminates hallucinated feature explanations generated from feature names and sibling column names alone.
  • documentation_not_available error replaced with clean message — the raw error key that surfaced in the chatbot UI is now a user-readable "No documentation found for X." explanation, integrating cleanly into multi-object section output.
  • Multi-object DEFINITION consolidation pass (agent/consumer_agent.py, agent/graph.py) — when multiple features are queried in a single DEFINITION request, node_skill_definition flags the result with _multi_object=True and synthesize_answer runs a single consolidation LLM call to merge the independent per-feature sections into one coherent, cross-referenced answer. Sections for undocumented features clearly state they are not documented; the LLM is instructed not to infer their meaning from other sections. Falls back to the concatenated sections if the consolidation LLM call fails.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• DATA_QUERY intent for MCP data retrieval (agent/graph.py, agent/consumer_agent.py, agent/chatbot.py, skills/ca-data-query/SKILL.md) — the consumer agent can now route sample and aggregation requests (e.g. "show me 20 rows from DS_FRAUD", "how many claims per region?") to the MCP server when mcp_enabled=True. The new intent replaces the vague MCP_TOOLS label with a specific, actionable description that the classifier reliably distinguishes from feature store metadata intents (DEFINITION, FRESHNESS, EXPLAIN). Feature store skills always take priority; MCP is the fallback for data retrieval only.
  • Automatic MCP fallback routing — _route_intent now falls back to DATA_QUERY (instead of the first registered skill) when mcp_enabled=True and the classifier returns an unrecognised intent.
  • Process view guard preserved — _get_forbidden_objects() already blocks direct queries against process views (FS_V_*) and feature storage tables; only registered datasets are queryable via MCP.
  • Step indicator — Gradio chatbot shows "Querying data — DS_X..." during MCP execution.

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text: • Multi-object DEFINITION returns documentation_not_available (agent/graph.py) — when the user named multiple features, datasets, or a mix (e.g. "what is combined_score and risk_band?"), the classifier produced a comma-separated object_name that was passed as a single lookup key. _get_feature_catalog_with_docs did an exact match and found nothing, so all fallback paths failed. node_skill_definition now splits on commas, calls skill_definition once per name, and combines the individual explanations into one response with per-object section headers. Works for features, datasets, and mixed questions.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Real-time step indicator in Gradio chatbot (agent/chatbot.py, agent/graph.py) — the chatbot now shows a live progress label as each graph node completes, instead of a frozen interface. Labels include the argument being processed (e.g. ⏳ Looking up definition — **combined_score**..., ⏳ Detecting data domain → **MY_DOMAIN**...).
  • consumer_agent_stream() (agent/graph.py) — new generator variant of consumer_agent that uses graph.stream(stream_mode="updates") to yield one ("step", node_name, partial_state) event per completed node, followed by a ("done", answer, trace_md) event. MemorySaver multi-turn memory is fully preserved.
  • Immediate user-message display — the first yield in _respond() pushes the user question to the chatbot and clears the input box before the agent starts, so the question no longer disappears on Enter.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Entity recognition node (agent/graph.py) — new node_recognize_entity inserted between detect_domain and skill dispatch. For each named object (comma-separated), queries the feature catalog, dataset catalog, and process catalog to identify the object type (feature/dataset/ process/unknown). Stores results in recognized_entities state field.
  • Secondary DATASET intent — when a DEFINITION question contains dataset-related keywords (e.g. "same dataset?") and all named objects are features, node_recognize_entity sets secondary_intent = "DATASET". node_synthesize then also runs skill_dataset and appends a dataset-availability section after the definition answer.
  • _recognize_object_type(name) helper in consumer_agent.py — lightweight catalog lookup returning {type, entity_name, view_name, process_id}.

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text:
• Documentation lookup gap in skill_definition — when a feature description is stored in FS_BUSINESS_DICTIONARY_COLUMNS against a dataset view (not the process view), the view-scoped lookup in path 1 returned nothing. Path 1 now also runs a broad column-name-only search (COLUMN_NAME = feature_name, any TABLE_NAME) so docs are found regardless of which table name was used when they were written.
  • Catalog-only fallback — when a feature exists in the catalog but has absolutely no documentation (all BD paths return nothing, vector search returns nothing), the agent now returns what the catalog knows (entity, process view) with a note that docs are pending, instead of the generic documentation_not_available error that triggered the "reach out to data team" response.
  • No-doc error message — synthesize_answer documentation_not_available path now produces a factual "not found in feature store, verify spelling" message instead of the unhelpful "reach out to your data team" suggestion. This path only fires when the object is truly absent from every catalog.
  • MCP StructuredTool monkey-patch — _make_guarded_tools now creates proper StructuredTool wrappers instead of patching invoke/ainvoke (which raised "has no field 'invoke'" in Pydantic v2).
  • MCP context-manager — _skill_mcp_tools_async updated to the non-context-manager API (client = MultiServerMCPClient(...); tools = await client.get_tools()) required by langchain-mcp-adapters >= 0.1.0.

docs.title: Updated skills
docs.text:
• tdfs4ds/skills/ca-definition/SKILL.md — documents the new resolution chain (step 0 entity recognition, step 1b broad BD_COLUMNS fallback, step 5 catalog-only fallback, from_catalog_only return key). Adds multi-feature + dataset classifier example.
  • tdfs4ds/skills/ca-dataset/SKILL.md — notes secondary-intent triggering from DEFINITION.

docs.title: Changed
docs.text: • MCP transport is now configurable (agent/consumer_agent.py, __init__.py, config.py) — The hardcoded "transport": "sse" has been replaced by a tdfs4ds.MCP_SERVER_TRANSPORT global (default 'streamable_http', the modern MCP standard) settable via TDFS4DS_MCP_SERVER_TRANSPORT in .env or OS env. Use 'sse' for legacy SSE servers.

docs.title: Changed
docs.text: • Classifier examples moved to SKILL.md (agent/consumer_agent.py, tdfs4ds/skills/ca-*/SKILL.md, tdfs4ds/cli.py) — The hardcoded _INTENT_CLASSIFIER_EXAMPLES constant has been removed from Python. Each ca-*/SKILL.md file now contains a ## Classifier Examples section with its own few-shot examples. _read_skill_classifier_examples() in cli.py parses these sections; skill_catalog() exposes them under the "examples" key; _build_classifier_examples() in consumer_agent.py assembles the classifier prompt dynamically. Plugin skills installed via TDFS4DS_SKILLS_FOLDER are automatically included if they have a ## Classifier Examples section.

docs.title: Changed
