docs.title: __init__
docs.text:
__init__(table_name, schema_name, filter_id_name='filter_id', time_column=None, storage=None, ofs_map='TD_MAP2')

Initialize the FilterManager.

If the hidden table/view already exist, metadata (column names, maximum filter id, and time filtering status) are detected and cached. If they do not exist yet, attributes are initialized but no objects are created until load_filter() is called.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default storage

Where to create the hidden backing table when this manager (re)creates it. None (default) = system default (block storage, unchanged DDL); 'OFS' = Object File System on Vantage Cloud Lake (MAP = <ofs_map>, STORAGE = TD_OFSSTORAGE). Per-manager, so block and OFS managers can coexist in one session. Only affects table creation; reading an existing table is unchanged. NOTE: for a tdstone2-Apply view, storage='OFS' is necessary but not sufficient on its own — the ACC also rejects scalar subqueries against this filter (Error 6881); reference it via a JOIN instead. OFS + JOIN scores; OFS + scalar subquery still 6881s (see tdfs4ds.utils.storage).

None ofs_map

Map paired with STORAGE = TD_OFSSTORAGE when storage='OFS'.

'TD_MAP2'

docs.title: load_filter
docs.text:
load_filter(df, primary_index=None, time_column=None)

Load a new filter set into the hidden table and (re)point the public view at filter_id=1.

Each row in df is assigned a deterministic filter_id based on ROW_NUMBER() over the ordered set of its columns (plus BUSINESS_DATE when time filtering is enabled). If time_column is provided, values from that column are copied into BUSINESS_DATE and the view will include that time dimension.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default df DataFrame

Incoming filter definitions (one row per filter).

required primary_index list[str]

Primary index columns for the hidden table. Defaults to ['filter_id'] when omitted.

None time_column str

Name of the time column in df to map into BUSINESS_DATE. If provided, time-based filtering is enabled.

None

Raises:

Type Description ValueError

If time_column is provided but not present in df.

docs.text:
get_filter_condition(output_type: str = 'str')

Retrieve the currently applied filter condition from the database.

This method executes a SQL query that aggregates the filter columns from a filter manager view and returns the result either as a Python dictionary or a raw JSON string.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default output_type str

Format of the returned filter condition. - "dict": Returns a Python dictionary (default). - "str": Returns a raw JSON string.

'str'

Type Description

If output_type is not "dict" or "str".

RuntimeError

If the SQL execution fails.

Example

docs.title: build_dataset
docs.text:
build_dataset(entity_id, selected_features, view_name, schema_name=None, comment=None, return_query=False, feature_store_time=False, join_type='INNER', grouped=False, document=False, vector_indexing=False, record_lineage=False)

Generate a SQL query and create a Teradata view to retrieve records for the specified entity IDs based on selected features.

docs.title: Parameters:
docs.text:
entity_id : list, dict, or str The entity IDs for which records are needed. Can be a list, dictionary, or string. - If list: contains multiple entity IDs. - If dict: keys are the entity IDs. - If str: a single entity ID.

dict

A dictionary where the keys are feature table names, and the values are lists of tuples (feature_id, feature_version, feature_name) specifying the features to retrieve. NOTE: feature_version may be either: - a single UUID string, or - a list of dicts like:

str

The name of the view to be created in the database.

str, optional

The name of the database schema where the view will be created. Defaults to the global tdfs4ds.SCHEMA if not provided.

str, optional

A comment to attach to the created view for documentation purposes. Defaults to None.

bool, optional

If True, the function will return the SQL query string instead of executing it. Defaults to False.

bool, optional

If True, the query will use a specific VALIDTIME based on tdfs4ds.FEATURE_STORE_TIME. If False, the query will use CURRENT VALIDTIME. Defaults to False.

str, optional

Specifies the type of join to use between features: 'INNER' (default) or 'FULL OUTER'.

bool, optional

If False (default), uses one sub-query per feature joined together — the classic multi-join approach. Every individual feature sub-query can be targeted by a PI-equality filter pushed from the outer WHERE, making single-entity lookups single-AMP. If True, groups features that share the same storage table and process version into a single sub-query with MAX(CASE WHEN …) pivoting, reducing the number of JOINs from N_features to N_distinct_(table, version) pairs. A HAVING clause enforces non-NULL for every feature in the group when join_type='INNER', preserving the same missing-feature semantics as the classic approach. PI push-down is preserved for INNER JOIN (outer SELECT uses A1.entity); for FULL OUTER the COALESCE in the outer SELECT prevents push-down, same as the classic approach.

bool, optional

If True, generate and persist business-dictionary documentation for the newly built dataset via document_dataset_incremental (LLM-powered). Requires tdfs4ds.INSTRUCT_MODEL_PROVIDER and tdfs4ds.INSTRUCT_MODEL_MODEL to be configured; silently skipped (with an info log) otherwise. Failures are non-fatal — the dataset view is always created/returned regardless. Defaults to False (opt-in). Mirrors upload_features. Leave False when the dataset is built via upload_features/run() — that path already documents it, so enabling it here would double-document.

bool, optional

If True (and document=True and an instruct model is configured), refresh the consumer-agent vector index via build_vector_index(force_rebuild=False) after documentation so the new dataset is searchable immediately. Non-fatal. Defaults to False (opt-in).

bool, optional

If True, persist the dataset view's lineage subgraph into FS_LINEAGE_GRAPH via record_process_lineage (no LLM required). Non-fatal. A standalone dataset with no upstream processes records an isolated node. Defaults to False (opt-in).

docs.title: Returns:
docs.text: str or tdml.DataFrame - If return_query is True, returns the generated SQL query as a string. - Otherwise, returns a Teradata DataFrame containing the results.

docs.title: Raises:
docs.text: ValueError If an invalid join_type is provided.

docs.title: is_acc_ineligible_error
docs.text:
is_acc_ineligible_error(exc)

True if exc looks like a Teradata ACC-ineligibility error (6881).

docs.title: _extract_art_name
docs.text:
_extract_art_name(statement)

Return the volatile ART name declared by an INTO VOLATILE ART(<name>) statement, or None when the statement is not of that shape / can't be parsed.

Used only in UAF ART view mode to DROP a stale volatile ART before recreating it (idempotent same-session re-run). A miss is harmless — the DROP is simply skipped.

docs.title: _materialise_art
docs.text:
_materialise_art(art_sql)

Execute the UAF ART materialisation statements (art_sql) in list order.

For each statement: DROP the volatile ART it declares first (idempotent — a fresh session has none; a re-run/next-partition has a stale one), then execute the statement. All via tdml.execute_sql on the active session, so the resulting session-scoped volatile ART is visible to the ingestion CTAS that follows.

art_sql order is authoritative and must NOT be sorted/deduped — a later statement may read an ART an earlier one created. A no-op when art_sql is falsy (the classic non-UAF path). Re-raises on statement failure so the caller's followup_close(status='FAILED,...') records it.

docs.title: _compute_features_batch
docs.text:
_compute_features_batch(df, entity_id, feature_names, selected_features, primary_index, partitioning, entity_null_substitute, process_id, filtermanager, dataset_view_name, dataset_already_created, grouped=False, art_sql=None)

Execute one feature ingestion batch: followup_open → [materialise ART] → prepare → store → followup_close (COMPLETED) → optional dataset build.

Returns (features_infos, dataset_or_None) on success. On failure: logs the error, attempts to record FAILED in the followup table, then re-raises. Callers that want to continue after a failure (e.g. the filtermanager loop) should wrap this call in their own try/except and use continue rather than re-raising.

Does NOT call apply_collect_stats — the caller is responsible for that.

grouped is forwarded to build_dataset — see upload_features for details.

art_sql (UAF ART view mode only): a list of INTO VOLATILE ART statements materialised before ingestion, in this same session — so the session-scoped volatile ART is visible to the ingestion CTAS. Runs after filtermanager.update() (done by the caller), so the series-input view's scalar subquery already points at the current partition. None/empty for the classic path (a no-op).

docs.title: _upload_features
docs.text:
_upload_features(df, entity_id, feature_names, feature_versions=_FEATURE_VERSION_DEFAULT, primary_index=None, partitioning='', filtermanager=None, entity_null_substitute={}, process_id=None, force_compute=False, force_varchar_length=None, dataset_view_name=None, grouped=False, art_sql=None)

Uploads a set of features into the Feature Store for a given entity.

This function registers an entity and its associated features in the feature catalog if they are not already defined, prepares the data for ingestion, and stores it in the feature store. It also supports incremental feature computation and conditional execution depending on prior runs.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: df : pandas.DataFrame Input dataframe containing entity keys and feature columns to upload. entity_id : str, list, or dict Identifier(s) for the entity. List/str inputs have their SQL types inferred from df with exact length + character set preserved (e.g. CHAR(14) keys are not truncated); a dict is used verbatim. feature_names : list of str List of feature column names to upload from df. feature_versions : dict or str, optional Feature version(s). Default is _FEATURE_VERSION_DEFAULT ('dev.0.0'). primary_index : str or list, optional Primary index for Teradata feature tables. partitioning : str, optional Partitioning clause. Default is ''. filtermanager : FilterManager, optional If provided, features are built iteratively per filter step. entity_null_substitute : dict, optional Replacement values for nulls in entity keys (applied via COALESCE). A NULL in an entity key column not listed here raises a ValueError during ingestion (NULL keys cause a cartesian-product MERGE). process_id : str, optional Process execution identifier for follow-up logging. force_compute : bool, optional If True, forces recomputation even if already completed. Default is False. force_varchar_length : int, optional If provided, all VARCHAR feature columns are resized to this length. dataset_view_name : str, optional Name of the output dataset view to create after ingestion. grouped : bool, optional Forwarded to build_dataset whenever a dataset view is built. See upload_features for details. Default: False (classic multi-join). art_sql : list of str, optional UAF ART view mode. Forwarded to every _compute_features_batch call so the volatile ART(s) are materialised per partition before ingestion. None for the classic path. See upload_features / _materialise_art.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: pandas.DataFrame or None If BUILD_DATASET_AT_UPLOAD is enabled, returns the built dataset. Otherwise None.

docs.title: run
docs.text:
run(process_id, return_dataset=False, force_compute=False, force_varchar_length=None, dataset_view_name=None, grouped=False)

Execute a specific process from the feature store identified by the process ID. Uses global logger for diagnostics (gated by tdfs4ds.DISPLAY_LOGS).

grouped is forwarded to build_dataset whenever a dataset view is (re)built. See upload_features for details. Default: False (classic multi-join, unchanged behaviour). Note: not persisted in the process catalog — re-running an existing process_id without passing grouped=True again reverts to the classic join.

docs.text:
_split_schema_object(name)

Split schema.object into (schema, object) (both de-quoted, stripped).

Returns ("", object) when no schema qualifier is present — the caller enforces the mandatory-schema rule on the empty schema.

docs.title: _resolve_feature_source
docs.text:
_resolve_feature_source(df, sql_query=None, target_view_name=None)

Normalise an upload_features source to a teradataml DataFrame.

Accepts three input shapes and returns (df, view_name):

  • a teradataml DataFrame → returned unchanged (back-compat);
  • a fully-qualified schema.view → resolved to a DataFrame over that view;
  • sql_query + target_view_name → the view is CREATED from the SQL, then resolved to a DataFrame (the created view is permanent and becomes the process view re-run by run(process_id)).

On any recoverable problem (missing schema, view already exists, SQL/​view fails to compile or is not executable) it logs a clear, prescriptive message via logger_safe('error', …) and returns (None, None) so the caller aborts without raising a traceback (the users are non-developers).

docs.title: upload_features
docs.text:
upload_features(df, entity_id, feature_names, metadata={}, primary_index=None, partitioning='', filtermanager=None, entity_null_substitute={}, force_compute=True, force_varchar_length=1024, dataset_view_name=None, optimize_query=False, update_view=True, document=True, max_candidates=None, cascade_redocumentation=False, vector_indexing=False, sql_query=None, target_view_name=None, art_sql=None, art_layers=None, grouped=False)

Upload feature data to the feature store for a specified entity. All diagnostics go through logger_safe() which respects tdfs4ds.DISPLAY_LOGS.

The feature source can be supplied in three ways:

  1. A teradataml DataFrame (df=...) — the classic path.
  2. A fully-qualified view name (df='SCHEMA.view') — for SQL users who already have a view. The schema is mandatory; a bare view name (no schema) is rejected. The view must already exist and be executable.
  3. A SQL query (sql_query='SELECT ...') together with a mandatory target_view_name='SCHEMA.view'. The query is validated with EXPLAIN, a view is created at target_view_name (the schema is mandatory; the view must NOT already exist), and that view — which is permanent and becomes the process view re-executed by run(process_id) — is used as the feature source. When sql_query is given it takes precedence over df.

Recoverable input problems (missing schema, target view already exists, SQL or view fails to compile / is not executable) do not raise a traceback: a clear message with a fix is logged via logger_safe('error', …) and the function returns None. Check the log when a call returns None.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: sql_query : str, optional A SQL SELECT defining the feature source. Requires target_view_name. The view is created from this query (after an EXPLAIN check) and kept. target_view_name : str, optional 'SCHEMA.view' — where to create the view for sql_query (mandatory when sql_query is given; schema required; must not already exist). art_sql : list of str, optional Opt-in UAF ART view mode. A list of solitary SQL statements that materialise a Teradata UAF operator's Analytical Result Table (ART) using EXECUTE FUNCTION ... INTO VOLATILE ART(<name>). When provided, the process is registered with PROCESS_TYPE='uaf art view' and, on every run(), these statements are executed in list order, per FilterManager partition, immediately before the ingestion CTAS (same session, so the session-scoped volatile ART is visible to the CTAS). This unlocks features whose value lives in a secondary ART layer (e.g. ARIMA fit-metrics R_SQUARE/MAPE/AIC in ARTFITMETADATA, residual tests over ARTFITRESIDUALS, TD_PACF) — which a single ingestion statement cannot reach. df/sql_query+target_view_name still define the plain feature view (the one reading the layer via TD_EXTRACT_RESULTS(ART_SPEC(..., LAYER(...)))); art_sql is stored in the process METADATA and never turned into a view (a view cannot contain INTO VOLATILE ART). Order matters — a later statement may read an ART an earlier one created. For this mode optimize_query is force-disabled and the view EXPLAIN in documentation is skipped (the ART does not exist at optimize/document time). Requires art_layers. Default: None (classic 'denormalized view' path, unchanged). art_layers : list of str, optional Required when art_sql is given: the declared LAYER(...) name(s) the feature view reads (e.g. ['ARTFITMETADATA']). Used for documentation and lineage only — it is not validated against the view DDL (the view is authoritative for what is computed). Default: None. entity_id : str or list (recommended), or dict (advanced override) Entity / primary-index key(s). Pass a string for a single-column key or a list for a composite key, e.g. 'CUSTOMER_ID' or ['CUSTOMER_ID', 'EVENT_DATE'] — this is the recommended form. You do not need to specify SQL data types: they are inferred from the source DataFrame with their exact length and character set preserved (a CHAR(14) key stays CHAR(14) in the created feature table, not truncated to CHAR(1)). Only pass a dict (e.g. {'CUSTOMER_ID': 'BIGINT', 'EVENT_DATE': 'DATE'}) in the rare case where you need to override an inferred type; the supplied type strings are then used verbatim. entity_null_substitute : dict, optional Per-column replacement values for NULLs in entity keys (e.g. {'CLI_CD': '-'}), applied via COALESCE. NULL values in an entity key column that is NOT covered here will raise a ValueError during ingestion: a NULL key produces a cartesian-product MERGE (severe skew / storage blow-up). Filter the NULL rows upstream or provide a substitute. Default: {}. optimize_query : bool, optional If True and an instruct model is configured, run the query optimizer on the registered process after ingestion. The full report is saved to a file named tdfs4ds_optim_{timestamp}_{view}_{process_id}.md in the current working directory. Only the score summary is emitted to the log. Default: False. update_view : bool, optional If True and query optimization finds an improvement, replace the process view DDL with the optimized SQL. Default: True. document : bool, optional If True and an instruct model is configured, run incremental documentation on the registered process (and after any view update). If dataset_view_name is also provided, document_dataset_incremental is called after run() so that the dataset-level documentation can propagate the already-generated feature descriptions bottom-up. Default: True. max_candidates : int, optional Maximum number of SQL rewrite candidates to generate during query optimization. Overrides tdfs4ds.QUERY_OPTIMIZER_MAX_CANDIDATES for this call only. Default: None (uses the global value). cascade_redocumentation : bool, optional If True (and document=True with an instruct model configured), re-document every downstream process/dataset that depends on this process — but only when this process's documentation actually changed. The descendant set is read from the persisted lineage graph (FS_LINEAGE_GRAPH). This propagates a changed parent meaning down to everything that consumes it, at the cost of one extra LLM pass per affected descendant; hence it is opt-in. Default: False. vector_indexing : bool, optional If True (and document=True with an instruct model configured), rebuild the vector index after documentation is written so the consumer agent's search results reflect the new/updated business descriptions immediately. Uses an additive upsert (force_rebuild=False) — fast but stale entries from deleted/renamed objects persist until a full rebuild_vector_indexing() is run. Non-fatal: a failure logs a warning without aborting the ingestion. Default: False. grouped : bool, optional Forwarded to build_dataset whenever a dataset view is built (i.e. when dataset_view_name is set or tdfs4ds.BUILD_DATASET_AT_UPLOAD is True). If False (default), uses one sub-query per feature joined together — the classic multi-join approach; every feature sub-query can be targeted by a PI-equality filter, keeping single-entity lookups single-AMP. If True, groups features sharing the same storage table and process version into one sub-query with MAX(CASE WHEN …) pivoting, reducing JOIN count from N_features to N_distinct_(table, version) pairs — recommended for wide datasets with many features from the same source table. See build_dataset for the full explanation. Default: False (unchanged behaviour). Not persisted in the process catalog — a later run(process_id) without grouped=True reverts to the classic join for any dataset rebuild.

upload_tdstone2_scores(model)

Upload features from a tdstone2 model's predictions to the feature store.

Handles the full workflow: extract feature names/types, register in the feature catalog, prepare for ingestion, store in the feature store, and build a dataset view.

Returns: - DataFrame: A DataFrame representing the dataset view created in the feature store.

docs.title: roll_out
docs.text:
roll_out(process_list, time_manager, time_id_start=1, time_id_end=None, force_compute=False, force_display_logs=False)

Execute a series of processes for each date in a time manager, managing time, computation settings, and logging.

Iterates over a range of time steps, updating the TimeManager with each step, and executes all processes in process_list for that time step.

Parameters: - process_list (list): List of process IDs to execute for each time step. - time_manager (TimeManager): Object that manages time-related operations. - time_id_start (int, optional): Starting time step ID. Default is 1. - time_id_end (int, optional): Ending time step ID. If None, runs until last step. - force_compute (bool, optional): Force recomputation even if results exist. Default False. - force_display_logs (bool, optional): Force log display during rollout. Default False.

docs.text: dataset_id : str The DATASET_ID to remove. purge : bool, optional When False (default), a CURRENT VALIDTIME DELETE valid-time-closes the currently-valid rows (temporal soft delete; history is retained). When True, a NONSEQUENCED VALIDTIME DELETE physically removes every temporal row for the dataset — use this to fully reset a dataset that carries stale/closed history before re-registering it.

When None (default) views are co-located with the tables in database — byte-identical to the historical behaviour. docs.title: tdfs4ds.read_federation
docs.text:
Cross-store READ federation (v0.3.1.148).

Attach one or more OTHER feature stores to the current session in READ-ONLY mode. upload_features / documentation / lineage writes still target only tdfs4ds.SCHEMA; when read-only stores are attached, catalog / business-dictionary / lineage reads become a UNION ALL across the primary + the read-only stores, and every unioned row is tagged with a synthetic FEATURE_STORE provenance column naming the store it came from (primary first).

Empty tdfs4ds.SCHEMA_READONLY (the default) ⇒ every read funnel is byte-identical to the historical single-store behaviour — this is the same opt-in shape as the database_view / view_schema() split.

docs.title: Design notes
docs.text:
• Every persisted catalog / BD / lineage row already carries its own absolute database name (FEATURE_DATABASE / DATABASE_NAME / PARENT/CHILD_DATABASE_NAME), so a UNION ALL of each store's public views composes cleanly with no SQL rewriting — a read-only store's rows describe that store's own physical tables, so build_dataset resolves them cross-store given SELECT grants.
  • A read-only store is described by {'schema','schema_view','vector_prefix'} (see :func:tdfs4ds._ro_descriptor): schema_view is where its FS_V_* views live (what reads consult), schema is its table database (the vector target_database). A bare string 'DB' is shorthand for schema == schema_view == 'DB' with the default vector prefix.

docs.title: Public helpers
docs.text:
• :func:extend_read_stores — attach one or more read-only stores.
  • :func:detach_read_stores — detach named stores (or all).
  • :func:list_read_stores — current read-only descriptors.

docs.title: Internal
docs.text: • :func:catalog_union_sql — the shared UNION ALL builder used by every federated read funnel. Returns None when only the primary is attached, so the caller keeps its existing single-store read (byte-identical).

docs.title: _primary_view_columns
docs.text:
_primary_view_columns(view_const)

Column names of the PRIMARY store's view_const view (cached).

Used to project an explicit column list into every UNION ALL leg so the union has matching degree/names and tolerates version skew across stores.

docs.title: catalog_union_sql
docs.text:
catalog_union_sql(view_const, columns=None, provenance_col='FEATURE_STORE', schemas=None)

Build a UNION ALL over view_const across the federated view schemas.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: view_const : str The public view name (e.g. FS_V_FEATURE_CATALOG) — the SAME name in every store, qualified per leg by that store's view schema. columns : list[str], optional Explicit column list projected into every leg. When None it is discovered once from the primary view (a lazy metadata read). provenance_col : str Name of the synthetic provenance column (default FEATURE_STORE). schemas : list[str], optional Override the schema list (defaults to tdfs4ds.read_view_schemas()); primarily for testing.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: str | None The UNION ALL SQL when ≥2 stores are federated, else None — a None return tells the caller to keep its existing single-store read (byte-identical default behaviour).

docs.title: federated_catalog_df
docs.text:
federated_catalog_df(view_const, provenance_col='FEATURE_STORE')

Return a teradataml DataFrame over view_const federated across the read schemas, or None when only the primary is attached (so the caller keeps its existing single-store read → byte-identical). Convenience wrapper over :func:catalog_union_sql for the plain browse choke-points.

docs.title: _read_schemas_for
docs.text:
_read_schemas_for(capability)

View schemas (primary first) that support an OPTIONAL capability ('has_bd' / 'has_lineage'). The primary is always included; a read-only store is included only when its attach-time probe set the flag True (a store with features but no documentation/lineage is skipped so its missing view can't black-hole the whole union). Fail-open: an unprobed/legacy entry counts as capable.

docs.title: federated_bd_base
docs.text:
federated_bd_base(view_const, columns, capability='has_bd')

Return the SELECT <cols> FROM ( <UNION ALL of store VIEWS> ) t prefix for a federated business-dictionary / lineage read (the store views already apply CURRENT VALIDTIME, so the caller drops its own outer prefix), or None when only the primary supports the capability — then the caller keeps its byte-identical single-store base-table read (with its own CURRENT VALIDTIME).

docs.title: federated_from_clause
docs.text:
federated_from_clause(view_const, columns=None, alias='fed_src', provenance_col='FEATURE_STORE')

Return a derived-table FROM fragment "( <UNION ALL> ) alias" over view_const across the federated schemas, or None when only the primary is attached (caller keeps its single-store FROM → byte-identical).

Lets a domain-scoped <validtime> SELECT ... WHERE ... keep its outer valid-time prefix while the source becomes a cross-store union of VIEWS (the prefix applies at the statement level across the unioned temporal tables). The FEATURE_STORE provenance column is available inside the derived table for joins that must not cross store boundaries.

docs.title: _validate_store
docs.text:
_validate_store(desc)

Return True if the read-only store at desc['schema_view'] exposes the catalog views, is SELECT-able, and is column-compatible with the primary. Logs a prescriptive error and returns False otherwise (fail-open: the caller skips this store and keeps the good ones).

docs.title: _probe_optional_capabilities
docs.text:
_probe_optional_capabilities(desc)

Set desc['has_bd'] / desc['has_lineage'] by cheaply probing whether the store exposes the business-dictionary objects view and the lineage view. A store that lacks them still federates its catalogs; only BD / lineage federation skips it. Fail-open (unprobed ⇒ treated as capable).

docs.title: _discover_table_schema
docs.text:
_discover_table_schema(desc)

Best-effort: if the descriptor's table schema was defaulted to its view schema (bare-string attach) but the store actually uses a table/view split, learn the real table DB from a DISTINCT FEATURE_DATABASE probe so the vector target_database is correct. Fail-open — leaves desc unchanged on error.

docs.title: extend_read_stores
docs.text:
extend_read_stores(databases, validate=True)

Attach one or more feature stores to the session in READ-ONLY mode.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: databases : str | dict | list A store (or list of stores). Each is either a string ('DB_B' ⇒ schema == schema_view == 'DB_B', default vector prefix) or a dict descriptor ({'schema':..., 'schema_view':..., 'vector_prefix':...}) for a table/view split or a custom vector prefix. validate : bool When True (default), each store is probed for the catalog views + SELECT access + column compatibility; a store that fails is logged and skipped (the good ones still attach).

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: list[dict] The full read-only descriptor list after the attach.

docs.title: detach_read_stores
docs.text:
detach_read_stores(databases=None)

Detach named read-only stores (matched on schema or view schema, case-insensitively), or all of them when databases is None.

docs.title: skill_optimize_query
docs.text:
skill_optimize_query(sql_query: Optional[str] = None, process_id: Optional[str] = None, user_request: str = 'Optimize this Teradata SQL query for performance.', history: Optional[list] = None) -> dict

Analyse and rewrite a Teradata SQL query for better performance.

Either sql_query or process_id must be provided. When process_id is given, the SQL and any existing EXPLAIN documentation are pulled from the tdfs4ds process catalog, avoiding a redundant LLM call.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default sql_query Optional[str]

Raw Teradata SELECT statement to optimise.

None process_id Optional[str]

UUID of a registered tdfs4ds process. When supplied, the SQL view DDL and stored EXPLAIN analysis are fetched from the catalog. If sql_query is also given it takes precedence.

None user_request str

Optional context sentence describing the optimisation goal (surfaced in the report and fed to the LLM).

'Optimize this Teradata SQL query for performance.' history Optional[list]

Prior LangGraph messages for multi-turn context.

None

Returns:

Type Description dict

dict with keys: answer — Structured Markdown optimisation report. best_sql — Recommended SQL (original if already optimal). steps — List of {step, ...} dicts for each pipeline step. filtermanager_applicable — True if a FilterManager loop was recommended. original_analysis — Structured EXPLAIN analysis of the original query. candidates — List of candidate dicts (sql, strategy, analysis). comparison — Plan comparison result. process_info — Process catalog info dict (or None).

Raises:

Type Description ValueError

If neither sql_query nor process_id is provided.

docs.title: skill_generate_candidates
docs.text:
skill_generate_candidates(sql_query: str, user_request: str, original_analysis: dict, ddls: dict, lineage_info: dict, history: Optional[list]) -> tuple[list[dict], list[dict], Optional['StrategiesOutput']]

Strategy-based candidate generation with multi-round refinement.

docs.title: Pipeline
docs.text:
1. Plan strategies — combinatorial: one strategy per [You] item (sequential), then one per pair (simultaneous), then triplets, etc. No LLM call needed; capped at QUERY_OPTIMIZER_MAX_CANDIDATES.
  2. Execute each strategy — For each strategy sequentially: a. Generate one candidate implementing that strategy. b. EXPLAIN the candidate → extract remaining [You] items. c. If [You] items remain, refine the candidate (up to QUERY_OPTIMIZER_MAX_REFINE_ROUNDS rounds), each round targeting the [You] items from the previous round. d. Early stop the entire pipeline when a strategy achieves user_score = 5/5 (no [You] items remaining).

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: candidate_analyses : list[dict] One entry per strategy executed. Each has index, sql, strategy, strategy_description, strategy_targets, rationale, analysis, and rounds (list of per-round dicts). steps : list[dict] Pipeline step records for skill_optimize_query. strategy_plan : StrategiesOutput | None The raw plan returned by the strategy-planning LLM call (None when the query was already optimal and no strategies were generated).

docs.title: display_optimization_result
docs.text:
display_optimization_result(result: dict) -> None

Render an optimization result dict in a Jupyter notebook cell.

Displays a score comparison widget (before vs. after user/global scores) at the top, then converts the Markdown answer to HTML using the markdown package (with fenced_code, tables, and sane_lists extensions) and renders it inside a styled container via IPython.display.HTML.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default result dict

Dict returned by query_optimizer / aquery_optimizer / skill_optimize_query.

required

docs.title: skill_simplify_query
docs.text:
skill_simplify_query(sql_query: Optional[str] = None, process_id: Optional[str] = None, max_fix_attempts: int = 3) -> dict

Simplify a Teradata SQL query by removing unnecessary nesting.

Applies deterministic passes (comment stripping, identity-wrapper flattening, reserved-word alias fixes) followed by an LLM pass that merges derived-table layers into CTEs and inlines date arithmetic.

The simplified SQL is validated with EXPLAIN; if the score regresses below the original, the deterministic-only version is returned.

Can be used standalone or as a pre-step in skill_optimize_query.

Parameters:

Raw Teradata SQL to simplify.

None process_id Optional[str]

UUID of a registered tdfs4ds process (fetches SQL from catalog).

None max_fix_attempts int

Max syntax-error repair retries for the LLM output.

3

Returns:

Type Description dict

dict with keys simplified_sql, original_sql, simplified,

dict

original_score, simplified_score.

docs.title: detect_data_domain_for_object
docs.text:
detect_data_domain_for_object(object_name: str, prefer_domain: Optional[str] = None) -> Optional[str]

Return the DATA_DOMAIN that contains object_name as a feature name.

Searches the full feature catalog (all data domains). Falls back to the process catalog (VIEW_NAME) if the feature is not found by name.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default object_name str

Feature or view name to locate.

required prefer_domain Optional[str]

When the object exists in multiple domains, return this one if it is among the matches. Used to prevent silent context switches on ambiguous feature names (e.g. "Claims" appearing in multiple feature stores).

None

Returns:

Type Description Optional[str]

Matching DATA_DOMAIN string, or None if not found.

Cross-store federation limit (v0.3.1.148): the catalog scanned here is the federated UNION across the primary + read-only stores, but resolution returns a bare DATA_DOMAIN with NO store tag (and _with_domain only switches tdfs4ds.DATA_DOMAIN, not the active store). With DISJOINT stores a domain uniquely identifies its store, so this is correct. If two stores share a domain name, _pick returns the first match (primary rows sort first → primary wins); making resolution store-aware is the documented follow-up.

Give a high-level summary of available features for a domain or a specific dataset.

When object_name resolves to a registered dataset, the response is scoped to that dataset's feature list and the resolved dataset context is returned in the result so the caller can persist it in agent state for follow-up EXPLAIN turns.

When entity_filter is given (or object_name resolves to an entity type), the feature list is scoped to that single entity — answering "what features belong to X?".

User question.

required domain str

Therapeutic domain to filter by (optional).

Entity name to scope the feature list (optional).

None

docs.title: skill_lineage
docs.text:
skill_lineage(question: str, object_name: str, hint_view_name: Optional[str] = None, accumulated_context: Optional[dict] = None, entity_name: Optional[str] = None, hint_table_name: Optional[str] = None) -> dict

Return the upstream data dependencies of a feature, object, or column.

Walks the dependency graph starting from the registered process view, and returns a structured list of upstream nodes (tables and views) with their types. Does not require LLM — the graph is deterministic.

When object_name is not a feature/dataset/process view (e.g. a plain documented table, view, or a non-feature column), it falls back to the persisted lineage graph (FS_LINEAGE_GRAPH) to position ANY documented object. A column is first resolved to its owning object (TABLE_NAME) and that object is positioned instead — see _position_object_from_graph.

Implements fast-path caching: queries accumulated_context_graph first (cache hit → return immediately), falls back to expensive DDL scans (cache miss → rebuild and merge results for next turn).

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default question str

User question (used to determine response language).

required object_name str

Feature, object (table/view/dataset), or column name.

required hint_view_name Optional[str]

Pre-resolved view name from a prior turn (skips catalog lookup).

None accumulated_context Optional[dict]

The accumulated_context_graph from state (used for cache).

None entity_name Optional[str]

Entity name for disambiguation (used in cache lookups).

None hint_table_name Optional[str]

Owning object for a column (from a prior EXPLAIN/DEFINITION turn via resolved_column_sources) — positions that object directly.

None

Returns:

Type Description dict

Dict with object_name, process_view, dependencies (list of

dict

{name, type, depth}), plain_summary (LLM plain-language answer),

dict

_from_cache (bool), and resolved_triplet when resolution succeeded.

When object_name contains comma-separated feature names, explains each one.

synthesize_answer(question: str, skill_result: dict, intent: str, resolved_feature_list: Optional[list] = None, accumulated_context: Optional[dict] = None) -> str

Convert structured skill output into a plain-language response.

Parameters:

required skill_result dict

Dict returned by the skill function.

required intent str

The classified intent (SEARCH, DEFINITION, etc.).

required resolved_feature_list Optional[list]

Optional list of feature names from current/prior turns (for group context).

None accumulated_context Optional[dict]

Optional accumulated context graph with cached metadata.

None

Returns:

Type Description str

A natural language answer suitable for a non-technical business user.

docs.title: build_graph
docs.text:
build_graph(skills=None, mcp_enabled: bool = False)

Build and compile the LangGraph StateGraph.

The skill set is loaded from the ca-* SKILL.md files bundled with the package (via :func:tdfs4ds.consumer_agent_skill_catalog). Only skills whose intent is in _INTENT_NODE_MAP are registered as graph nodes.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default skills

Optional list of intent names (e.g. ['SEARCH', 'DEFINITION']) to restrict the agent to a subset of skills. When None, all installed consumer-agent skills are included.

None mcp_enabled bool

When True and tdfs4ds.MCP_SERVER_URL is set, adds an MCP_TOOLS intent node that delegates to the configured MCP server.

False

Returns:

Type Description

A compiled LangGraph with MemorySaver checkpointer for multi-turn support.

Raises:

Type Description ValueError

When no consumer-agent skills are found in the package.

docs.title: reset_consumer_agent
docs.text:
reset_consumer_agent()

Clear the graph singleton cache.

Call this after adding or removing consumer-agent SKILL.md files so that the next :func:consumer_agent call picks up the new skill set.

docs.title: get_embeddings
docs.text:
get_embeddings() -> Embeddings

Return a LangChain Embeddings instance based on the active provider.

Resolution order for each setting: - EMBEDDING_MODEL_PROVIDER -> falls back to INSTRUCT_MODEL_PROVIDER - EMBEDDING_MODEL_API_KEY -> falls back to INSTRUCT_MODEL_API_KEY - EMBEDDING_MODEL_URL -> falls back to INSTRUCT_MODEL_URL - EMBEDDING_MODEL_MODEL -> used as-is (default: text-embedding-3-small)

Injects tdfs4ds config variables into environment so that boto3, Azure, and Google Cloud clients pick up credentials and endpoints automatically.

Returns:

Name Type Description Embeddings Embeddings

A configured LangChain embedding model.

Raises:

Type Description ValueError

If the resolved provider is not supported.

docs.title: list_embedding_models
docs.text:
list_embedding_models(sub_paths: list[str] | None = None) -> 'pd.DataFrame'

List available embedding models from the configured provider.

For LiteLLM setups with multiple TEI backends (e.g. /v1/e5, /v1/code), pass the sub-path names to query each one::

list_embedding_models(sub_paths=['e5', 'code'])

Each sub-path is appended to INSTRUCT_MODEL_URL and queried for /models. Results are merged into a single DataFrame with a SUB_PATH column.

When sub_paths is None, the function derives the sub-path from EMBEDDING_MODEL_URL (if set) or queries INSTRUCT_MODEL_URL directly.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default sub_paths list[str] | None

List of LiteLLM route prefixes to query (e.g. ['e5', 'code']).

None

Returns:

Type Description 'pd.DataFrame'

DataFrame with columns: MODEL_ID, OWNED_BY, SUB_PATH (OpenAI-compatible) or

'pd.DataFrame'

MODEL_ID, PROVIDER, MODEL_NAME (Bedrock).

Raises:

Type Description ValueError

If the provider is not recognized or not configured.

docs.title: build_vector_index
docs.text:
build_vector_index(force_rebuild: bool = False) -> None

Build or refresh the three vector-store collections from the business dictionary tables.

Dispatches to the configured backend (tdfs4ds.VECTOR_STORE_BACKEND): 'chroma' (default) or 'teradata'.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default force_rebuild bool

If True, delete existing collections and re-index from scratch (Chroma backend only — no admin privileges required locally). The Teradata backend does NOT delete on force_rebuild: it always reuses/upserts into the existing collection instead, because deleting there requires admin privileges that may not be available (see vector_store_teradata.build_teradata_index's docstring). Use tdfs4ds.agent.vector_store_teradata.reset_teradata_store() directly for a true destroy-and-recreate on Teradata when you have admin rights and want stale rows purged. If False, upsert all rows (add new, update existing) on both backends.

False

docs.title: search_vector_index
docs.text:
search_vector_index(query: str, sources: list[str] = ('objects', 'sections', 'columns'), section_names: Optional[List[str]] = None, top_k: int = 10, k: Optional[int] = None) -> pd.DataFrame

Search the requested Chroma collections using similarity search.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default query str

Natural language search query.

required sources list[str]

Which collections to search (subset of 'objects', 'sections', 'columns').

('objects', 'sections', 'columns') section_names Optional[List[str]]

If provided and 'sections' is in sources, filter by SECTION_NAME.

None top_k int

Number of results per collection.

10 k Optional[int]

Alias for top_k — accepted because k is the near-universal convention in the surrounding ecosystem (Chroma, FAISS, LangChain retrievers), unlike tdfs4ds's own top_k naming. If both are given and differ, k wins and a warning is logged.

None

Returns:

Type Description DataFrame

DataFrame with columns: SOURCE, OBJECT_NAME, SECTION_NAME, COLUMN_NAME, TEXT, SCORE.

docs.title: launch_chatbot_app
docs.text:
launch_chatbot_app(port: int = 7860, server_name: str = '0.0.0.0', title: str = 'Feature Store Assistant', description: str = None, inline: bool = False, share: bool = False, thread_id: Optional[str] = None) -> gr.Blocks

Launch the consumer-agent chatbot with a Teradata-styled tabbed interface.

The layout uses a left-oriented tab nav (sidebar look) + a hero header, themed via :data:_TD_CSS (Teradata orange/navy palette). Tabs:

  • 💬 Chatbot — the conversational feature-store assistant (left) plus a right Additional skills pane that lists the plugin skills in TDFS4DS_SKILLS_FOLDER and can import/export a skill's SKILL.md.
  • 🔗 Lineage — interactive pyvis dependency graph of the full persisted lineage (FS_LINEAGE_GRAPH). Click Refresh lineage graph to load.
  • 🧪 Evaluation — generate / import / run evaluation scenarios + report.

  • ⚙️ Parameters — live admin controls:

  • Feature store database — switch the active feature store. The target is validated with tdfs4ds.connect(database=..., create_if_missing=False); a database that does not contain a feature store is rejected (no switch).

  • Vector store — backend (chroma/teradata) + Chroma path, and a Refresh/Rebuild button (build_vector_index(force_rebuild=True)).
  • Instruct model / Embedding model — provider, model, URL, API key, proxy (and embedding dimension), applied to the tdfs4ds globals.

Applying any change that affects the data or the model resets the agent (reset_consumer_agent()), rebuilds the index when needed, rotates the conversation thread, and clears the chat — so the session starts clean.

The existing :func:launch_chatbot / :func:launch_chatbot_with_index are unchanged; this is an additive wrapper.

Returns:

Type Description Blocks

The Gradio Blocks demo instance.

docs.title: tdfs4ds.agent.chart_render
docs.text:
Host-side rendering of MCP tool chart data for the consumer-agent chatbot.

The teradata-mcp-server plot_* tools return Chart.js JSON (not MCP-UI / MCP Apps resources)::

{"data": {"labels": [...], "datasets": [{"label", "data", ...}]},
 "metadata": {"tool_description", "table_name", "labels", "columns"}}

This module maps that JSON to a Plotly figure and emits a self-contained HTML document with plotly.js inlined (no CDN — works air-gapped), then wraps it in a sandboxed <iframe srcdoc> for safe embedding in Gradio.

Only the Chart.js path is handled here (generic ui:// MCP-UI resources are out of scope by design).

docs.title: tdfs4ds.agent.chatbot
docs.text:
Gradio-based chatbot interface for the tdfs4ds consumer agent.

Provides a simple web UI that non-technical users can interact with directly from a Jupyter notebook or as a standalone server.

Usage in a notebook::

from tdfs4ds.agent.chatbot import launch_chatbot
demo = launch_chatbot()          # opens inline + new-tab link

Standalone::

python -m tdfs4ds.agent.chatbot   # starts on port 7860

docs.title: _SKILL_AUTHORING_GUIDE module-attribute
docs.text:
_SKILL_AUTHORING_GUIDE = '
A **plugin skill** is a subdirectory inside `TDFS4DS_SKILLS_FOLDER`.
Each subdirectory needs at minimum a `SKILL.md` file (frontmatter + description).
Optionally add a `skill.py` with a `node(state)` function for custom logic.

---

### `SKILL.md` structure

```
---
name: My skill name          # Human-readable label shown in this pane
intent: MY_INTENT            # UPPER_CASE token the classifier emits
description: >
  One or two sentences the intent classifier reads to decide when to
  route here. Be specific: name the question shape and list SKIP cases.
argument-hint: [object name] # (optional) shown as a tooltip in the UI
allowed-tools: Read, Grep    # (optional) tools this skill may call
---

Longer explanation of what the skill does (not shown to the classifier).

## Queries
...

## Returns
...

## Classifier Examples

- "What is the regulatory status of X?" -> MY_INTENT, object_name=X
- "Quel est le statut réglementaire de X ?" -> MY_INTENT, object_name=X
```

---

### `skill.py` (optional — for custom logic)

```python
# skill.py
def node(state: dict) -> dict:
    """Called by the agent graph when intent == MY_INTENT."""
    obj = state.get("object_name") or state.get("resolved_object_name") or ""
    # ... your logic here ...
    answer = f"Regulatory status of {obj}: ..."
    # Setting 'answer' bypasses the synthesize step (no extra LLM call).
    return {"skill_result": {"answer": answer, "docs_sources": ["MY_SOURCE"]}}
```

Key rules:
- `state` is the LangGraph `AgentState` dict — read `object_name`, `resolved_data_domain`, `resolved_feature_triplet`, etc.
- Return a dict with `skill_result` key. Set `skill_result["answer"]` for a direct response (no second LLM pass). Leave it unset to let `synthesize_answer` render the result.
- Fail-open: return `{"skill_result": {"error": "my_error_code", "details": "..."}}` on errors.
- Access Teradata via `import tdfs4ds; tdfs4ds.feature_catalog()`, `tdfs4ds.process_catalog()`, or `import teradataml as tdml; tdml.DataFrame.from_query(...)`.

---

### Minimal working example

Directory layout:
```
TDFS4DS_SKILLS_FOLDER/
└── ca-status/
    ├── SKILL.md
    └── skill.py        ← optional
```

`ca-status/SKILL.md`:
```
---
name: Regulatory status
intent: STATUS
description: >
  User asks about the regulatory or compliance status of a feature or dataset.
  SKIP when the user asks for a definition or formula — use ca-definition or ca-explain.
---

## Classifier Examples

- "Is total_amount compliant with GDPR?" -> STATUS, object_name=total_amount
```

`ca-status/skill.py`:
```python
def node(state):
    obj = state.get("object_name") or ""
    return {"skill_result": {"answer": f"No compliance record found for '{obj}'.",
                              "docs_sources": []}}
```

After saving, click **Refresh list** and the new skill appears. The agent will route matching questions to it automatically.
'

docs.title: consumer_agent
docs.text:
consumer_agent(question: str, thread_id: str = 'default', return_trace: bool = False, return_context: bool = False, skills: Optional[List[str]] = None, mcp_enabled: bool = False) -> Union[str, tuple]

Run the LangGraph agent for a single conversational turn.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default question str

User question in natural language (French or English).

required thread_id str

Conversation thread ID for multi-turn memory.

'default' return_trace bool

When True, includes trace_md in the return tuple.

False return_context bool

When True, includes accumulated_context_graph in the return tuple.

False skills Optional[List[str]]

Optional list of intent names (e.g. ['SEARCH', 'DEFINITION']) to restrict the agent to a subset of skills. When None (default), all installed consumer-agent skills are used. Threads are isolated per skill subset — mixing skills values in the same thread_id is not recommended.

None mcp_enabled bool

When True and tdfs4ds.MCP_SERVER_URL is set, the agent can route questions to the MCP server's tools. The toggle changes the graph variant (different cache key) so it is safe to flip mid-conversation — but note that conversation history is not shared between the MCP-on and MCP-off graph instances.

False

Returns:

Type Description Union[str, tuple]
  • answer: str when both return_trace and return_context are False (default).
Union[str, tuple]
  • (answer, trace_md) when return_trace=True and return_context=False.
Union[str, tuple]
  • (answer, accumulated_context_graph) when return_trace=False and return_context=True.
Union[str, tuple]
  • (answer, trace_md, accumulated_context_graph) when both are True.

docs.title: consumer_agent_stream
docs.text:
consumer_agent_stream(question: str, thread_id: str = 'default', skills: Optional[List[str]] = None, mcp_enabled: bool = False)

Generator variant of consumer_agent for real-time step streaming.

Yields:

Type Description

("step", node_name, partial_state) after each graph node completes,

where partial_state is the state accumulated so far (all fields set by

nodes that have already run).

("done", answer, trace_md) as the final event with the complete result.

The MemorySaver checkpointer behaves identically with .stream() as with .invoke() — multi-turn memory is fully preserved.

docs.title: _build_context_graph_pyvis
docs.text:
_build_context_graph_pyvis(state: dict) -> Optional[str]

Build an interactive Pyvis network graph from the agent's context state.

Visualises the accumulated context as a knowledge graph with: - Draggable nodes with physics-based layout - Rich hover tooltips for nodes (type + name) and edges (relationship description) - HTML legend showing all 6 node types

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default state dict

Final state dict from the graph.

required

Returns:

Type Description Optional[str]

HTML string wrapping an iframe with the graph + legend, or None if no context/pyvis unavailable.

docs.title: build_vector_index
docs.text:
build_vector_index(force_rebuild: bool = False) -> None

Build or refresh the three vector-store collections from the business dictionary tables.

Dispatches to the configured backend (tdfs4ds.VECTOR_STORE_BACKEND): 'chroma' (default) or 'teradata'.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default force_rebuild bool

If True, delete existing collections and re-index from scratch (Chroma backend only — no admin privileges required locally). The Teradata backend does NOT delete on force_rebuild: it always reuses/upserts into the existing collection instead, because deleting there requires admin privileges that may not be available (see vector_store_teradata.build_teradata_index's docstring). Use tdfs4ds.agent.vector_store_teradata.reset_teradata_store() directly for a true destroy-and-recreate on Teradata when you have admin rights and want stale rows purged. If False, upsert all rows (add new, update existing) on both backends.

False

Build the chat components + wiring inside the current gr.Blocks context.

When with_skills_pane is False the right-hand "Additional skills" column is omitted (used by :func:build_chat_blocks, where skills are managed by the surrounding FastAPI shell). The returned dict then carries None for every skills_* key.

Must be called while a gr.Blocks (or layout) context is active. Returns a dict of the components an embedding app may need to drive from the outside (the chatbot, trace, viz panel, step indicator, thread-id state, input box).

The behaviour here is identical to the pre-refactor inline body of :func:launch_chatbot — extracted verbatim so both launch_chatbot and launch_chatbot_app share one implementation.

docs.title: _md_to_html
docs.text:
_md_to_html(md: str, *, inline: bool = False, with_css: bool = True) -> str

Render a Markdown string to styled HTML using the real markdown package.

Handles GFM pipe tables, fenced code, inline code, bold/italic and lists — unlike the various hand-rolled regex renderers it replaces. Scoped via the .td-md class. inline=True keeps short single-line content (e.g. a warning item or a judge rationale cell) from forcing a block paragraph. with_css=False omits the <style> block when the caller emits it once for many fragments. Fail-open: returns an escaped <pre> on any error.

Reads the follow-up log for all processes (bounded window: most recent 50 runs per process or last 90 days), then computes stats. Fail-open: returns None for figures when data is missing.

docs.title: _dash_storage_counts
docs.text:
_dash_storage_counts(feat_df, *, domains=None, processes=None, pid_to_tables=None, emit=None)

Per-table CURRENT VALIDTIME + NONSEQUENCED VALIDTIME row counts (+ DBC size).

Refactored core of the live row-count pass (formerly inlined in _dash_compute_counts). Fast on a healthy store (stats up to date, PI + partitions). Optionally scoped to a list of domains and/or to the feature tables of a list of processes (pid_to_tables maps PROCESS_ID → set of "DB.TABLE" the process writes to). emit is a callable(str) per-table progress hook; structured PROGRESS k/N lines drive the SSE bar.

Returns a pandas DataFrame with columns: Database, Table, Domains, Entities, # Features, CurrentRows, NonseqRows, HistoryRows, SizeBytes. Fail-open per table (missing counts → None).

docs.title: _dash_orphans
docs.text:
_dash_orphans(feat_df, *, emit=None)

Detect FEATURE_IDs present in an FS_T_* table but absent from the catalog.

For each storage table, the catalog assigns a set of FEATURE_IDs; any DISTINCT FEATURE_ID physically in the table but not in that set is an orphan (a feature that was de-registered without purging its stored rows). Read-only. Returns (status_msg, plotly_table_fig_or_None, orphan_rows_list).

docs.title: launch_chatbot_app
docs.text:
launch_chatbot_app(port: int = 7860, server_name: str = '0.0.0.0', title: str = 'Feature Store Assistant', description: str = None, inline: bool = False, share: bool = False, thread_id: Optional[str] = None) -> gr.Blocks

Launch the consumer-agent chatbot with a Teradata-styled tabbed interface.

The layout uses a left-oriented tab nav (sidebar look) + a hero header, themed via :data:_TD_CSS (Teradata orange/navy palette). Tabs:

  • 💬 Chatbot — the conversational feature-store assistant (left) plus a right Additional skills pane that lists the plugin skills in TDFS4DS_SKILLS_FOLDER and can import/export a skill's SKILL.md.
  • 🔗 Lineage — interactive pyvis dependency graph of the full persisted lineage (FS_LINEAGE_GRAPH). Click Refresh lineage graph to load.
  • 🧪 Evaluation — generate / import / run evaluation scenarios + report.

  • ⚙️ Parameters — live admin controls:

  • Feature store database — switch the active feature store. The target is validated with tdfs4ds.connect(database=..., create_if_missing=False); a database that does not contain a feature store is rejected (no switch).

  • Vector store — backend (chroma/teradata) + Chroma path, and a Refresh/Rebuild button (build_vector_index(force_rebuild=True)).
  • Instruct model / Embedding model — provider, model, URL, API key, proxy (and embedding dimension), applied to the tdfs4ds globals.

Applying any change that affects the data or the model resets the agent (reset_consumer_agent()), rebuilds the index when needed, rotates the conversation thread, and clears the chat — so the session starts clean.

The existing :func:launch_chatbot / :func:launch_chatbot_with_index are unchanged; this is an additive wrapper.

Returns:

Type Description Blocks

The Gradio Blocks demo instance.

docs.title: tdfs4ds.agent.consumer_agent
docs.text:
Consumer agent skills, intent classifier, and answer synthesizer.

Business consumers (data managers, study managers, regulatory affairs) interact with the feature store in natural language. This module provides: - intent_classifier — routes questions to the right skill - Five skills: search, definition, usage, freshness, summary - synthesize_answer — converts structured skill output to plain language

docs.title: _STYLE_DIRECTIVES module-attribute
docs.text: _STYLE_DIRECTIVES = {'concise': 'Style directive: Be terse. 3-6 short bullets or two compact sentences. No analogies, no preamble, no closing summary. Skip narrative framing.', 'standard': None, 'verbose': 'Style directive: Long-form is allowed. Use sectioned bullets, analogies, and worked examples when they help the reader.'}

docs.title: _GROUNDING_DIRECTIVE module-attribute
docs.text: _GROUNDING_DIRECTIVE = 'GROUNDING RULE: Ground all claims strictly in the documentation and catalog information provided. You MAY volunteer a helpful interpretation or idea beyond what is documented, but you MUST explicitly label it — prefix it with "Not documented — interpretation:" (in English) or "Idée non documentée :" (in French) — so the reader can tell a stored fact from an inference. NEVER present an inference as a documented fact. Omit sections (e.g. Inclusion/Exclusion Criteria, Interpretation) entirely when the provided documentation contains no content for them — do not write placeholder text like "not documented" for every structural section.'

docs.title: calculation_sql class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text: calculation_sql: Optional[str] = Field(None, description="The SQL expression that computes this specific feature, extracted verbatim from the SELECT list. If the outer SELECT merely passes through an alias defined in a subquery, drill down into that subquery to find the real computation — repeat for every nesting level until the actual arithmetic or aggregation is found. Include only the expression itself (e.g. 'CAST(CURRENT_DATE - MAX(transaction_date) AS INT)'), never the full query. Return null only when the expression genuinely cannot be resolved at any nesting level.")

docs.title: calculation_latex class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text: calculation_latex: Optional[str] = Field(None, description="LaTeX representation of the calculation in display-math notation, WITHOUT surrounding delimiters. Populate ONLY when the user explicitly requested a formula, equation, or LaTeX. Use \\text{} for business names and standard LaTeX for operators (\\max, \\sum, \\frac, \\cdot, etc.). Example: r'\\text{nb\\_days} = \\text{TODAY} - \\max(\\text{transaction\\_date})'. Return null when not requested or when the expression is too complex to render meaningfully as a single formula.")

docs.title: source_columns class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text: source_columns: Optional[list[SourceColumnRef]] = Field(None, description="For every distinct column or alias referenced in the feature's calculation expression, provide its bare name and the source table or view it originates from (resolve through subquery aliases to the real base table/view wherever possible). Bare names only — no schema prefix. Omit entity-key columns (e.g. primary-key columns). Return null if the formula contains no identifiable column references.")

docs.title: logger_safe

logger_safe(level, message, *args, **kwargs)

Wrapper around the global logger that only emits logs when tdfs4ds.DISPLAY_LOGS is True. level is a string like "info", "error", etc.

docs.title: follow_up_report
docs.text:
follow_up_report(filtermanager: Optional[object] = None, process_id: Optional[str] = None)

Return a follow-up report as a tdml.DataFrame, optionally filtered by process_id and/or a filtermanager's applied filter.

Behavior by arguments
  • process_id is None and filtermanager is None: Return all rows from SCHEMA.FOLLOW_UP_NAME, sorted by START_DATETIME desc.
  • process_id is not None and filtermanager is None: Return rows for the given PROCESS_ID.
  • process_id is not None and filtermanager is not None: Return rows for the given PROCESS_ID whose APPLIED_FILTER matches the JSON_AGG of filtermanager's columns coming from its schema/view.
  • process_id is None and filtermanager is not None: Return rows whose APPLIED_FILTER matches the JSON_AGG of filtermanager (no PROCESS_ID constraint).

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default filtermanager Optional[object]

An object exposing col_names, schema_name, and view_name. Its columns are aggregated via JSON_AGG(col1, col2, ...) to compare against A.APPLIED_FILTER.

None process_id Optional[str]

Optional process identifier used to filter by PROCESS_ID.

None

Returns:

Type Description

tdml.DataFrame: The resulting dataframe sorted by START_DATETIME (descending).

Raises:

Type Description ValueError

If filtermanager is provided but is missing required attributes or has an empty col_names list.

RuntimeError

If the query fails.

docs.title: follow_up_report_many
docs.text:
follow_up_report_many(process_ids)

Return follow-up rows for several process_ids in a single query.

Batched counterpart to follow_up_report(process_id=…): issues one WHERE PROCESS_ID IN (…) query instead of one round-trip per process. Callers group the result by PROCESS_ID in pandas to reproduce the per-process aggregation. Returns a tdml.DataFrame sorted by START_DATETIME descending.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default process_ids

Iterable of process identifier strings. Empty/None entries are dropped; duplicates are collapsed.

required

Returns:

Type Description

tdml.DataFrame with the same columns as the follow-up table. When the

id list is empty, an empty result (WHERE 1=0) is returned so the

caller's downstream pandas logic still works.

Raises:

Type Description RuntimeError

If the query fails.

Returns an empty string when the style is unset, unknown, or 'standard'.

docs.title: _grounding_prefix
docs.text:
_grounding_prefix() -> str

Grounding directive prepended to every _llm_call system prompt.

Instructs every skill to stay grounded in the provided docs while explicitly labelling non-grounded interpretations (so the judge does not penalise them as hallucinations). Also suppresses empty boilerplate sections in DEFINITION answers.

docs.title: _llm_call
docs.text:
_llm_call(system_prompt: str, user_prompt: str, skill_name: str = 'unknown') -> str

Execute a single LLM chat completion with token tracking.

The active tdfs4ds.AGENT_ANSWER_STYLE is prepended to the system prompt so every answer (search, definition, explain, freshness, ...) inherits the same verbosity preference. The grounding directive is also prepended to instruct the model to label non-grounded interpretations explicitly.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default system_prompt str

System message content.

required user_prompt str

User message content.

required skill_name str

Name of the skill calling this (for token tracking).

'unknown'

Returns:

Type Description str

The assistant's response text.

docs.title: get_token_usage_markdown
docs.text:
get_token_usage_markdown() -> str

Get current token usage as markdown for trace display.

Shows the usage of the current turn (since the last mark_turn()), not the cumulative session totals — per-turn numbers are what the trace panel and LLM-as-judge evaluations need. The session total is appended as a single summary line.

docs.title: get_data_domain
docs.text:
get_data_domain() -> pd.DataFrame

List all DATA_DOMAINs available in the feature store.

Returns:

Type Description DataFrame

DataFrame with columns DATA_DOMAIN and FEATURE_COUNT.

docs.title: detect_data_domain_for_object
docs.text:
detect_data_domain_for_object(object_name: str, prefer_domain: Optional[str] = None) -> Optional[str]

Return the DATA_DOMAIN that contains object_name as a feature name.

Searches the full feature catalog (all data domains). Falls back to the process catalog (VIEW_NAME) if the feature is not found by name.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default object_name str

Feature or view name to locate.

required prefer_domain Optional[str]

When the object exists in multiple domains, return this one if it is among the matches. Used to prevent silent context switches on ambiguous feature names (e.g. "Claims" appearing in multiple feature stores).

None

Returns:

Type Description Optional[str]

Matching DATA_DOMAIN string, or None if not found.

Cross-store federation limit (v0.3.1.148): the catalog scanned here is the federated UNION across the primary + read-only stores, but resolution returns a bare DATA_DOMAIN with NO store tag (and _with_domain only switches tdfs4ds.DATA_DOMAIN, not the active store). With DISJOINT stores a domain uniquely identifies its store, so this is correct. If two stores share a domain name, _pick returns the first match (primary rows sort first → primary wins); making resolution store-aware is the documented follow-up.

docs.title: skill_list_data_domains
docs.text:
skill_list_data_domains(object_name: Optional[str] = None) -> dict

Answer questions about the feature store's DATA_DOMAINs.

Two modes:

  • list (object_name empty) — inventory every registered DATA_DOMAIN with its distinct feature count.
  • detail (one or more names in object_name, comma-separated) — for each named domain report its feature count and per-entity breakdown. Names are matched case-insensitively against the registered domains, with typo-tolerant fuzzy fallback (_best_name_similarity gated by tdfs4ds.FUZZY_NAME_MATCH_CUTOFF) mirroring the DEFINITION/FRESHNESS did_you_mean contract.

Returns a dict carrying its own answer (direct-answer skill — no second LLM pass), or a did_you_mean / not_found error payload.

docs.title: _build_intent_model
docs.text:
_build_intent_model(intents: list)

Build a dynamic Pydantic model for intent classification from a list of intent names.

Uses Union[Literal["A"], Literal["B"], ...] which equals Literal["A", "B", ...] per PEP 586, constructed at runtime via pydantic.create_model.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default intents list

List of uppercase intent names (e.g. ["SEARCH", "DEFINITION"]).

required

Returns:

Type Description

A Pydantic BaseModel subclass with an intent field constrained to

the provided values, plus object_name, domain, confidence,

and interpretations optional fields.

docs.title: _build_classifier_examples
docs.text:
_build_classifier_examples() -> str

Build the few-shot classifier examples string from ca-* SKILL.md files.

Examples are read from the '## Classifier Examples' section of each skill's SKILL.md file, making the SKILL.md files the single source of truth. Plugin skills loaded from TDFS4DS_SKILLS_FOLDER are included automatically.

Also injects a low-confidence counter-example to teach the LLM to assign below-0.5 confidence and populate interpretations for bare topic words.

intent_classifier(question: str, intent_model=None, intent_descriptions: dict = None, context_hint: Optional[str] = None)

Classify a user question into one of the available intents.

Parameters:

User question in natural language (French or English).

required intent_model

Pydantic model for structured output. When None, falls back to the hardwired :class:IntentResult covering all 8 intents.

None intent_descriptions dict

None context_hint Optional[str]
